(now asst. dist. comsnr.), Zanzibar, 14th Oct. 1910; ag. consular agt. at Pemba, Dec., 1911 to 13th July, 1912; ag. collr., Zanzibar, 5th Apr. to 19th July, 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 31st July to 31st Dec., 1914; and from 8th Mar., 1919; ag. port offr., 29th Aug. to 2nd Nov., 1915; and from 2nd Nov., 1918 to 4th Mar., 1919; ag. comdt. of police and ag. gov. of prisons, 1st Sept. to 6th Oct., 1915; 26th Dec., 1918 to 30th July, 1919; and from 9th Dec., 1919; offr.-in-charge, Mafia, 26th Aug., 1916 to 5th Dec., 1917.
CLARK, DONALD GEORGE, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1868; ed. Wellington Coll. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; joined tax dept., 1885; dep. comsnr. of taxes, 1913; comsnr., 1914.

CLARK, FRANCIS WILLIAM, B. 1864; M.D. (Durham), 1900; M.B., 1892; D.P.H. (Cantab.), 1891; D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), 1908; M.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1909; L., 1886; M.R.C.S. (England), 1886.—Ed. St. Paul's schl., and St. Barts. and Middlesex hosps.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; med. offr. of health and supt. fever hosp., Lowestoft, 1893; med. offr. of health and supt. of statistics, Hong Kong, 1895; dean of medical faculty and prof. of med. jurisprudence, Univ. of Hong Kong; mem. sanitary board, 1895; president, 1905; J.P., 1896; M.L.C., 1902; M.E.C., 1905; ag. P.C.M.O., 1905-6.

CLARK, GEORGE HARVEY.—B. 1873; ed. Brixton gram. schl., London, and Potsdam schl., Jamaica; elk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Mar., 1890 to July, 1891 (resigned); admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 17th Sept., 1897; clk. of cts., St. Mary, Jamaica, 2nd Nov., 1903; super. res. mag., Dec., 1903 to May, 1904; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, 1st Aug. to 29th Nov., 1904; ag. res. mag., Clarendon, Aug. to Sept., 1905; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 12th Aug., 1906; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, June, 1906; nd July, 1906 to 8th Aug., 1907; and from 12th to 16th Nov., 1907; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, in 1909; ditto, Clarendon, in 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., Kingston, in 1910; ag. res. mag., St. Mary, in 1911 and 1912; ditto, Portland, for 6 wks. in 1912; res. mag., St. Mary, 1st Feb., 1913.

CLARK, COL. W. E.—B. 1863; entd. pub. serv., Jamaica, 1879; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1890; commanded constab. depot, 1901-1908; wounded during riots at Montego Bay, St. James, 1902; ag. dep. inspr.-gen. of constab., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen., 1913 inspr.-gen. of police, and comdt. of local forces, Barbados, 1913; inspr.-gen. of police, and comdt. of local forces, B. Guiana, 1916.
CLARKE, ALBERT JAMES, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1884; supernumerary med. offr., Trinidad, 11th Aug., 1914.

CLARKE, CECIL HARRY GEORGE, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1878; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Jesus Coll., Camb.; cadet, F.M.S., 1901; cls. V., 17th Feb., 1907; cls. IV., S. Sttlnts., 1st Jan., 1912; cls. III., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. S.D.O., Prov. Wellesley, May, 1919; dist. judge, Penang, Aug., 1919.

CLARKE, SIR CHARLES MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.B. (1901); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; entd. Army, 1856; served in N. Zealand, 1861-66 (medal); Zulu war (promoted brevet-col., C.B., medal and clasp), 1879; Basutoland, 1880-81; commdt.-gen. of col. forces at Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; A.A.G., War Office, 1884; D.A.G., Ireland, 1886-88; maj.-gen. commanding 3rd infantry brig., Aldershot, 1889-92; D.A.G. at headquarters, 1892-93; com.-in-chief and lieut. gen. commanding the Forces, Madras, 1893-98; quartermaster-gen. to the Forces, 1899; gov., Malta, 1903-1907.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, KT. BACH (1894). LL.B.—B. 1861; called to the bar, Mid. Term, Easter, 1876; N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; ag. ch. just. and ch. judicial commr. for the W. Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884; confirmed, 1888; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; ch. just., 1891; ch. just., Jamaica, 1896; ret., 24th Feb., 1911.
CLARKE, F. H.—Asst. game warden, E.A.P., 1919.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FREDERICK JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1911), C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. Christ Church Mid. Schl., Barbados, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1879; M.H.A., Barbados, 1887; pres., Barbados agric. soc'y., 1905; mem. cent. poorlaw board; M.R.C., 1905; J.P.; O.C., Barbados vol. force, 1906; speaker, H. of A., Barbados, since 1898.

CLARKE, GEORGE HERBERT.—B. 1874; prin. teacher of bd. schl., Calabash Ray, Andros Is., Bahamas, Feb., 1897, to Dec., 1902; asst. res. just., Dec., 1902; ag. asst. res. just., coroner, public vaccinator for Berry Is., revising offr., regar. of births, deaths, and marriages, 1907; Out Island commr. (3rd div.), 1909; commr. (2nd div.), 1912.
CLARKE, J. C. O.—Midshipman, R.N.R., 1898; ag. sub-lieut., 1901; sub-lieut., 1903; lieut., 1905; N. Nigeria marine, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st April, 1908.

CLARKE, ROBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1870; ed. at Wesleyan High Schl., B. Honduras; entd. post office dept., B. Hond., 1893; asst. warehouse-keeper, 1895; 3rd cust. offr., 1897; 2nd ditto, 1898; 1st excise offr., 1900; 2nd cls. super int'or of cust., G. Coast, 1902; 1st cls. ditto, 1907.

CLARKE, ROBERT WILLIAM, F.I.C., diploma, food and drugs, agric. chem., biol. chem., and bacteriology.—Analyst to sanry. commrs., Gibraltar, 21st Oct., 1913 to 3rd Jan., 1921; govt. analyst, Cyprus, 4th Jan., 1921.

CLARKE, THOMAS LISLE EVELYN, M.D., C.M., McGill (1911).—B. 1885; ed. at the Lodge Schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and McGill Univ., Montreal; junr. res. surgeon, general hosp., Barbados, Aug., 1911; med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1912; ag. commr. and mag., May, 1912; mem. of quarantine bd., health offr., mem. of exec. coun., and J.P., Virgin Is., July, 1912; ag. commr., Virgin Is., 9th Apr., 1913 to 7th Jan., 1914; ag. commr., mag. and dep. judge, summary jurisdiction ct., Virgin Is., from 7th Dec., 1914 to 7th May, 1917, and from 5th July, 1917 to 31st Oct., 1918; med. offr., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1st Nov., 1918; provisional mem. fed. leg. and exec. couns., Leeward Islands, Mar., 1915; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

CLARKE, WILLIAM ESDAILE CATTLEY.—B. 1864; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen; formerly inspr. of schl. in Cape Colony; sec. to educn. dept., Transvaal, 1906; inspr. of secondary schl., Transvaal, 1911.

CLAUSSON, GERARD LESLIE MAKINS, O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919.—B. 1891; scholar of Eton, 1903, and C.C.C. Oxford, 1910; Boden Sanskrit Scholar, 1911; 1st cls. Class. Hon. Mods., 1912; Hall-Houghton Syriac prizeman, 1913; 2nd cls. Greats, 1914; B.A., 1919; James Mew Arabic Scholar, 1920; apptd. after compet. exam. 2nd cls. clk., B. of Inland Rev., Sept., 1914, but released for mil. service; 2nd lieut., 7th Som. L.I., 21st Aug., 1914; transf'd. to gen. list, 19th April, 1915; served in War Office, Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; capt. Gen. Staff, 26th Dec., 1915, to 12th Apr., 1916, and 23rd Apr., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; mentioned in despatches Mesopotamia, 1917, Egypt, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, with Palms, 1917; 2nd cls. clk. in C.O., 1st Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

CLAXTON, THOMAS FOLKES, F.R.A.S.—B. 1874; joined mag. and met. dept. R. Obser., Greenwich, Feb., 1890; apptd. asst. dir. Royal Alfred Obser., Mauritius, Jan., 1896; dir., Dec., 1896; sec. mem. soc., Maur., mem. and hon. sec. of bd. of directors of institute and museum, and mem. of comte. of primary educn.; dir., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, June, 1912; J.P., April, 1913; asst. cable censor, 1914 to 1917 in addn.; dep. cable censor, 1917-18; ag. cable censor, 1918-19; received thanks of col. govt. for services rendered as censor.

CLAYTON, GEORGE EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, 1910-15; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 13th Dec., 1915; served in France, 55th (West Lancs.) divn., 1917-18; Mily. Cross, Apr., 1918; served in Germany, 1919; demob., 1st Nov., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Aug., 1920; attached to secretariat, Singapore, 1920; trans. to Penang, Jan., 1921; dep. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, 29th May, 1921.

CLAYTON, REGINALD JOHN BYARD.—B. 1875; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1898; attached to land off., Batang Padang, July, 1899; in charge Kuala Kangsar treasy., Dec., 1899; passed final law exam., Feb., 1900; ag. regiar., Taiping, Apr., 1900; ag. mag., Larut, May, 1900; ag. collr., land rev., Matang, Aug., 1900; in charge of Matang dist., Sept., 1900; passed final language exam., Nov., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Matang, Jan., 1901; ditto, Batang Padang, Feb., 1901; 1st cls. mag., Perak, Dec., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Feb., 1904; ditto, Kinta, June, 1904; and dep. regar. of titles, Perak South, till June, 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 14th Nov., 1906, to 6th May, 1909; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 11th May, 1909; offr. of cls. IV., 25th Dec., 1910; dist. offr., Pekan, 12th Oct., 1913; offr. of cls. III., 19th Nov., 1913; dist. offr., Kuantan, 4th May, 1915; dist. offr., Krian, 25th May, 1916; on special duty, 26th Apr., 1918; resumed duty as dist. offr., Krian, 16th July, 1918; seconded for special duty under the food contrl. at Singapore, 3rd Jan., 1919; dir. of food production, S. Sttlmts., May, 1919; ag. mag., Seremban, Mar., 1920; ag. collr. of land r.v., Seremban, May, 1920; dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1920.

CLAYTON, THOMAS WATTS.—B. 1877; ed. Cheltenham and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.)—B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; passed cadet, Apr., 1903; offr., cls. VI., Jan., 1905; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1906; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1912; on mily. serv., Aug., 1915 to Apr., 1919; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1918; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

CLEALL, ERNEST JOHN.—B. 1899; ent. C.O., Oct., 1914; apvtd. after compct. exam., asst. clk., 25th Apr., 1917; assgd. to C.O., 15th Apr., 1919; on mil. ser. from 8th Oct., 1917 to 11th Dec., 1918, employed in the C.O. as ordl. clk. to staff offr., W.A.F.F., 8th Oct., 1917 to 10th Oct., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; prmt., undck order-in-council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., 24th Feb., 1920.
CLEARE, W. T.—B. 1867; Resident just. and collr. of cuat., Bimini, Bahamas, Nov., 1899; ditto, Long Island, Mar., 1901; ditto, Green Turtle Cay., Abaco, 1906; Out Island commr. (2nd div.), 1909.
CLEAVER, WILLIAM FIDDLER, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1855; supernumerary surg., Trinidad, 19th Mar., 1897; dist. med. offr., 1897.
CLELAND, JOHN STOCKWIN.—Draughtsman, P.W.D., Transvaal, Mar., 1909; asst. archt., P.W.D., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; archt., Sept., 1920.

CLEMENT, HON. WM. HENRY POPE, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1858; ed. pub. schls., Ontario and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1880; legal adviser to, and mem. of Yukon council, 1898; county court judge, Yale dist., B. Columbia, 1905; judge, sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1906.

CLEMENTI, CECIL, C.M.G. (1916).—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy., 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; 1st cl. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cl. lit. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., and ag. clk. of councils, Dec., 1900, to Oct., 1901; asst. regar.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of exams. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; seconded for spec. serv. under govt. of India, May, 1902; J.P., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of councils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land ct., New Territories, Dec., 1903; ag. asst. land offr. and pol. mag., New Territories, May, 1906, to Sept., 1906; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Oct., 1907; attended Internat. Opium Conf. at Shanghai, Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the Admstr., 30th Apr., 1910; ag. col. sec., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 22nd Feb. to 6th June, 1911, 29th Nov., 1911, to 1st Feb., 1912, and from 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912; sec. to govt., B. Guiana, 1913; admstd. govt., 8th June, 1916, to 14th Apr., 1917.
CLEMENTI, LOCKHART.—B. 1881; served in Cape Mounted Rifles; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; served in European War.

CLENDENNING, J., B.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; 2nd lieut., 9th S. Staffs. Regt. (Pioneers), 1915; wounded, 31st July, 1916; lieut., Royal Engineers, 17th Nov., 1917; asst. dir., survey, Gold Coast, 1919; dep. dir., cadastral branch, 1920.

CLEVERTON, T. C. A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1880; med. offr., quarantine, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1908; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr., Labuan, Mar., 1908; med. offr., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1915; med. offr., Labuan, June, 1915.

CLIFFORD, SIR HUGH CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1940).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887 (recd. thanks of S. of S.), ag. govt. agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. res. Pahang, Sept., 1890 to Mar., 1891, Apr. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; visited Coqos-Keeling Is. as special commr., June, 1894; led armed exped. into native states of Trénggant and Kelantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, May-June, 1895 (recd. thanks S. of S.); res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, 14th Sept., 1904; sent on deputation to B. Guiana in Nov., 1905, to enquire into municipal institutions; admstd. govt., 26th Mar. to 30th Aug., 1904, and from 1st Apr. to 23rd Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, 3rd May, 1907; admstd. govt., 11th July to 3rd Aug., 1907, 3rd June to 11th Oct., 1909, 30th June to 17th Nov., 1911, and from 23rd Nov., 1911, to 3rd Jan., 1912; gov., G. Coast, 11th Dec., 1912; gov., Nigeria, 23rd July, 1919; concluded with M. Noufflard, the lieut.-gov. of Dahomey, in Aug., 1914, an agreement for the temporary partition between the British and French Govts., and for the provisional administration of Togoland; admstd. Br. sphere of occupation, Togoland, concurrently with the G. Coast, from Aug., 1914, to Apr., 1919; is part author, with Sir Frank Swettenham, of a Malayan dictionary, and author of more than a dozen books, mostly on Malay subjects, and of a translation of the penal code of into Malay; also of a history of the G. Coast regt. in the E. Africa; can.

CLIFT, HON. J. AUGUSTUS, C.R.E. (1818), K.C.—Called to the bar, 1884; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1889; speaker of assembly, 1891; clk. of assembly, 1893-97; elected to assembly, representing Twillingate dist., 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1904; re-elected Twillingate dist., 1908 and 1913; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1913.

CLINCKETT, ROBERT JAMES, M.B.E. (1918).—Entered provost marshal's office, Barbados, as jun. clk in 1869; accntant, in 1870; acted as provost marshal from Feb. to Sept., 1873; again in 1880; reag. 1890; apptd. official assignee, 1st Oct., 1882; served as mem. of health and quarantine bd., from 10th Oct., 1899 to 6th May, 1901; acted as mast. in-chancery, from Nov., 1901 to Apr., 1902; mem. of educn. bd.; gov. of "Harrison" and "Queen" Colls. and Combermere schl.; M.L.C., from 10th Oct., 1899 to 24th Mar., 1903.
CLIPSHAM, PHILIP RICHARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 31st Sept., 1920.
CLOETE, S. W.—Tempy. ast dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1919.

CLOUGH, ERNEST MARSHALL OWEN, C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1873; ed. at the Mercers' Schl. and in Germany; served in S. African war, H.A.C. field battery (Queen's medal with three clasps), 1900-01; sec. to Pretoria asylum under mil. admin., 1901; priv. sec. to Sir R. Solomon, K.C.M.G., 1901-1903; accompanied Sir R. Solomon when representing S. Africa at Delhi Durbar, 1903 (medal); clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Transvaal, 1903-1907; clk. of legis. couns., Transvaal, 1907-1910; clk. of senate, Union of S. Africa, 1910; rec. Union commemoration medal; admitted sworn translator (English-Dutch of sup.), 1908; on mil. serv., 1914-15 and 1917-19; lieut.-Cape Garrison Arty., 1914; O.C. armoured train, S. African rebellion and O.C. batty., Heavy Arty. Brig., German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; served in France and Italy as capt., R.F.A., 1913-19; demob., 15th Jan., 1919; sec. speaker's confere on the future constitution of the senate, 1920; hon. sec., S. African branch, Empire Parly. Asm.; author of the "South African Parliamentary Manual" and various papers on constitutional law.

CLUTTERBUCK, P.A., M.C.—B. 1897; schol. of Malvern Coll. and of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1915; coman., sp. res. of offrs., Coldstream Guards, Jan., 1916; lieut., Sept., 1916; capt. and adjt., 3rd batt., Oct., 1917; M.C. and ment. in despr., 1918; demob., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., G.P.O., 1st Sept., 1919; seconded to C.O., 13th Feb., 1922.

COAKER, HON. WM. F.—President, fisherman's protective union, Newfoundland; M.H.A. for Bonavista, 1913; M.H.A., Twillingate, 1914; mem. of tonnage comte., 1917; M.E.C., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

COATES, J.—Ast. govt. printer, Uganda Prot., 27th May, 1910; ag. ast. govt. press, E. Africa Prot., June, 1911 to Feb., 1912; govt. printer and controller of stationery, Uganda Prot., 17th June, 1914.

COATES, HON. JOSEPH GORDON, M.C.—B. 1878; ed. privately; elec. mem. of New Zealand parl., 1911; re-elec., 1914 and 1919; min. of justice, 1919-20; P.M.G. since 1919; min. of pub. wks. since 1920 and native min., 1921; served with N.Z. Exped. Force as capt. and major, 1916-1919; awarded M.C. with bar.

COATS, ROBERT HAMILTON, B.A.—B. 1874; U. Toronto Univ.; bankers' scholarship in polit. economy, and Wyld prize. English; on staff of "Toronto Globe," 1897-1900; editor, labour gaz., and ch. statistician, dept. of labour, 1901-12; mem. coman. on the cost of living, 1913-14; dom. statistician and contrl. of census, 1914; author of "The Labour Movement in Canada" (Canada and its provinces); joint author of life of James Douglas makers of Canada series); mem. of coman. on statistics under the League of Nations, 1920.
COBB, R. S.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.
COBB, W. GRAHAM, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Cert. of London S.T.M.; medical officer, N. Nigeria (W.A.M.S.), 24th April, 1912.

COCHRAN, PETER CAMPBELL, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1870; ed. Loretto schl., Scotland, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; Scotch law agt., Boer war, 1900-02; ast. mag., Lydenburg, 1902; Vereeniging, 1904; ast. sec., law dept., 1906; mag., Heidelberg, 1906; Barberton, 1911; Ermelo, 1914; Germiston, 1915; Bloemfontein, 1920.

COCHRANE, C. S., M.I.C.E.—Grad. at Trin. Coll., Dub., 1868; was employed on rly. wks. in S. America, 1871-74; ast. engr. on the Cape Govt. rlys., 1874; ag. dist. engr., 1877; engr. in the pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1878; engr. in charge of surveys, 1879.

COCHRANE, CHARLES WALTER HAMILTON.—B. 1876; ed. Repton and Merton Coll., Oxford (exhr.); 1st cls. class. mods., 1897; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; ag. ast. sec. to res. gen., 1901; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 1904; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak, 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1908; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Krian, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Coast, Negri Sembilan, 1911; ag. collector land rev., Seremban, 1912; ag. temporarily as British res., Negri Sembilan, 15th to 24th May, 1914; offr. of cls. III., 1st July, 1915; attached to C.O., Sept., 1915 to June, 1916; dep. pub. pros., Perak, 6th Oct., 1916; chief ast. dist. offr., Kinta, 21st June, 1917; sec. to res., Perak, 18th May, 1919.

COCKBURN, THE HON. SIR JOHN ALEX., K.C.M.G. (1900); M.D. (Lond.).—B. 1860; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1884-1898; min. of educn., 1885, to June, 1887; premier and ch. sec., 1889-90; again min. of educn. and agricul., 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1898-1901.

COCKER, THOMAS BERNARD.—B. 1898; ed. Oldham Gram. Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; R.N. acnt. branch, 1918-19; B.A., 1920 (pt. I., class. tripods); LL.B., 1921 (pt. II., law tripods), Gray's Inn; cadet, S. Stirling, 1921.

COCKEY, C. E. E.—Ed. St. Edwards Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; M.A.; served in France and Salonica; 3rd Devons, atchd. 2nd Gouc. Regt., May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; ag. capt.; mentd. in despr.; ast. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, 13th June, 1919.

COCKSHUTT, HENRY.—B. 1868; ed. pub. and high schls., Brantford; pres., Cockshutt Plough Co., since 1911; pres. of several other large manufacturing concerns; mayor of Brantford, Ont., 1899-1900; pres., Brantford bd. of trade, 1898; pres., Canadian manufacturers' assn., 1906-7; organised and financed the 215th batt., Brantford, of which he was lieut.-col., and now hon. col.; unsuccessful candidate for the H. of C., 1917; lieut.-gov., Ont., 10th Sept., 1921.

CODE, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1864; ent. Canadian civ. ser. in cust. dept., 1st Apr., 1882; 1st cls. clk. and ast. acct., 1st July, 1897; transf. to trade and commerce dept. as acct., 1st July, 1904; ch. clk., 1st July, 1905; ast. and chief ast. to dep. min., 1st July, 1908; ast. to dep. min. and ch. acct., 1st Apr., 1919.

CODERRE, HON. LOUIS, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1865; ed. primary schl., St. Ours, Hyacinthe and Montreal Colleges and Laval Univ.; called to the bar, 1892; head of the firm Coderre and Coderre, advocates, Montreal; city attorney, St. Henri, 1896-1905; syndic of the Montreal bar, 1904-1909; unsuccessful cand. for Quebec legislature, 1908, and for H. of C. in same year; elec. to H. of C. for Hochelaga, Sept., 1911; sworn of the P.C. for Canada and sec. of state, Oct., 1912; also min. of mines, 1913; judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1915.

CODRINGTON, Humphrey William.—B. 1879; ed. Winchester and New Coll. Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. asst., Galle Kachcheri, July, 1904; Batticaloa Kachcheri, Jan., 1905; Kurunegala Kachcheri, May, 1906; Kandy Kachcheri, Nov., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1907; off. asst. to govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1908; ditto, Kegalla, Aug., 1911; addtl. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Aug., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, July, 1915; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1915; ditto, Kandy, Oct., 1915; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Apl., 1916; on military duty, 1918; ag. govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1919; comsnr. under the Buddhist Temporalities Ord., Mar., 1920; govt. agt., prov. of Sabaragamuwa, in addn., Sept., 1921.

CODRINGTON, Stewart.—B. 1874; cadet, S. Stthnts., Nov., 1898; ag. sub-inspr. of schls., Malacca, May, 1899; ag. supt. Malay coll., Mar., 1900; ag. distr. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1900; distr. offr., Penang, Aug., 1902; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, June, 1907; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, Jan., 1908; distr. offr., Xmaas, Feb., 1910, but cont. to act in Butterworth; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1910; promoted to Cls. III., 1st Jan., 1912, and continued to act as 2nd asst. col. sec.; ag. offr. assignee, Singapore, 7th May, 1913; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Stthnts., 10th Oct., 1913; ag. collr. land rev. and regis. of deeds, Singapore, 12th Oct. to 19th Nov., 1916, in addition; asst. sec. to high comsnr. for Malay States, 19th Jan. to 3rd Mar., 1917, in addition; officiating (temp.) as 2nd asst. col. sec., S. Stthnts., 14th Jan., 1918; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Stthnts., 23rd Feb., 1918; asst. sec. to high comsnr. for Malay States, 11th May to 1st July, 1918, in addition; sec. to mil. ser. tribunl., 1st Aug., 1918, in addition; supprmry. offr. 2nd cls., Jan., 1919; mag., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1920; ag. collr. of land rev., Penang, Sept., 1920; ag. pres. munici. comsnr., and pres., rent assessment bd., in addition, Aug., 1920.

COE, Thomas Perowne, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. Norwich Gram. Schol. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. class. mods., 1908, 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Knaia Kangsar, July, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. sec. to ch. sec., Feb., 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1912; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1913; temp. 2nd-lieut., 7th (service) battn. Norfolk Regt., 11th Mar., 1915; temp. lieut., 7th (service) battn., Norfolk Regt., 27th Oct., 1915; G.S.O., 3rd grade, with rank of temp. capt., trans. to Gen. List, 25th July, 1917; temp. capt., Gen. List (Gen. Staff), 1st May, 1918; brig. maj., 9th May, 1918; offr., cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addition, Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; ag. 2nd asst. sec. to govt., 1st July, 1920.
COGHILLAN, Frederick Albert.—B. 1859; Under sec., chief secretary's dept., New South Wales, Aug., 1910-1914; auditor gen., N.S.W., May, 1914.

COGHLAN, Sir Timothy Augustine, K.C.M.G. (1918), Kt. Bach. (1914), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1856; ed. Sydney gram. schol.; apptd. to pub. wks. dept., 1873; assoc. mem. Inst. of Civil Engrs., 1882; asst. engr. harbours and rivers dept., 1884; govt. statistician, 1886; registr. of friendly societies, 1892; honry. fellow roy. stats. soc., 1893; mem. of pub. ser. bd., 1896; justice of the peace, 1896; roy. comsnr. to inquire into the working of the marine bd., 1896; roy. comsnr. to inquire into working of Sydney water supply and sewerage bd., 1897; supervised the N. S. Wales census of 1891; pres. of the confce. of Australian statisticians to determine the population of the colonies, and to arrange for a uniform census of Australasia, 1900; agent-gen. for N.S. Wales in London, Feb., 1903; ret., 1915; mem. of comte. for stintl. of ex-service men within the empire, 1917; ag. agent-gen. for N.S. Wales, 1920; author of "Wealth and Progress of N. S. Wales," "The Seven Colonies of Australasia," "Childbirth, a Study in Statistics," "Notes on the Financial Aspect of Australian Federation," "Report on the Eleventh Census of N. S. Wales," "Treatise on the Deaths of Women in Child-birth," "Picturesque N. S. Wales," "Child Measurement," "Discharge of Streams in Relation to Rainfall," "Australia and New Zealand" (with Hon. T.T. Ewing), "Progress of Australia in the 19th Century," &c.

COHEN, E.—B. 1882; clk., Cape govt. rlys., May, 1900; transf'd to Imperial Mil. Rlys., Nov., 1900; transf'd to Bech. Prot. service and attached to office of high comsnr. for S. Africa, Aug., 1901; asst. Imp. acctnt., Apr., 1918.
COHEN, J. J.—Speaker, legis. assem., New South Wales, 1917; now judge of dist. court.
COHEN, W. S.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 2nd class tripods, 1893; M.A.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; sec., imigr. (tempore), O.R.C., 1902.
COLE, A. H.—B. 1874; elk., educn. offr., Bahamas, 1889; sec. to bd. of educn., 1901; keeper of prison, 1907, supt. of prisons, S. Nigeria, 1913.

COLE, Arnold Kirtland.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. res. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. supt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd elk., treasy. dept., 1913; 1st elk., P.O., 1914; ch. elk. and asst. examr. offr., cust. dept., 1st Aug., 1921.
COLE, Edward Hamilton.—B. 1878; accnt. elk., Basutoland, Feb., 1902; sub-inspr., pol., June, 1902; inspr., 1913; asst. comsnr., 1914; served in European War.
COLE, Henry William.—B. 1873; ed. Bedford Modern schl.; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 6th May, 1914.

COLE BOWEN, Mervyn William.—B. 1875; B.A. (Dublin); B.A.I., M.I.C.E.; asst. engrn., rly. extns., Ceylon, July, 1900; engrn., stats. extns., Aug., 1905; chief construction engrn., rly. extns., Mar., 1907.

COLEMAN, Percy.—B. 1872; M.A., Oxford; ed. Kingswood schl.; scholar, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1890; 1st cls. math. mods.; 1st cls. final maths., 1894; asst. master, Owen's Coll., Mill Hill Schls.; head of maths. dept., Northern Polytechnic Inst., London, 1903; teacher and examiner, Univ. of London; adviser on technical educn., Union of South Africa, 1914; author of "Co-ordinate Geometry" (Oxford Press).

COLL, Sir Anthony Michael, Kt. Bach (1912).—Ed. Haileybury, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; M.A. and B.C.L., 1888; called to the bar, Inn Tem., Jan., 1887; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1892; atty.-gen., 1901; K.C., 1902; ag. ch. just. and ag. col. sec. on various occasions; chief just., Jamaica, 1911.

COLLENS, ARCHIBALD EDGAR, F.I.C., F.C.S.—Lab. astt., and chem. demstr. to the prof. of chem., Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1900-08; lect., agrl. chem., govt. training schls., 1902-03; astt. analyst, govt. lab., Trinidad, 1903-16; offr. in charge, rubber tapping experiments, dept. of agr., 1911-17; scientific astt., bd. of agric., 1908-10; science master, Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1906-08, 1910, and 1914; ag. prin. astt. anal., govt. lab., 1914-15; sup't., field experiments, 1915-16; examr. in pharm. chem., to med. coun., Trinidad, 1908-17; examr. in agr., bd. of educ., 1908-17; govt. chem. and sup't. of agr., Leeward Is.; chmn., granary bd., Antigua; mem., food control prices bd., Antigua, 1917.
COLLENS, HENRY KIRKHAM.—B. 1870; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st Dec., 1888; astt. sup't. of prisons, and chief clk., royal gaol, Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1907.

COLLER, FRANK HERBERT.—B. 1866; ed. Westminster schl. and Christ Church, Oxford; 1st cls. class. mods.; 1st cls. lit. hum.; M.A.; pres. of Oxford Union Society, 1890; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1893; ch. just., St. Lucia, 912; seconded for special war duty in the U.K., st Jan., 1917.

COLLET, Sir WILFRED, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1856; ed. at Univ. Coll., Lond.; LL.B., Lond., 1889; 1st cls. intermed., 1889, and final, 1903; provisional nd clk., native dept., Fiji, Jan., 1881; inspr. of immigr. and astt. native commrs., Jan., 1883; sec. W. Pacific high comm., Oct., 1894; dist. commr., Cyprus, 1897; also British deleg. of sqad., 1901; ag. rec-gen., 1900-01, and in 1902 and 1904; cd. sec., Br. Hond., 18th Oct., 1905; dist. gov't., 18th Oct., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906, 5th May, 1908, to 9th Jan., 1909, and from 5th Aug., 1911, to 10th Apr., 1912; gov., B. Hond., 6th Apr., 1913; gov., B. Guiana, 22nd Mar., 917; assumed govt., 15th Apr., 1917.
COLLETT, J.W., M.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.—Med. fr., S. Nigeria, 1901; author of "Text Book of physiology and Hygiene."

COLLINS, ARTHUR ERNEST, C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1871; ed. at City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; scholar of Trin. Coll., 1890; 1st class classic, pos., 1893; B.A., 1893; M.A., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in C.O., June, 1894; iv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Mar., 1897; astt. iv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Feb., 1898; 1st ass'g. clk., Nov., 1898; sec. of S.S. currency mtece., 1902-3; prin. clk., May, 1907; mem. of W. African currency comte., 1911-12; astt. sec., 1st iv., 1920; joint editor of C.O. List since 1893.

COLLINS, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Lond., 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 12th Nov., 0; attached to the Karunegala Kachcheri, 1st Dec., 1910; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Apr., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Putalam, 1912; office astt., Kandy Kachcheri, July, 3; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, June, 1914; astt. d settm't. offr., May, 1915; ag. astt. govt., Putalam and Chilaw, July, 1915; astt. d settm't. offr., Sept., 1915; ag. astt. govt., Putalam and Chilaw, Oct., 1915; addtl. t. govt. agt., ditto, Sept., 1916; office astt. to t. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1916; 2nd astt. col., Oct., 1917; 3rd astt. col. sec., Sept., 1920.
OLLINS, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS.—B. 1863; i. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st Oct., 1884; sup't. clk. and dep. regis., sup. ot., 17th Feb., 1914.
OLLINS, JAMES RICHARD, C.M.G. (1920).—1869; treas'y. clk., Victoria, 1883; acctnt. to Commonwealth of Austr. treas'y., 1901; sec. to treas'y., 1916; mem. of C. of A. coun. of finance.

COLLINS, JOSEPH THOMAS, M.A., LL.M.—Scholar and medallist of Trin. Coll., Melbourne; exhibitioner, scholar and honourman in arts and laws, Melbourne Univ.; barrister-at-law, Victoria, 1894; legal adviser to Roy. Comn. on Local Gov't. Law, 1902; mem. and subsequently chmn. of Roy. Comn. on Unification or Federation of Municipalities in Victoria, 1903-5; partly, draughtsman, Victoria, since July, 1910; author, in conjunction with Mr. C. H. Meaden, of "Local Government Law and Practice."

COLLINS, COL. ROBERT JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1911); I.S.O. (1909); V.D. (1902).—Cik., militia office, 1865-77, including sub-storekeeper, P.W.D., New Zealand, 1871 to 1877; store audit, 1877-8; clk. in treas'y., 1878-9; 1st clk., treas'y., 1879-85; clk-in-charge, 1885-90; acctnt. to treas'y., 1890-1906; astt. sec., 1903-6; sec. to treas'y., paymaster-gen. and recvr.-gen., 1906-1910; also finanoe mem., N.Z. coun. of defence, 1906-1910; contr. and audr.-gen. for N.Z. since 1910; J.P., 1904; has served in N.Z. volunteers since 1865; colonel, 1907; lieut.-col., N.Z. militia, for services rendered in connection with enrolment of S. Africa contingent, 1902; exec. offr., N.Z. rifle asso., since 1879; stat. offr., ditto, 1886-1903; ch. exec. offr., 1904; commdt., N.Z. Bizley rifle team (winners of Kolapore cup), 1904; hon. A.D.C. to gov., 1911; chmn., pub. debts sinking funds commrs.

COLLINS, ST ROBT. HENRY MUIRHEAD, KT. BACH. (1919), C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1852; retired lieut. R.N.; entered R.N., 1866; served in flying squadrons in 1868 and 1872; sub-lieut., June, 1872; served on Channel and Australian stations to 1876; lieut., June, 1876; apptd. to Victorian naval forces (Australia), Dec., 1878; comdr. Victorian naval forces, 1884; sec. for defence for Victoria, 1888; sec. to dept. of defence, Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901; late offl. sec. in Great Britain for the C. of A.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.O.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Heidelberg Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Univ. of Wisconsin, 1911; astt. geologist, geol. surv. of Can., 1905; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; fellow, Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir., geol. surv. of Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. Min. Met.; mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; has pub. numerous papers and memoirs on pre-cambrian geol. and petrology.

COLLISSON, PERCIVAL LORIMER, O.B.E. (Mil.), 1918.—B. 1883; ed. at Warminster and King's Coll., London; apptd. examr., E. and A. dept., 1901; col. aud. branch, Sept., 1905; examr. of accts., O.R.C., Jan., 1906; rejoined col. aud. branch, Jan., 1908; astt. auditor, N. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; ag. local auditor, 1909-10; 2nd cls. clk., col. audita., 1914; mil. audit., E. Africa Exped. Force, 1916 (membr. in depts.); sec. astt. auditor, E.A.F., Apl., 1919; ag. auditor, E.A.F., 1920.

COLLYMORE, H. W. I.S.O. (1918).—Super-num. G.P.O., Barbados, Oct., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., 1878; 3rd clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1879; 3rd clk., correspndnce branch, Jan., 1881; ag. 2nd clk., 1883; ag. pub. librn., 1884; 2nd clk. correspndnce branch, col. sec.'s office, July, 1884; ag. ch. clk., June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; sec. highways coman., 1893-6; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1909; col. postmaster, Aug., 1918.

COLLYNS, JOHN MOORE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., 1900; M.B., Lond., 1904; D.P.H., Lond., 1904.—Ed. at Blundell's Schol., Tiverton, Devon, and St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; certif. of Schol. of Trop. Med., Lond., 1905; civil surg., S. African campaign; Queen's Medal with five clasps; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 6th July, 1906.

COLMAN, EUGENE ERNEST.—B.A. Cantab.; cadet, S. Stlmts., Oct., 1902; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Oct., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Mar., 1906; asst. to res., Malacca, July, 1910; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, and asst. to res., Malacca, 8th July, 1911; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, 1st Jan., 1912; ag. dep. regir., Penang, 23rd Sept., 1912; offr. of cls. IV., 21st Dec., 1914, but continued to act as dep. regir., ag. 1st mag., Penang, 20th Apr., 1915; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, 26th Apr., 1915; on spec. duty in Singapore, 28th Dec., 1915; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct. and asst. assignee, Penang, 27th July, 1916; supr. offr. of cls. 3, 1st Jan., 1919, continuing as ag. dep. regir., sup. ct. and asst. assignee, Penang; regir., dist. judge and mag., Malacca, June, 1920.

COLVILLE, ERNEST FREDERICK.—Ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 1901; asst. res., Nyasaland Prot., Mar., 1905; ag. asst. chief sec., Mar., 1914; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Sept., 1914; res., 2nd grade, Apr., 1917; prov. comsrr., 1st June, 1921.
COLVILLE, J. W.—Ck., lands dept., Victoria, apptd. 1863; head of registry of that dept., Jan., 1878; sec. cent. bd. of health, May, 1885.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLYNEUX, KT. BACH. (1920).—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; atty-gen., E.A.P., 1912; atty-gen., Nigeria, 1913.

COMMISSIONG, TERENCE BERTRAND.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., col. s.c.'s office, Grenada, 1st Apr., 1909; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, 1st June, 1915; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1st Dec., 1919; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., med. regir., and man., govt. printing sub-dept., Feb.-Sept., 1920; seconded for active serv., 19th Sept., 1915 to 25th Oct., 1919; awarded meritorious serv. med. in German E. African campaign, 3rd June, 1916; apptd. J.P., Grenada, in recog. of war serv., 19th July, 1919; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. to exec. couns., med. regir., and man., govt. printing dept., 1st May, 1921.

COMMISSIONG, TYRRELL MILLIMAY.—Ed. at S.E. College, Ramsgate; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1895; joined Grenada bar, Oct., 1899; ag. pol. mag. on several occasions, 1898 to 1903; pol. mag., W. Dist., 1st Aug., 1903.

COMMISSIONG, WILLIAM SATTER, C.M.G. (1911), K.C.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1867; escheator-gen., July, 1872; has acted on numerous occasions as atty.-gen. and as ch. just.; is an unofficial member of legis. coun.

CONDELL, CLAUDE FORLONG.—B. 1865; ed. Edinburgh Acad., Stonyhurst Coll. and Paris Univ.; lecturer on mod. languages and English literature, Paris, 1895; educn. dept., Malta, 1900; examr. in French and German, Malta R.A. exam., 1901; mem. of bd. of examrs. for Malta civ. ser., 1902; inspr. of schls., St. Lucia, 1904; examr. of secondary schls., Grenada, 1905; ag. M.L.C., St. Lucia, 1906; govt. deleg. to agric. conf., Jamaica, 1907 (earthquake), and Barbados, 1908; ag. M.E.C. and L.C., St. Lucia, 1912; col. sec., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed office, 21st Mar., 1915; admtd. govt., Apr.-May, 1915; postal censor during war; comsrr., Montserrat, Oct., 1918.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL, KT. BACH. (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick Pub. Schol. and Catholic Schol.; M.L.C., Western Australia, 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and lab., 1906; col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leader of leg. coun. from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. for Perth, 1914; agt. genl. in London for Western Australia, 20th Nov., 1917.
CONRAN, JOSEPH GEORGE.—Emigration agent at Madras, for Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1899.

CONSTANTINE, BAXANDALL, B. 1868; ed. Bradford Grammar School and St. John's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1891; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., July, 1892; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1896; Jaffna, Jan., 1898; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Sept., 1898; Puttalam, Feb., 1902; pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya-Hatton, Sept., 1902; Galle, Apr., 1904; Colombo, Sept., 1905, and Feb., 1908; deputy collr. of cust., Colombo, June, 1907; dist. judge, Negombo, July, 1909; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1911; govt. agt., N. C. Prov., Dec., 1912; offr. of cls. I, grade 2, Jan., 1913; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1915; ditto, N. Prov., Apr., 1919.

CONWAY, WILLIAM EWAN.—B. 1893; ed. High Schol., Dundee; govt. travelling schol. master, 10th Dec., 1913; mily. duties, Aug., 1914 to June, 1915; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1916; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., June, 1916 to Apr., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., 1920; collr. of cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1920.

COOK, FREDERICK.—B. 1858; ed. Leeds Gram. Schol.; in journalism in Canada since 1882; Canadian correspondent of "The Times," London, for 20 years; sch. ol trustee and alderman, Ottawa; mayor of Ottawa, 1902-3; sec. British Columbia Better Terms Comsrs., 1914-15; dep. King's printer of Canada, 1st July, 1915 to 1st May, 1920; now chmn. of editorial comtece. of Dom. govt.; author of "Commercial Canada," "The Relations of Ottawa to the Federal authority"; edited "Canadian Who's Who," (pub. by "The Times"), 1909.

COOK, Rt. Hon. Sir Joseph, P.C. (1914); G.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1860; mem. of H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia, since 1901; postmnr., N.S. Wales, 1894-1898; min. for mines and agric., 1898-9; min. of defenc., C. of A., June, 1909; prim. minister, C. of A., June, 1913-Sept., 1914; min. for the Navy, 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; high comsrr. in London for C. of A., Nov., 1921.

COOK, WALLACE HAYNES.—Ck. pol. settlement, Massaruni, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1881; steward, July, 1883; ag. sec. to poor law comsrrs. and supt. of girls' reformatory, Apr., 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1895, to Feb., 1896; ck. of supplies prison dept. and sec. to bd. of prisons, Nov., 1890; supt. almshouse, Georgetown, June, 1892; amalgamated offices of sup. almshouse, and sec. to poor law comsrrs., May, 1899.

COOKE, A.—B. 1890; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (Charles Oldham classical scholar); 2nd cls. class. Hons. Mods., 1910; Goldsmith's exhib., in Classics and Divinity, 1910; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., 1912; B.A. 1912; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. cll. C.O., 13th Oct., 1913; on military service from 13th Apl., 1917 to 16th March, 1919; asst. priv.

COOKE, HERBERT SUTTON, O.B.E. (1919).—C.M.R., 1893-99; Basutoland service, 1899-1901; inspr., native affrs., dept., Transvaal, Dec., 1901; sen. inspr., Mar., 1902; dist. contr., Johannesburg, July, 1903; chief pass offr., July, 1908; asst. dir. of native labour (Union), Apr., 1912; maj., Union Defence Force—political services in East Africa, 1916-17.

COOKE, JOHN HAMILTON, B.A., M.B., B.Ch.—Ed. at Portora Royal Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; senior exhibitioner and royal scholar; honours in classics, 1883; in anatomy, 1885; sen. house surg. Western gen. dispensary, Marylebone, Lon., 1890; med. offr., dist. C., Dominica, 1891; dist. 4, St. Kitts, dist. 3, Nevis, 1892; Yaws hosp., 1892-7; mem. bd. of health, 1893; surg.-cap., local forces, 1898; med. offr., dist. 3 Antigua, 1905; to England in medical charge of 1st Leeward Is. and St. Lucia contingents of British West India Regt., and volunteered for service under War Office, 1915; Fellow of Royal Col. Inst.
COOKE, S. V.—Asst. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917.

COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; asst. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W.A., 1896; govt. astronomer, New South Wales, 1912; prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ.

COOKSON, CLAUDE EDWARD.—Capt., reserve of offs.; barrister-at-law; educ. at Eton (King's scholar, 1891-97); served in regular army, 1899-1910; capt., 1908; with Bedfordshire regt. in India, Gibraltar and Bermuda; station staff offr., Aden, 1907-8; ag. brigade major, 1908; asst. comntr. of police, Gold Coast, Sept., 1911; extra A.D.C. to Mr. J. J. Thorburn, C.M.G., gov. of G. Coast, Feb. to May, 1912; comntr. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1915; asst. col. sec., July, 1915; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1919; inspr.-gen. of prisons, 28th Feb., 1920.

COOMARASWAMY, CHINNAPPAN.—B. 1887; ed. Hindu Coll., Jaffra, and Royal Coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., April, 1910; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Apr., 1910; ag. office asst. to ditto, May, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1913; pol. mag. and office asst. to govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1913; pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Point Pedro, June, 1915; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 918; ditto, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1920; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, in idln., Aug., 1920.

COOMARASWAMY, VALUPILLAI.—B. 1892; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 913; attached to Puttalam Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; Anuradhapura Kach., June, 1913; ag. addnl. office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Feb., 1914; extra office asst. to asst. govt. agent, Puttalam and Chilaw, in addition to his own duties, Mar., 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, July, 1917; ditto, Dandagamuwa, July, 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1919; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1920.

COOPER, EVANS GUSTAVUS.—B. 1875; asst. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; clerk of cts., Hanover, 13th Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, Apl. to Aug., 1914.

COOPER, PHILIP WARD, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Marlborough Coll.; col. audit branch, E. and A. Dept., 1st Dec., 1899; asst. local auditor, E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 9th Oct., 1900; ag. local auditor, Uganda, 23rd Dec., 1901 to 15th June, 1902; asst. col. ir., Uganda, 4th Jan., 1904, with seniority from 31st Mar., 1902; collir., 1st A.F., 1905; ag. prov. comntr., Western Prov., Uganda, 9th Sept., 1913.
COOPER, HON. SIR POPPE ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1898), KT. (1904)—Atty.-gen. Queensland, Dec., 1890; northern judge, 5th Jan., 1893; 1st puisne judge, sup. ct.; ch. justice, 1903.

COOPER, ROY SIDNEY FYFE, M.C. (1918).—B. 1893; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Schl., Canterbury; 2nd Lieut., Cheshire Regt., Sept., 1914; temp. capt., May, 1916; demob., Nov., 1919; asst. dist. comntr., Zanzibar, 20th Mar., 1920.
COOPER, HON. SIR THEOPHILUS, KT. BACH. (1921).—B. 1850; puisne judge, sup. ct. of N. Zealand, Feb., 1901-20; retired.
COOPER, WILLIAM D.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 16th Oct., 1920.
COPE, JOHN MATTHEW FLETCHER, F.R.G.S.—B. 1893; capt., Royal Marine Artv.; asst. dist. comntr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.

COPE, WILLIAM VIPOND.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and model schls., Ottawa; clk. and ch. clk. in supt.'s office, C.P.R., Ottawa, 1890-1903; later, divn.-engnr.'s office, Calgary, Alberta, 1903-06; clk., acct., s.b.r., dept. of rly. and canals, Ottawa, 1st Jan., 1907; asst. deptl. audr., 1st Oct., 1911; deptl. audr., 1st Apr., 1913; finan. comptlr., 1st Apr., 1919.

COPP, HON. ARTHUR BLISS.—B. 1870; ed. pub. schls., prov. normal schl., Mt. Allison Univ., New Brunswick, Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax, N.S., Harvard Law Schl. (LL.B. 1894); admitted to bar, N.B. 1895; mem., legis. ass., N.B., 1901-12; el. to H.C., bye-el., Feb., 1915; re-el., g.e., 1917 and 1921; sec. of state of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921.

CORBET, F. H. M.—B. 1862; ed. privately; barrister-at-law, Gray's inn, 1897; librarian of Colombo museum, 1896-1938; ag. sec. of central immigr. bd., Ceylon, 1890-91; hon. exec. offr. and home agent of govt. of Ceylon at Imp. Inst., 1893-1904; hon. sec. of Ceylon gen. com'tee. for Paris Exposition, 1889; mem. of coun., and successively hon. sec. and hon. treasr. of Ceylon branch, Royal Asiatic Soc'y., 1887-1903; recd. Jubilee Gold Medal of Ceylon, 1897; H.M.'s advocate-gen. for Madras Presidency, 1912; author of "The Laws of the Empire," etc., etc.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. tripes, 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept. as 4th asst. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1898; asst. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admstd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in desps., medal and 3 clasps); dep. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admstd. govt., June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr.-in-chief, 15th May, 1906; gov., Uganda, 1st Feb., 1910; gov., St. Helena, 22nd Nov., 1911; assumed govt., 21st Feb., 1912; gov., Bahamas, 1920.

CORMACK, G.—B. 1880; entd. War Office, 1900; army accounts dept., 1905; transf. to col. audit dept. as asst. auditor, G. Coast, 3rd May, 1911; asst. auditor, Hong Kong, 15th Aug., 1912; ag. auditor, 21st Aug. to Oct., 1915, and from 31st July to 14th Aug., 1916.

CORNEY, LEONARD GEORGE.—B.A. Cantab.; classical exhibttr., St. John's Coll., Camb.; class. tripes., 1908; examiner, col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Feb., 1910; asst. auditor, Gold Coast, Aug., 1910; ag. auditor, July, 1913, July to Dec., 1914, and Ap. to Oct., 1916; ag. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1916, to Jan., 1917; ag. senr. asst. auditor, Jan. to Ap., 1917; seconded to G. Coast Regt., 23rd Ap., 1917, to 6th Jan., 1918; ag. senr. asst. auditor, 7th Jan. to 31st Dec., 1918; senr. asst. auditor, 1st Jan., 1919; ag. auditor, Jan. to Oct., 1919; dep. audr., 1st Jan., 1920.
CORNISH-BOWDEN, ATHELSTAN HALL.—Second asst. surv.-gen., Cape Colony, 16th Jan., 1903; ag. surv.-gen., 15th Dec., 1904; apptnt., confirmed, 1st Jan., 1906; is a mem. of the geological comsn.

CORSELLIS, ERNEST SHAWE, L.R.C.P.I., L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (Dub.), 1896; house surg., Jervis St. Hosp., Dublin, 1897; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1902; ag. ch. med. offr., 28th June to 28th Oct., 1912, 13th June, 1917 to 22nd Jan., 1918, 7th Feb. to 13th June, 1920, 19th Apr. to 17th June, 1921 and 4th Sept. to 4th Dec., 1921.

CORSE, LIEUT. E. E.—Ed. Shrewsbury; 2nd lieut., 4th Batt. King's Shropshire Light Infantry (T.), Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1916; served in France, 1917-18; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 3rd Dec., 1919.

CORY, WILLIAM WALLACE, C.M.G. (1909).—B. 1865; entd. civ. serv., Canada, 1901; apptnt. inspr. Yukon offrs., 1901; asst. comsnr., Dominion lands, 1904; dep. min. of the Interior, 1905; also comsnr. of N.W.T., June, 1919.

CORYNDON, SIR ROBERT THORNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1870; joined Bechuanaland Border Police, under B.S.A. Co., 9th Nov., 1889, and the Pioneer force for the occupation of Mashonaland, June, 1890; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. C. J. Rhodes, 1896-7; and during parly. inquiry into Jameson Raid; served in Matabele War, 1893, and Matabele Rebellion, 1896 (medal and clasp); Brit. res. with Lewanika, and B.S.A. Co.'s representative in Barotseland, June, 1897; took expdn. to Lealui, Upper Zambesi River, 1897; admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1900; dep. res. comsnr., Swaziland, 15th April, 1907; res. comsnr., Swaziland, 15th Oct., 1907; chmn., S. Rhodesia native reserves comsn., 1914-15; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 13th January, 1916; gov., Uganda, 27th Nov., 1917; assumed govt., 10th Feb., 1918.

COSSER, S. C. A.—B. 1880; elk., treasy., Transvaal, 8th May, 1911; asst. acctnt., treasy., 1st Dec., 1905; asst. acctnt., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; provincial acctnt., Transvaal Prov., 21st Nov., 1910.
COSTELLOE, MATHEW.—B. 1866; detective sub-inspector, Trinidad, 7th June, 1911; detective-insp. of constab., 1916.

COSTLEY-WHITE, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1918).—B.A. Oxon., 1900; elk. to armed forces, B.C. Africa, Apl., 1900; asst. collr., Nyasaland Prot. Mar., 1904; 2nd grade res., Jan., 1911; ag. supt. of native affairs, Sept., 1916, to May, 1919; transport offr. and carrier recruiting offr., Nyassaland Field Force, with temp. rank of major, 1916-18 (ment. in desps.); ag. chief sec. to govt., May, 1919 to June, 1920; prov. comsnr., 1st June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., Oct., 1921.

COTTLE, HENRY ROSS.—B. 1887; supr. monotype dept., govt. printing office, Ceylon, June, 1909; ag. 3rd asst. govt. printer, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1916, to May, 1917; on military duty, 1918; ag. govt. printer, Sept., 1919; govt. printer, Dec., 1919.

COULSON, NORMAN.—B. 1891; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1913 (2nd cls. hons., class., 1913, 2nd cls. hons., mod. hist., 1914); M.A. 1917; 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Regt. Cav., 12th Sept., 1914; attd. 8th (K.R.L.) Hussars, 20th Sept., 1915; cadet, F.M.S., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. agt., food contr., Kedah, 27th Nov., 1920; ag. sec., sany. bds., Alor Star, 1st Feb., 1921.
COULTER, ROBERT MILLER, C.M.G. (1967), M.D.—Ed. Toronto and Victoria Univ.; practised med. at Aurora, Ontario, for several years; deputy postm.-gen. of Canada since Aug., 1897.
COUNSEL, E. A., I.S.O.—Tasmanian govt. dist. survr., 1889; survr.-gen. and sec. for lands, 1894; represented Commonwealth of Australia and Dominion of New Zealand at Imp. Conf. of Survrs.-Gen., 1911.

COUPER, SYDNEY.—Ed. at Kelvinside Acad. and Univ. Coll., Victoria Univ., Liverpool; served with L. & N.W. rly.; sen. asst. engr., Uganda rly.; dist. engr., Uganda rly.; awarded Africa General Service (Nandi, 1905-6) Medal; director of rlyws., Jamaica, 1912; gen. man., Uganda rly., 1919.

COUSINS, CLARENCE WILFRED.—M.A.; elk. col. sec.'s dept., Cape, 1896; dep.-inspr. of prisons, 1901; prin. elk. and offr. in charge of imigrn. and labour, 1905; ch. imigrn. offr., 1906; prin. imigrn. offr. and ch. elk. in charge dept. of interior, Cape Town, 1913; prin. imigrn. offr. and regsr. of Asiatics, Pretoria, 1916; dir. of census, Union of S. Africa, 1917.

COUSINS, HERBERT HENRY, M.A., F.C.S.—Late postm., Merton Coll., Oxford; 1st class hons. nat. science, 1889; lecturer in chemistry, S.E. Agric. Coll., Ventnor, 1894; govt. analytical and agric. chemist, Jamaica, 1900; nominated M.L.C., 1907; mem. of bd. of agric.; chemist in charge of sugar experimental statn.; author of publications on chemistry and agriculture.
COUZENS, CHARLES.—B. 1861; apptnt., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 11th Jan., 1892; promoted 2nd cl. mess., Nov., 1899; King's mess., 1st Feb., 1902; asst. office-keeper, 3rd July, 1915.
COWAN, J.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., Aug., 1912.

COWELL, H. RUSSELL, B.A.—B. 1877; ed. Malvern and Cambridge; scholar of Clare, 1896-9; apptnt., after compet. exam., elk. sec.'s dept. inland revenue, Oct., 1901; 2nd cl. elk., C.O., 27th Mar., 1902; sec. to inter-deptmtl. comtee. on oil fields of W. Indies, 1904; sec. to Roy. Coms. on trade relations between Canada and W. Indies, 1909-1910; priv. sec. to Sir J. Anderson, G.C.M.G., 26th June, 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emott, 9th Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Ilington, 8th Aug., 1914; ag. 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 10th Mar., 1916; ag. prin. elk., 1st Jan., 1920; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920.

COWGILL, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1888; ed. Durham Sch., and All Souls Coll., Oxford (bible-clerks), 1907-11; 3rd cls. mods., 3rd cls. lit. hum., R.A., 1911; Malayan civ. serv., 1911; studied Chinese, 1912-14; 2nd lieut., 4th battn. Sherwood Foresters, Mar., 1915; lieut., 1917; seconded to R.E. Signals, 1916; served in France with Signal Service, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; ment. in despatches, 1919; retd. to Malaya, 1919; ag. rev. audr., Aug., 1919; ag. ast. sec. to rev., Selangor, Sept., 1919; dist. offr., Temerloh, Dec., 1919; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and N. Simbilan, Oct., 1920.
COWPER, HERBERT WELLINGTON.—B. 1883; ed. at St. Paul's School, London, Hertford Coll., Oxford, and in Germany, France and Italy; last res., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910.

COX, CHARLES EDWARD LANGLETON.—B. 1889; std. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1907; 2nd cls. clk., std. sec.'s office; called to the bar, 1918; stip. mag. for the colony; revising barrister, 1919; served in France as lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.); injured 4 Loss, Sept., 1916; invalided out of army, Nov., 1917; served under Admiralty as A.T.S.O., mining depot, Grangemouth, Mar. to Dec., 1918; xtra A.D.C. to Sir W. Egerton, K.C.M.G., 913-15; lieut., artillery coy., B. Guiana Militia, since 1908.

COX, SIR CHARLES T., K.C.M.G. (1913), K.M.G. (1903).—B. 1888; Govt. sec.'s office, B. Guiana, July, 1874; sen. clk., 1883; ch. clk. and sec. to the quarantine bd., 1888; sec. to the royal comm. on adminstr.-gen.'s dept. of Br. Guiana, 1882; called to the bar, Mid. Term., 1899; compiled "Index to Laws of Br. Guiana," 1887 (2nd edition, 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; ast. gov't. sec., and clk. to ct. of policy and combined ct., 1889; also clk. of exec. cun., 1892; ag. gov't. sec., in 1893, 1894, 1895, and 1896, with seat in exec. cun. and legislature; mem. of comites. on pensions, 1898; ag. ast. sec. on several occasions; admiral. of St. Kitts and Nevis, 1899-1904; ag. gov't., Leeward Is., July to Oct., 1903, and May to Sept., 1904; gov't. sec. and ag. gov't., B. Guiana, Sept., 1904; Ap. Oct., 1906; Ap. to Oct., 1909, and Aug., 1911 to Apr., 1912.

COX, E. A. W.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., Mar., 1886; ast. res., May, 1891; of Lundr., June, 1896; res., 2nd class, Mar., 1898, of Trusan and Pan May, 1899; in charge of Lawas, 16th Apr., 1899, in addition to Trusan.

COX, HUGH BERTRAM, C.B. (1902).—B. 1861; Lt. Westminster schl. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; 1st am classical mods., 1881; 1st class final clas. hl., 1883; M.A. and B.C.L., 1884; called to the ir. 1885, and joined S. Wales circuit; assisted sec. atty.-gen. (Sir R. Webster) in partly. and offic. work, 1886-1897; jun. counsel to the treasy. in ex-gratia cases, 1892, and to H.M. Customs, 1896; employed in connection with the Br. Guiana dry. question, and as one of the jun. counsel in the preparations of the Br. case in the arbitrn.; pal ast. under-sec., C.O., Nov., 1897 to 1910; mem. of Indian emigr. comitee, 1909; solr. to the Board of Inland Revenue, 1911.
COX, N. B.—Imp. customs serv., Feb., 1906; st. to chief of customs, E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dep. chief of customs, Apl., 1914; ch. of customs, Zanzibar, Feb., 1915.

COX, MAJOR-GEN. SIR PERTH ZACHARIAH, G.C.M.G. (1922), G.C.I.E. (1917), K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1920), K.C.I.E. (1911), C.S.I. (1909), C.I.E. (1902), F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1864; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined army 1884; served with 2nd batt. Cameronians till 1889; joined Indian Staff Corps, 1889; Indian polit. dep., 1890; vice-consul, Zaire, Somali Coast, 1893; Barbera, 1894-5; consul and polit. agt., Muscat, 1890-1904; consul-gen., Bushire, 1904; polit. res., Persian Gulf, 1909; sec., foreign dept. govt. of India, 1914; ch. polit. offr., Indian Expdy. Force "D," 1914; ment. in despatches, 1916; ag. British min. to Persia, 1919-20; high commr., Mesopotamia, 1920.
COX, WILFRED BERNARD.—B. 1896; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; col. audit dept., May, 1920; ast. audr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1920.

COX, SIR WILLIAM HENRY LIONEL, KNT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1844; substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, Feb., 1880; puisne judge of the sup. ct., Aug., 1880; procureur and advoc.-gen., Aug., 1886; ch. justice, S. Sttlmtns, 1893; ret., 1906.
COXON, S. N. H.—Tempy. ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1919.

COZENS-HARDY, EDGAR WRIGLEY.—B. 1872; ast. survr. of roads, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Feb., 1901; ast. engr., G. Coast rly., Aug., 1903; maintenance engr., G. Coast rly., June, 1906; seconded as engr.-in-charge, Koforidua rly. extn., 1912-16; gen. man., G. Coast rly., July, 1919.

CRABB, RICHARD BRUCE.—B. 1891; ed. Cranleigh Sch.; lieut. (S.R.) 3rd Batt. E. Surrey Regt.; qualified Indian Police exam., 1911; passed Tropical African Services Course (all subjects), 1915; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 28th Apl., 1915.
CRAIG, ERNEST DUNCAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.Z.S.—B. 1888; ed. Aberdeen Univ. and at Exeter Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt. educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 17th Jan., 1920.
CRAIG, JOHN.—B. 1871; educ. at Glasgow and Glasgow Univ.; trained certif. teacher; principal, Benoh pub. sch., O.R.C., 1906; inspr. of schla., O.R.C., July, 1909.

CRAIG, JOHN.—B. 1884; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1st cl. hon., Classics; univ. prize in Latin, 1906; and at Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 2nd cl. hon., Lit. Hum., 1908; appld., after exam., examr., E. and A. dept., 5th Jan., 1909; ast. auditor, S. Nigeria, May, 1909; temporarily translt. to N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; ast. local auditor, N. Nigeria, Nov.-Dec., 1909; joined col. audit dept., Jan., 1910; auditor, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to gov', Sir W. Collet, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1914; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov', R. Walter, C.M.G., gov', W. Hart-Bennett, C.M.G., and gov', Eyre-Hutton, C.M.G., 1915-19; lieut., B. Honduras Territorial Force; audr., Mauritius, Apr., 1920.

CRAIG, WM.—Supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore; ast. supt. money order branch and savings bank, Oct., 1903, to June, 1905; ast. ast. P.M.G., Singapore, Mar., 1907; supt. money order branch and govt. savings bank, Singapore, Feb., 1908; ast. ast. postmr.-gen., Singapore, June, 1909; ast. ast. postmr.-gen., Singapore, Jan., 1912; ast. ast. postmr.-gen., in addition, Apr., 1918; ast. ast. postmr.-gen., June, 1919.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C.—Ag. priv. sec. to gov', Falkland Islds., Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1897; priv. sec. to adminstr., Apr., 1897; elk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov', Nov., 1898; treasy.

CREMER, JOHN AWDRY.—B. 1831; B.A. (Oxon.), 1903; clk., col. audit branch, R. and A.D., 1903; astt. auditor, Lagos, 1905; astt. auditor, Nyasaland, 1908; 2nd cls. clk., cent. office, 1913; auditor, Nyasaland, 1914.

CRERAR, HON. THOMAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1876; ed. pub. schl. and coll. inst. Portage la Prairie, Manitoba, and Manitoba Coll., Winnipeg; farmer; dir. of the Grain Growers Grain Co. Ltd., Winnipeg; now pres. of United Grain Growers Ltd.; joined fed. union govt., Canada as min. of agriculture, Oct., 1917; resigned portfolio, 1919; el. for Marquette, g.e., 1917; re-el. 1921; leader of the progressive party in H.C.

CRESSWELL, GEORGE HENRY.—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schl., and in Dublin; astt. supt. of pubs. wks., Kisumu, E. Africa Prot., 13th June, 1904; ag. exec. engin., Fort Hall, June, 1905; exec. engin., 1st Apr., 1909.

CRESWELL, REAR-ADMR. SIR WILLIAM ROOKE, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1897).—Joined R.N. Dec., 1866; lieut., 1873, for service in action at Laroot River (wounded); engaged in suppression of slave trade on African coast, 1875-77; ret. from Navy, 1878; apptd. to S. Australian naval force, 1885; comdr., 1891; post capt., 1895; naval comdt., 1893-1900; commanded H.M.C.S. "Protector" to China during Boxer outbreak, 1900; naval comdt. of Queensland, 1909-10; dir. of commonwealth naval forces, 1904; attended Imp. Conf. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; rear-admiral, 1911; retired, 1919.

CREWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911); ROBERT OFFLEY ASHBURTON CREWE-MILNES, G.H., P.C., M.A., F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Great Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl of Crewe (cr. 1895); Earl of Maleyde, (cr. 1911) ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; astt. priv. sec. to sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), 1883-84; lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-95; lord pres. of the coun., 1905-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr. 1908; Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with Secretaryship of State) 1908; S. of S. for India 1910-15; accompanied H.M. King George V. on the occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; lord pres. of the coun., 1915.

CREWE, BRIG.-GEN. THE HON. SIR CHARLES PRESTON, K.C.M.G. (1915), C.B. (1900).—B. 1855; served in Cape M.R., 1878-81; M.L.A. for Griquaand, 1899; M.L.A. for Alwal North as col. sec., Feb., 1904, to June, 1907; sec. for agric., June, 1907; raised and was major commanding Border Horse Regt., 1900; promoted to lieut.-col., May, 1900; colonel, 1901; in command of western div. of Cape, Nov., 1901, to termination of hostilities; ret. from Cape Colony forces, 31st Dec., 1902; sec. for agric., June, 1907, to Feb., 1908; M.L.A., East London, 1908; mem. for East London in Union parl., 1910 to 1918.
CREWE-READ, E. C.—Astt. dist. commr., E.A.P., 5th Jan., 1907; dist. commr., Apr. 1913.
CRICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; medical offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914.

CRICHTON, REGINALD—B. 1877; ed. privately and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Perak, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; passed in Malay, May, 1902; passed in law, Oct., 1902; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 30th May, 1902; ditto, Brusa, 23rd Oct., 1902; ag. astt. dist. offr., Gopeng, 21st Feb., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, 16th Sept., 1904; ag. astt. regir. offr., Ipoh, 16th Mar., 1906; dist. trear., Ranau, 1st Aug., 1906; state trear., Pahang, 1st Jul. 1907; ag. regar., sup. ct., 23rd Jan., 1908 to 31st Oct., 1911; dist. offr., Kuantan, 24th Mar., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, 5th June, 1908; ag. regar. of sup. ct., 26th Nov., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1912; asst. adviser to govt. of Johore at Muar (seconded), 23rd Apr., 1913; ag. offr. of cls. sec., S. Stltms., 7th Feb., 1915; offr. of cls. III., 1st Jan., 1918; cls. II., 1st Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 7th Mar., 1920.

CRITTIEN, ATTILIO, M.B.E. (1919), M.A., M.D., Malta Univ., D.P.H., D.T.M. (Liverpool), 1906—B. 1872; demonstr., schl. of anatomy, 1899 to 1901; civil surgeon attchd. R.A.M.C., 1901 to 1904; med. offr. of hth., Malta, 1906 to 1917; chf. gort. med. offr. and asst. pub. hth., with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1917.

CROFTON, RICHARD HAYES.—B. 1880; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. class, tripus, 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 1st July, 1901; clk., audit dept., Hong Kong, 25th Aug., 1903; ag. local auditor, 14th Feb., to 22nd Dec., 1904; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 11th Mar., 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of coun., for periods in 1907, 1911, 1912, 1913; asst. chief sec., Zanzibar, 6th Feb., 1914; ag. chief sec., for various periods, 1914-18; ag. sec., admtn. Tanganyika Terry., Dec., 1919, to Mar., 1920; ag. treas., Zanzibar, July to Aug., and Oct. to Dec., 1920.
CRONSHAW, THEODORE JAMES, M.A.—B. 1890; ed. Sherborne Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn., dept., N. Nigeria, 3rd Dec., 1913; attchd. Nigeria Regt., 1915-16.

CROOK, ALFRED H., B.A.—Ed. at Wesley Coll., Dublin; honorand gold medallist, Trin. Coll., Dublin; M.A., 1905; F.R.G.S.; asst. mast., Wesley Coll., Dublin; jnr. ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, Oct., 1902; sen. ast., 1908; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of Med., 1903-1912; ag. 2nd mast., Jan., 1914 to Aug., 1915; ag. headmast., Aug.-Sept., 1915; headmast., Yamati govt. schl., Feb., 1917; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., July, 1918; ast. mast., cls. I., Jan., 1920.

CROOK, JOHN ROWLAND, O.B.E. (1914), M.I.C.E.—B. 1866; entered Crystal Pal. Engrg. Schl., 1881; ast. to E. F. Griffith, C.E. (consulting sanitary engr.), 1883; supt. of drawing office, C. P. Engrg. Schl., 1888; awarded 1st prize for civ. engrg., City of Lon. Coll., 1889; ast. engrg. water and drainage dept., Hong Kong, 1891-92; exec. engrg., P.W.D., with control of sewerage and water wks., 1893-1900; in charge of roads and telephones, 1897; surrv. to sanitary bd., 1892-1906; read. thanks of govt. and S. of S. for services during plague, 1894; govt. engrg., Gibraltar, 1900; took over crown lands dept., 1903; offr. for rates and expenses local revenue, 1907; san. comrn., 1919; ag. inspr. of schls., 1919; chief ast. and cable censor, 1914-19; local priority authority, (Ministry of Munitions), 1917-19.

CROSSIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919).—M.H.A., Hav de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comtee., 1915; chmn., tonnage comtee., 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSSMAN, ARNOLD LANGLEY—B. 1885; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1908; off. ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa Prov., Dec., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Nov., 1910; pol. mag., Puttalam, May, 1911; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, Jan., 1913; dist. judge, Ratnapura, June, 1914; office ast. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1915; attchd. to censor's office, Oct., 1917; ast. settm't. offr., Oct., 1917; ag. dep. commr. of stamps, Oct., 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Galle, July, 1921; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1921.

CUBITT, GEORGE EATON STANNARD.—B. 1874; ed. Ipswich Schl. and Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill; ast. comnr., Indian f. rest serv., Burma, 12th Dec., 1896; dep. comnr., 19th Nov., 1903; ast. inspr.-gen., forests, Simla, 1st Aug., 1912; on deputation to S.S. and F.M.S. as comnr., forests, 1st Apr., 1915; ret. from Ind. serv. and confirmed as comnr., forests, S.S. and F.M.S., 1st Apr., 1920.

CUBITT, THOMAS ASTLEY, Major (local lieut.-col.), C.M.G. (1916).—D.S.O.; R.F.A., 1903; lead comdg. corps of mounted infantry, Nigeria; entd. army, 18:1; capt., 1:00; served in W. Africa, 18:8 (medal with clasp); expedition against Munshie, 1900 (desp., clasp); W. Africa, 1901, as staff offr. (desp., brevet major, medal with clasp); W. Africa, 1902 (desp., clasp); W. Africa, 1903; Kano-Sokoto campaign (desp., clasp, D.S.O.); offr. comdg. troops and dep. commr., Somaliland Prot., July, 1914.

CULLEN, HON. SIR WM. PORTUS, K.C.M.G. (1912), KT. BACH. (1911), K.C., M.A., LL.D.—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South Wales, July, 1891; M.L.C., Aug., 1895; fellow of Senate of Sydney Univ. since 1896; vice-chancellor, 1908; resigned from legis. coun. and apptd. chief just. of N.S.W., Jan., 1910; lieut. gov. N.S.W., 30th Mar., 1910; chancellor of Sydney Univ., since Sept., 1914.
CULLIGAN, HON. ARTHUR.—B. 1879; lumberman and farmer; elected to legis. assem., New Brunswick, 1912; min. without portfolio in Murray admtn., 1917.
CULMER, J. J.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1866; ast. surgeon, I'ahamas asylum, 18:8; res. surg., gen. hosp., 1905; ag. chief med. offr., Dec., 1919, and in Jan. and July, 1920.
CULMER, J. S.—B. 1868; mtd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1894; Out Island comnr. (3rd div.), 1909; (2nd div.), 1914.
CUMMING, JAMES ANTHONY TENCH.—B. 1864; entered civ. ser., Trinidad, 6th Jan., 1886; 2nd clk., rec-gen.'s dept., and acctnt. to comnr.s. of currency, 19th Nov., 1912.

CUNNINGHAM, CHARLES ALUM.—B. 1877; ed. at Merchiston and Edin. Univ.; passed final exam., C.A. (Edin.), 1901; ast. acctnt., W.A.F.F., N. Nigeria, 1901; ast. treas., 1902; ag. chief comtn. acctnt., Baro-Kano rly., Dec., 1907, to June, 1908; dep. and ag. chief acctnt. Baro-Kano rly., July, 1912; dist. acctnt., Nigerian rly., 1913; chief constn. acctnt., Nigerian (Eastern) rly., 1914; has acted as chief ast. treas., N. Nigeria.

CUNYNGHAME, SIR PERCY F., BART.—B. 1867; cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., July, 1886; ast. res. of Trusan, May, 1890; res. (2nd class), Mar., 1896; lieut. 3rd battn. Middlesex Rifles, May, 1897; capt., Oct., 1901; ag. res., Limbang, May, 1899; res., 2nd class, Upper Rejang, Aug., 1901; commdt., Sarawak Rangers, July, 1903; res., 1st div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904; ret., 1908.
CURLEWIS, HERBERT RAINE, B.A., LL.B.—Judge of industrial arbitn. ct., New S. Wales, 1917.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advocate, sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advocate, high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel for Imperial govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge of high ct. (late S.A.R.), 15th Apr., 1899 to 31st Jan., 1900; pres. of crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, 2nd Nov., 1899 to 31st Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Sept., 1903; puisne judge, sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal Prov. Div.), 31st May, 1910.
CURRIE, TANCREDI, M.V.O. (1907).—Asst. supt. of pol., Malta, 1890; sen. asst., 1902; supt., 1903; ret., Sept., 1915.
CURRY, H. A. G.—Formerly chief inspr. and under sec., dept. of lands, New South Wales; pres. of land appeal ct., July, 1904.
CURRY, H. D.—B. 1893; ed. Dulwich coll.; cadet, Solomon Islands, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; on mily. serv., 1917-19; 2nd lieut., 1919; priv. sec. to gov. of B. Honduras, 1919.

CURWEN, HENRY.—B. 1879; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond and Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.); D.P.H. (Durham); certif. dis. trop. clim. (Edin.); late house surg., Royal Infirmary, Edin.; temp. plague offr., Zanzibar govt., Oct., 1905; ag. bacteriologist, 1906; senr. asst. med. offr., 1st Jan., 1907; ag. P.M.O., 1st May, 1915; P.M.O., 23rd Nov., 1915; ag. M.O.H. in addition, Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1920; 4th cls. order El Aliyeh; 3rd cls. order Brilliant Star.

CUSACK, HENRY VERNON.—B. 1895; ed. Aravon Schol. and Royal Coll. of Sci., Ireland; served in European War, Flanders and N. Russia, 1915-19; granted rank of capt., 1919; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, July, 1920; ag. audr., Oct.-Nov., 1921.

CUSACK-SMITH, SIR THOMAS BERRY, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Eton; barrister, Middle Temp., 1884; law scholarship, 1883; formerly lieut., 1st V.B. Essex regt.; apptd. H.M. Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890, and H.M. dep. consmr. for the W. Pacific at Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curaçoa" to Union Group to report on land claims, Aug., 1892; dep. consmr. for the Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of res. in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial consmr., Apr., 1893; apptd. to license and superintendent employment of Gilbert Is. natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894; consul-gen., Valparaiso, 1897.

CUSCADEN, GEORGE PERCY.—B. 1883; ed. Conig Schol., Dublin; attd. to Roy. Irish Constab., Nov., 1902 to Aug., 1903; ag. asst. supt., pol., S. Stilts., Dec., 1903; asst. consmr., pol. F.M.S., May, 1904; ag. dist. supt. of prisons, N. Sembilan, in addn. ch. pol. offr., May-June, 1907; ag. adjt., pol. depot, Apr.-Aug., 1908; consmr., pol., Johore, and pub. pros. in addn., Apr., 1912, to Apr., 1913; consmr. of pol., Johore, 1st July, 1915; offr. commgdg., Johore Vol. Rifles, Sept., 1915, to Aug., 1916; dep. consmr., pol., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1919.
CUSSEN, HON. LEO FINN BERNARD.—B. 1859; judge of sup. ct., Victoria, since Mar., 1906.

CUTFIELD, A. J.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. serv., Dec., 1904; asst. res., Jan., 1907; 2nd cls. res., June, 1908; resig., Dec., 1908; asst. dist. consmr., G. Coast, 3rd Aug., 1910; dist. consmr., Ashanti, 27th Jan., 1915.

CUTHBERT, MORTON.—Ed. Edin. Instit. and Glasgow and West of Scot. Tech. Coll.; apprent. engrnr. to Messrs. Dick, Kerr and Co., Ltd., London, 1895; electr. with Glasgow corpn. elec. dept., 1899; asst. engrnr. Govan corpn. elec. dept., 1901; chief asst. engrnr., Oban corpn. elec. dept., 1904; electr. engrnr., B. Honduras, 1905; exec. and electr. engrnr., P.W.D., B. Honduras, 1909; lieut., B.H.T.F., 1917; ag. adjt. B.H.T.F., Aug., 1917 to Jan., 1918; hon. A.D.C. to gov., B. Honduras, 1919.
CUTLER, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1870; entered civ. ser., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1890; asst. collr. of customs, 16th Dec., 1909.

CUZEN, ALLAN LECKIE.—Served in R.E., 1901-2; S. African War (medal and five clasps); Cape police, 1902-6; Bech. Prot. police, 1906-14; 2nd clk. to asst. consmr., N. Dist., Bech. Prot., 1914; ag. asst. res. mag., ditto, 1915.

DALE, CHARLES ERNEST, C.M.G. (1914), C.B.E. (1920).—B. 1867; ed. Greenwich and King's Coll., London; fell. soc. acctnts. and auditors, fell. roy. statistical soc.; apptd. dir. customs Niger Coast Protec., Jan., 1895; asst. postmr.-gen., Apr., 1896; ag. dir.-gen. customs and postmr.-gen., Feb., to Sept., 1896, Oct., 1897, to June, 1898, Oct., 1899, to Feb., 1900; Dec., 1900, to July, 1901; employed re-organising treasury, Oct., 1897; treas. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1898; ag. sec., Feb. to Apr., 1900; Jan. to Apr., 1903, and Dec., 1905, to Feb., 1906; dir. pub. officers' guarantee fund, Apr., 1900; treas., Lagos, Sept., 1904; financial consmr. to combined admin. of Lagos and S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; and mem. of leg. and exec. couns., ag. col. sec., 7th Jan. to 5th May, 1908; dep. gov., 7th Jan. to 5th May, 1908; ret., 1914; asst. passport offr., F.O., 1914-15; ag. clk., col. audit dept., 1915-17; dep. asst. dir., W.O., 1917-19.

DALLAS, FRANCIS HENRY.—Ed. Univ. Coll. School; asst. sec. Penang municipality, 1895; ag. sec., May, 1896; Sarawak civ. ser.; ag. editor Sarawak Gazette, and manager, Sarawak govt. printing office, 1897; ag. treas. and clk., supreme coun., June, 1902; treas. and recorder of sup. coun., 1903; mem. of comte. of adminstr., 1903-4.

DALLIN, THOMAS.—Late lieut. 3rd Batt. Essex Regt.; served in S. African War (medal and two clasps); hon. 2nd lieut. in the army, 6th Oct., 1902; joined col. audit dept., 22nd Feb., 1904; examr. exchequer and audit dept., 1st July, 1906; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, 6th Oct., 1906, to 15th Feb., 1910; reverted to ex. and audit dept., 16th Feb., 1910; asst. auditor, Hong Kong, 30th July, 1910; ag. auditor, 22nd July to 15th Sept., 1911, 22nd Aug. to 13th Nov., 1912, 1st June to 28th Oct., 1913, 5th June to 13th Sept., 1914; asst. cable censor, Aug.-Oct., 1914; on mil. serv., 7th Mar., 1915 to 13th July, 1916; ag. audr., 18th Aug., 1916 to 8th Dec., 1917; J.P., 1918; attd. to finan. branch, W.O., Sept., 1918 to Mar., 1919; ag. audr., 8th June to 19th Aug., 1920.

DALTON, JAMES.—Joined Imperial army, 25th Sept., 1877; served in Afghan war, 1878-1880; was present at the siege and battles around Kandahar (injured); joined English convict prison serv., 17th Oct., 1883; apptd. to Portsmouth convict prison; principal warden general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 18th Mar., 1891; chief warden, St. Catherine dist. prison, 16th May, 1901; ag. supt. of prison, 1908; keeper, Suva gaol, Fiji, 23rd Sept., 1909; supt., 1st Jan., 1914; man., Suva cemetery (conjunct), 1st Feb., 1915; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1st June, 1916 to 6th March, 1917, and from 29th Aug., 1917.

DALTON, THE REV. JOHN NEALE, M.A., K.C.V.O. (1911), C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1839; was gov. to T.R.H. Princes Albert Victor and George of Wales, and ag. chaplain to H.M.S. "Bacchant," chaplain in ord. to Her Majesty, 1886; canon of Windsor, 1885.

DALTON, LLANELYN CHISHOLM.—B. 1879; ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1900 (Hist. Tripos); M.A., 1905; called to the bar. Gray's Inn, 1901; legal ast., land settlmt. bd., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; ast. res. mag. and J.P., June, 1902 to Nov., 1910; advoc., sup. ct., 1904; regar., B. Guiana, Nov., 1910; judge advoc., local forces, 1914; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1912 to Feb., 1913; Nov., 1914 to Jan., 1915; July, 1916, to June, 1917; July to Sept., 1918; Mar. to June, 1919; ag. solr.-gen., May to Aug., 1914; Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915; ag. atty.-gen., Aug.-Sept., 1914; Feb.-Apr., 1918; ag. chief just., July-Sept., 1917; June-Oct., 1919; puisne judge, B. Guiana, June, 1919; compiled and edited "Statutory Rules and Orders (B. Guiana)," to 1914, 2 vols.; editor, B. Guiana Law Reports, 9 vols.; an ast. editor "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws," 2nd edit., vol. iv.

DALY, MAURICE DOMINIC.—B. 1875; ed. Downside Coll. and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 1st Dec., 1898; passed in Malay and Siamese, 1899; cadet, May, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Oct., 1901; ast. dist. offr., Pekan, Mar., 1902; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1903; offr., cls. VI., Jan., 1903; cls. V., Jan., 1906; ag. ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, July, 1906; ag. mag., Seremban, July, 1907; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Sept., 1908; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1911; offr., cls. IV., Feb., 1911; ast. sec. to ch. sec., cont. to ast. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1911; ag. ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, June, 1913; ch. ast. dist. offr., cls. III., Kinta, Dec., 1913; dist. offr., Krian, Apr., 1918; dep. pub. proc., Perak, July, 1918; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; dep. pub. proc., F.M.S., Feb., 1919; ag. commnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., as offr., cls. I.A., Apr., 1921.

DALZIEL, JOHN McKWEN, M.D.C.M. (Edin.), 1865; M.D. (Edin.), 1903; certif. in Trop. Diseases (Edin.), 1903; B.Sc. Pub. Health (Edin.) 1904; diploma Trop. Med. (Liverpool), 1904; F.L.S.; in China from 1895 to 1902; joined W.A.M.S., 14th Jan., 1905; sen. sanitary offr., G. Coast, 28th Dec., 1918; author of "A Haase Botanical Vocabulary" (Fisher Unwin), 1916.

DANIEL, JAMES HARCOURT.—B. 1866; ast. supt. of police, Ceylon, Jan., 1904; supt. of police, Feb., 1913; deputy inspr. gen., of police, C.I.D., Sept., 1915; ag. office ast. to ast. govt. ast., Matare, Sept., 1921.
DANIEL, REGINALD YOUNG, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attld. to Galle Kachcheri, Oct., 1920.

DANIEL, ROWLAND MORTIMER.—B. 1873; ed. at St. Edmund's, Canterbury; 2nd lieut. (glam. Artil. Militia, 1893; certif. of Musk., Hythe; capt. and instr. of gunnery, 1898; sub. inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. divn., B.S.A. pol., 1898; inspr., 1901; served through the S.
