HARCOURT, HAROLD WALLACE—B. 1886; examng. clk., audit dept., Fiji, Nov., 1909; book-keeper, treas., May, 1910; chief audr., Tonga, under three years agreement with native govt., June, 1911; audr.-gen. and govt. sec., Sept., 1911; ag. premier, 1st Jan. to 25th Feb., 1914; 2nd asst. audr., Fiji, 14th Oct., 1914; ag. ch. clk., treas., 15th Oct., 1918.

HARDING, ALFRED J., O.B.E. (1918)—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip, part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comte., to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; sec., colonial blue-books comte., 1917; asst. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920.

HARDING, COLONEL COLIN, C.M.G. (1886), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1863; formerly ch. inspr. of native police, Mashonaland; served in Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; comdt. of Barotse native pol., 1901-1906; dist. commnr., Northern territories, G. Coast, 1910; seconded for milly. serv., 1914; prov. commnr., 14th Feb., 1915; resumed duty in G. Coast, 9th May, 1917.

HARDING, EDWARD J., C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apprd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comte., 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Comm., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 10th Apr., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apr., 1916; sec. to comte. for attitl. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.; ag. prin. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1921.
HARDY, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1900; ent. C.O., 3rd Mar., 1916; apptd. asst. clk., C.O., after compet. exam., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES, C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1863; clk. treas., W. Australia, Apl., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. govt. treas. Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec. W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1891; ag. agent-gen., 1909.
HARKNESS, EDWARD BURNS.—Entered treas., New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., depts. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, 16th Apr., 1910; ag. protectorate med. offr., 2nd Jan. to 11th May, 1911.

HARMAN, H. A. A. F., D.S.O.—B. Sc.(Lond.), Capt. 4th (S.R.) Batt., S. Staff. Regt.; prin., govt. training inst. for teachers, Accra, Gold Coast, 17th Apr., 1912; ag. adjt. Gold Coast volunteers, 29th Jan. to 30th July, 1915; supervising offr., No. 5 Young Officer Compy., West Lancs. Divn., 29th Nov., 1915, to 8th Mar., 1916 (temp. major); ag. G. Coast Regt., 14th May, 1916, to 31st Dec., 1918, for service in E. Africa (slightly wounded twice ment. in desps.); major, 2nd in command 4th Batt. Gold Coast Regt., 1st Nov., 1918; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, Dec., 1919.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; to bil. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HARPER, CHARLES HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 5th May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, 24th Jan., 1900; attached to Ashanti field force, 1st June to 1st Sept., 1900; asst. dist. commr., 6th Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., 26th Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., 8th Feb., 1906; ag. chief asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 14th Apr. to 25th June, 1906; cantonment mag., Coomassie, 16th Oct., 1906; prov. commr., 23rd July, 1909; cenm. offr., 1911; ag. other asst. and clk. of couns., 16th May to 29th June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912, and from 3rd May to 27th Aug., 1914; attached to Togoland field force, 29th Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, 30th Sept. to 23rd Dec., 1914; chief asst. col. sec., 14th Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec., 26th June to 17th Nov., 1915, 27th Nov., 1915, to 27th June, 1916, and from 2nd Dec., 1916, to 23rd Apr., 1917.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; conf. clk., Nov., 1912.
HARRAGIN, ALFRED ERNEST ALBERT.—B. 1877; sub-inspr., constab., Trinidad, 1st Feb., 1903; inspr., 1st Nov., 1912; now serving with the army as temp. major.

HARRAGIN, WALTER.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; clk. of the peace and sub-registr., San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, 9th Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary captain; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1915.

HARRAGIN, WILLIAM THORNTON.—B. 1887; clk., sup. ct., Trinidad, 1910; asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., 1911; held comm. in Gordon Highlanders, 1915-16; served in France, May to Oct., 1915; asst. mast., Queen Victoria Schl., Fiji, 1916; supervisor of customs, 2nd cls., G. Coast, 1st June, 1918.

HARREL, MELVILLE DAVID.—B. 1872; attchd. Royal Irish constab. depot, 1895; certif., drill and police duties; sub-inspr. Jamaica constab., Jan., 1896; spcl. serv. to Nicaragua, Jan., 1897; 3rd cl. inspr., Nov., 1901; 2nd cl., Feb., 1907; 1st cl., June, 1913; deputy inspr.-gen., Oct., 1913; ag. inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, July to Sep., 1914; inspr.-gen. of police and comdt. local forces, Barbados, Aug., 1916; local rank of lieut.-col., 1919.

HARRIGAN, SAMUEL SIMONS.—Head teacher, Wesleyan schls., Virgin Is., 1878; resig., 1903; clerical asst., govt. offices, Road Town, 1903; ag. govt. offr., West End, Nov., 1904, Feb., 1905, and Feb. to Apr., 1907; govt. offr., Road Town, 1906.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—B. 1866; clk. to commr., and to legis.bd., Turks and Caicos Isld., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and 1902; ag. regir. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. commr. (dist. mag.), regir. sup. ct. and regir.-gen., man. savings bank, and inspr. of pol. and prisons), G. Turk, May to July, 1896, Aug. to Nov., 1901, and May to Aug., 1906; J.P., 1901; ag. and., Aug. and Sept., 1901, and June to Aug., 1906; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag.acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier and regir. shipping); commr.'s office, Apl. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; ag. offl. mem., legis. bd., 1906; 1st cls. clk., rev. dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. commr., Salt Cay, May to July, 1909; ag. deputy stamp commr., Aug. to Nov., 1909; served in cust. dept., occupied territory, E. Africa, 1916-18; collr. of taxes and parochial treas., St. Anne, Jamaica, Jan., 1920.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1885; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancred student in com. law, Lin's Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comm., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Comm., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1896; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bdry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bdry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundary arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comttee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1906; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comttee. of comml. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comttee. of Imp. Inst. 1909-16; gov. and c. in c., Newfoundland, 1st Nov., 1917; assumed govt., 17th Dec., 1917; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imperial Post Office, 1st Jan., 1900; transf. to dept. of posts and telegraphs, Cape of Good Hope, 13th Nov., 1902; resign., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia telegraph serv., 1st Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Telegraph Co., 12th Apr., 1908; resign., 31st Jan., 1910; provincial postmaster, S. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; asst. supt. of posts and telegraphs, Fed.Malay States, 14th June, 1918; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army res. of offrs., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 91st Punjabis, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyassaland Prot., 18th June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz. since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921.

HARRIS, HON. ROBERT EDWARD, D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. High School, Annapolis Royal; pri. tuition; D.C.L., King's Coll., Univ., Windsor, N.S.; LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S.; called to the N.S. bar, 1882; K.C., 1889; practised at Yarmouth, 1882-92; Halifax, 1892-1915; justice, sup. ct., N.S., 1915; ch. just., sup. ct., N.S., 7th Feb., 1918; selected as third arbitr. to determine value of Canadian Northern Rly. stock, on its acquisition by the Dom. Govt.; mem. of the coun. of the N.S. Barrister's Soc., and for some years pres.; chancellor, diocese of N.S., 1906; dir., schl. for blind, Halifax; gov. of King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.

HARRISON, ALBERT SENDON, M.Sc. Manchester, 1908.—B. 1887; on staff of Manchester Univ., and asst. mast., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1908-10; senr. mast., Halesowen comml. schl., 1910-12; on staff Manchester Univ. educn. dept., and headmr., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1912-14; inspr. of schl., Ceylon, Apr., 1914; on active serv., 1918-19; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Ceylon, May, 1919, to Apr., 1920; ag. dir. of educn., Apr. to Oct., 1920; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920.

HARRISON, Cuthbert Woodville.—B. 1874; ed. at Sherborne Sch., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren Esq.; 3rd cl. class. mods., 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; ag. aast. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899; also ag. harb. mast., Klang, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, Apr., 1901; aast. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, continued also as ag. aast. dist. offr., Klang, and harb. mast., at Port Swettenham, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1903; ditto Tampin, Mar., 1904; also aast. dist. offr., Matang, Oct., 1904; ag. aast. sec. to res., Mar., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Aug., 1906; ag. aast. sec. to res., Dec., 1906; ag. regis., sup. ck., Nov., 1907; ag. aast. dist. offr., Larut, Feb., 1908; also ag. dist. offr., Larut, and regis. of titles, Perak North, Aug., 1908; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Mar., 1909; ag. aast. dist. offr., Larut and Kris, and regis. of titles, Perak North in addition, Feb., 1911; ag. sec. to res., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Larut Sep., 1914; colls. of land rev., Seremban, and regis. of titles, Negri Sembilan, May, 1915; temporarily ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, in addition, Aug., 1917; colls. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regis. of titles, Selangor, 8th Mar., 1918; sec. to res., Selangor, 24th July, 1919; sec. to res., Perak, Dec., 1921; editor of "Illustrated Guide to F.M.S."; author of "The Magic of Malaya," and "Railway Guide to F.M.S. and Siam"; 2nd lieut., Malayan Vol. Infantry.

HARRISON, James Herbert Hugh.—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College (Lond.), and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag. and dist. surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dist. surg., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. comsnr. and dist. surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892; aast. col. surg., Belize, 12th May, 1897; col. surg., 1st Sept., 1906.

HARRISON, John Burchmore, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1856; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1878; M.A., 1887; prof. of chemistry and agricul. science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of chem., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; director of dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1905; is F.L.C., F.G.S., F.C.S., and F.G.S. of America; author of numerous papers relating to the agric. and geology of the W. Indies and B. Guiana; on spec. serv. to U.S.A. in connection with questions relating to sugar analysis, recd. thanks of U.S. govt.; specially thanked on several occasions by S. of S. for services in connection with tropical agriculture, etc.

HARRISON, Norman, C.M.G., D.S.O., (1918), A.M.I.K.E.—Jnrn. clk., dept. of posts and telegraphs, Pietermaritzburg, 1st Feb., 1890; 4th cls. clk., 27th Feb., 1894; 3rd cls. clk., 1st Sept., 1894; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1898; 2nd aast. engrnr., 1st Mch., 1898; 1st aast. engrnr., 1st Nov., 1901; ag. chief engrnr., from 8th June to 13th Sept., 1903; transf. to Transvaal as aast. engrnr. of telegraphs, 1st Dec., 1903; engrnr., 1st Apr., 1908; acted as chief engrnr., 6th Feb. to 4th Aug., 1908; ag. chief engrnr., 1st Nov., 1908; chief engrnr., 1st July, 1909; ag. engrnr.-in-chief, posts and telegraphs, Union of S. Africa, 1910; engrnr.-in-chief, 1st April, 1912; dir. of signals, S. African Forces, 1914; aast. dir. of army signals, B.E.F., France, 1916.

HARRISON-JONES, Charles.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1909; aast. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; ag. aast. gov. agt., Hambantota, Apr., 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; ag. aast. gov. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1913; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; ag. aast. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1920.

HARRISONSON, Sydney Thirlwall, C.M.G. (1908), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Blackheath; appd. aast. act., P.W.D., G.C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ch. act., 2nd June, 1897; aast. act., W.A.F.F., 26th Feb., 1898; ch. act., 8th Nov., 1899; treas., N. Nig., 1st Jan., 1900; comptroller of customs, Barbados, 1908.
HART, Walter Edward.—B. 1847; librarian, public library and museum, Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1903.

HART-DAVIS, Charles Henry.—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; aast. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dist. comsnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comsnr. for the Western Pacific, 1910; on mly. serv., 1915-19; ch. aast. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. inspr. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. regis.-gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comsnr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., 27th Oct., 1920 to 3rd Feb., 1921, 17th to 23rd Mar. and 11th May to 3rd Oct., 1921.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engrnr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918.
HARVEY, David Harold.—Entd. Natal civ. serv., Apr., 1898; transf. to Swaziland adminstr. as clk., Mar., 1903; sub-inspr., Swaziland police, Apr., 1907; dep. aast. comsnr., Apr., 1911.

HARVEY, Hon. Horace, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll., Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1889; admitted to practice in N.W.T., 1893; regis. of land titles for South Alberta regt. dist., 1896; dep. atty.-gen. of N.W.T., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., N.W.T., June, 1904; puisne judge, sup. ct. of Alberta, 1907; ch. just. of Alberta, Oct., 1910; ch. just., trial divn., sup. ct., Alberta, 15th Sept., 1921.
HARVEY, Hon. John Musgrave.—B. 1865; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913.

HASKINS, Wilfred J., A.M.C.E.A.—Aast. supt. of wks., S. Stithla, Mar., 1905; dist. engrnr., F.M.S. rlyw., May, 1912; senr. dist. engrnr., grade B., F.M.S. rlyw., July, 1919; ag. engrnr., for ways and wks., Nov., 1920.

HASTINGS, A. C. G.—Served in 43rd Co. I.Y. in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1901; attached to vice-consulate, Fez, Dec., 1901, to Sept., 1905; ag. vice-consul, Nov., 1903, to May, 1904; aast. res., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1911.

HASZARD, Hon. Francis Longworth.—B. 1849; ed. at Prince of Wales's Coll., Prince Edward Is.; called to the bar, 1872; K.C., 1894; elected to legis., P.E.I., 1904 and 1908; mem. exec. coun., 1905; prem. and atty.-gen., P.E.I., 1908; judge, supreme et., and master of the rolls, P.E.I., 1911.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compete exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transf. to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff clk., first grade, July, 1913.
HATHORN, Hon. Kenneth Howard, K.C.—B. 1849; puisne judge of supreme ct. of S. Africa (Natal prov. division), May, 1910.

HAUGHTON, T. H.—B. 1881; served in S. African Constab., Mar., 1901, to Dec., 1907; held rank of lieut. and sub-inspr. (Queen's medal with five clasps); asst. res., Muri Prov., N. Nigeria, 9th May, 1908.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schls., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. elk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd ds. elk., 1906; 1st cls. elk., 1910; ch. elk., 1917; asst. dep. min., 1st Apr., 1920.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; pte., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 8th Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 7th Feb., 1915; B.E.F., 29th Mar., 1915; ment. in desp., 20th Dec., 1917; wounded, and discharged with rank of capt., 5th May, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; add. to land office, Penang, 21st Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, 14th Jan., 1921.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., M.V.O.—Sec. to premier of New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887)—B. 1838; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot catt. at Dum Dum in 1869; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volks. in 1868 at their formation; asst. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. commr. of Accra, Oct., 1875; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; asst. col. sec., G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; admstr. of the Gambia, 1885; adminstr. the govt. of the W. Africa Stlttns., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1886; served in the Yonnie expdn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOR. WAGTAFFER KT. RCH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar. In Tem., 1886; practised in the S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1889; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDON, LEONARD GUSCOTE, D.S.O.—M.B., C.M., D.P.H. (Univ. of Aberdeen); admstr. M.O., plague suppression, Mandvi, Bombay, 1897; pub. health serv., Victorian Govt., 1898; civ. surg., etc., S. Africa, 1900; pub. health serv., Natal govt., 1902; asst. M.O.H., Union of S. Africa, 1910; A.D.M.S., central force, S.W. African campaign, 1914; prin. med. offr., S.W. African Prot., 1915; asst. M.O.H., Union of S. Africa, 1917; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1919.
HAYES, HON. J. B., C.M.G.—Rep. the dist. of Bass, Tasmania, in H. of A. since 1913; min. of lands, works and agr., 1916-19; min. for works and agr. from 1919; is a mag. of the territory.

HAYNES, ALVIN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. financial asst., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1908; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag. for Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. financial asst., Krian, Sept., 1903; administered estate of late Datoh Panglima Kinta in Perak, 1904-1906; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. inspr. of schls., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; 1st cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigrts., Penang, Nov., 1906; asst. supt., ditto, Nov., 1906; examiner in Tamil, Perak and Penang; ag. supt. of Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Stlttns., Feb. to May, 1910; ag. asst. supt. of immigrts., Klang, Jan., 1911; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1914; mem. of board of examiners in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., Tampin, 1916; sec. to high commr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920.

HAZELTON, ERIC.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., Monmouthshire Brig., R.F.A., June, 1915; joined B.E.F., France, Nov., 1916; lieut., R.H. and R.F.A., June, 1916; served with E.E.F., Suez Canal, Sinai, Palestine, 1916-19; cadet, F.M.S., Johore, Dec., 1919; 2nd asst. adviser to govt. of Johore, Jan., 1921.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918). K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers' soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, Jan., 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., Aug., 1914-May, 1915; capt., R.A.S.C., May, 1915-Nov., 1920; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Oct., 1920; ag. asst. land offr., May-Dec., 1921, in addn.
HKAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. elk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HEARSEY, HERBERT HYDE YOUNG, O.B.E. (1918).—M.B., C.M. (Edin.), 1890; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1896; prin. med. offr., Apr., 1902; dir. of med. serv. (with temp. rank of lieut.-col.), Nyasaland Field Force, Mar., 1916.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1884; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e., 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resign. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem. internat. joint comm. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, ASHTON MARLER, M.I.C.E. (1898); M.I.M.E. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Manchester gram. schl.; served at Messrs. Sharp, Stewart & Co.'s loco. wks., Manchester, 1877-1882; asst. under loco. supt., L. & S. W. Ryw., 1882-1887; inspr. under Sir A. M. Rendel, K.C.I.E., 1887-1897; chief asst. engnr. to J. Carruthers, Esq., M.I.C.E., consulting engnr. to W. Aust. and N. Zealnd govt., 1897-1900; entd. Crown Agents' office as head of engineering and wks. dept., 1900; head of engineering inspnt. dept., and chief inspecting engnr. since 1904; mem. of engineering standards comte., 1905; assoc. mem. of mech. transport comte. of War Office, 1907; mem. of deptl. conference on leadless glaze, 1908.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," 1904; ag. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; pres. defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-charge of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut. defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. commr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEDOG-JONES, DAVID.—Ed. at Univ. Coll. of Wales, 1902-1906; dept. of Agric. Science (agric. scholar), and at Jesus Coll., Oxford (Nat. Science exhibitioner), 1907-1911; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; holder of National Diplomas in the science and practice of agriculture and dairying (N.D.A. and N.D.D.); associate of Univ. Coll. of Wales; F.R. Anthrop. Inst.; apptd. prin. of secondary schools, Grenada, 1911; deleg. to W. Indian Agric. Conf., 1912; editor of the "Caribbean," and author of "The Development of the Negro" and "Negro Folk Lore," and various agricultural and educational papers; a lieut. and adjutant of the Grenada volunteer force; acted as adjutant to West Indian Overseas Contingent, 1916.

HEIDENSTAM, EDGAR HENRY DE.—Ed. Rossall and private tutors; ag. priv. sec. to Sir W. F. Haynes Smith, high commr. of Cyprus, 1st Nov., 1901, to 13th July, 1902; clk., exec. sec.'s office, 14th July, 1902; inspr. of Cyprus military police, 1st Feb., 1905; a.d.c. and priv. sec. to Sir C. King-Harman, and clk. to exec. coun., 1st Mar., 1906; clk. to legis. coun. and asst. clk., chief sec.'s office, 1st Oct., 1907; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., 13th July to 15th Oct., 1908; ag. asst. sec. to govt., July to Oct., 1915, and from Aug., 1916, to May, 1917; asst. sec., 1st Feb., 1918; ag. priv. sec., Nov., 1918; asst. audr., 1st Sept., 1920.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Sch., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrenia div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as commr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek, lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high commr., Sir C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sea.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in comd. of col., Kyrenia div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in comd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907; inspr. of police, Cyprus, Apr., 1910.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. [foundation school] and St. John's Coll., Camb., Stewart of Rannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip. ii, 1); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; seconded for mil. ser., Jan., 1915; 2nd lieut., 8th (Service) Batt. Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., May, 1916; lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., Sept., 1916; ag. capt., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., June, 1918; demob., Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batu (Gajah, Nov., 1919; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1920.
HEMSTED, CHARLES SELWOOD.—3rd treasry. asst., E.A.P., 20th Apr., 1900; asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1903; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1907.

HEMSTED, RUPERT WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919).—2nd treasry. asst., E.A.P., 12th Sept., 1899; oh. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; asst. collr., E.A.P., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; offr.-in-charge, Massai reserve, 1912.

HENDERSON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1874; appld. clk. refugee, campa, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1901; clk. attached to audit dept., Apr., 1902; examnr. of accts, treasry, 20th Aug., 1902; acctnt., dept. of agric., 1st July, 1904; asst. acctnt., Union dept. of agric., Transval, June, 1911; acctnt. irrigtn. dept., Aug., 1912; ch. clk. and acctnt., irrigtn. dept., 1st Apr., 1915; ch. clk., irrigtn. dept., 1st Apr., 1921.
HENRNDSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.) L.R.C.P. (Lond.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prote., 18th Oct., 1904.

HENDRIE, LIEUT.-COL., HON., SIR JOHN STRATHBURN, K.C.M.G. (1915), O.V.O. (1907).—B. 1857; ed. at pub. schls., Hamilton, and Upper Canada Coll.; engrnr.; mayor of Hamilton, 1901-2; elec. Ontario legis., 1902, 1905, 1908, 1911 and 1914; min. without portfolio, 1906; mem. hydro-electric comn.; mem. battlefields comn.; mem. Strathcona trust; lieut.-col. Canadian Militia; comd. 2nd Bde. Can. Field Artill., 1904 to 1909; ex-pres. Can. Artill. Assoc.: ex-pres. Ontario Artill. Assoc.; lieut. gov. of Ontario, 1914-1919.

HENDRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educnl. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in deep, 1919; seconded as dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, 1st July, 1888; asst. to conservator, 1st Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, 1st June, 1893; Stutterheim, 1st Aug., 1898; selected to proceed to Royal India Engineering Coll., Cooper's Hill, for scientific course of forestry: obtained coll. diploma and specially commended by pres. of coll., 6th Aug., 1902; asst. conservator of forests, Eastern Conservancy, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. conservator, Western Conservancy, 1st July, 1907; apptd. principal lecturer in forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, 29th Oct., 1906; resig. lecturship, 29th Feb., 1908; ohmn. of bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, 18th May, 1907 to 31st Jan., 1909; asst. conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 1st Feb., 1909; conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 2nd grade, 1st Apr., 1912; Conservator of Forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's medal; tranf., Rhodesian forest serv., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Feb., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., S. Sttlmtn., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr. gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st May,

HERDMAN, GEORGE WALKER, M.A., B.Sc., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1869; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; ast. engr. waterworks, in S. Africa, 1896-1899; engr. waterworks, England, 1900-1903; exec. engr., irrigtn. dept., Transvaal, Sept., 1903; inspecting engr., P.W.D., Transvaal, Mar., 1907; inspecting engr., P.W.D., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; ast. dir. of irrigtn., Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1917.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A.—Cadet, S. Sttlmt., Nov., 1898; ast. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; ast. dist. officer, Penang, Nov., 1902; ast. mag., Penang, Jan., 1904; ast. 2nd mag., Penang, Mar., 1906; ast. dist. officer, Batu Gajah, Jan., 1907; ast. mag., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1908; ast. dist. officer, Larut, July, 1909; ast. dist. officer, Kinta, Feb., 1910; ast. dep. pub. prosecutor, Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1910; dist. officer, Raub, continued ast. dep. pub. prosecutor, May, 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; passed final exam. for the bar, May, 1913; ast. regis., sup. ct., F.M.S., June, 1913; ast. censor, Penang, June, 1915; ast. senr. dist. officer, Prov. of Wellesley, June, 1915; ast. 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1917; ast. dist. officer, Butterworth in addition, Nov., 1917; ast. pres., munic. commrs., Penang, May, 1919; offr., coll., land rev., Penang, May, 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; ast. regis. of impts. and expts., Singapore, June, 1921.
HERMION-HODGE, H. B.—B. 1885; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hon. in mods. and hist.; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 26th Dec., 1908.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; ast. master, June, 1902; commr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn., 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912.

HERRIES, HON. SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1920), B.A., Camb., F.G.S.—B. 1858; entd. New Zealand parl., 1896; native min., 1912-21; min. of rlyws., 1912-21; min. of cust. and marine, 1919-21; min. of lab., 1920-21.

HERSHENSOHN, J. M. N. A.—1st cls., ast. govt. schls., Natal, 1899; clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1900; sworn translator, Dutch; senr. clk., 1906; civ. ser. exam., 1902; Dutch bd. of exams., 1903; one of the secretaries at S. Africa National Convention, 1908-1909; chief clk., admistr. dept., Natal Prov., 1910; ast. prov. sec., Aug.-Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; prov. sec., 1st Feb., 1918.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; ast. gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912.

HEWINS, WILLIAM ALBERT SAMUEL, M.P.—B. 1863; ed. at Wolverhampton Grammar schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; M.A.; dir. of London schl. of economics, 1895-1903; teacher of modern economic history, Univ. of London, 1902-3; Tooke professor of economic science and statistics, King's Coll., 1897-1903; mem. of senate of Univ. of London, 1900-1903; late sec. of Tariff

HILL, ARTHUR HEYLIGER.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treas., Br. Guiana, 1883; 3rd grade commr. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec. excise bd. and supervising commr., treas., in rev. branch, 1905; passed exam. in Hindi, 1906; 1st cls. clk., imgrn. dept., 1907; seconded as ast. emigrn. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, 14th Apr. to 7th Aug., 1914, and from 20th Nov., 1915 to 6th Nov., 1916.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; 7th clk., adminstr.-gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1896; 5th cls. clk., police dept., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr. of police, Dec., 1900; dist. inspr. of police, Apr., 1903; ag. adjut. and musketry instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of police, Nov., 1909 to Apr., 1910, and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qtrmr. from Sept., 1911; pay and qtrmr., 1st Jan., 1912.
HILLIER, GEORGE FRANCIS.—Ed. Highbury New Park Coll., Lond.; admitted to the bar of supreme ct., W. Aust., 1888; apptd. clk. of legis. coun. and clk. of parlt., June, 1901.

HILLYER, HERBERT KEYS, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; short'h'd writer and ast. clk., col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, Jan., 1908; attd. durbars of native chiefs, Colombo, July, 1908; Kandy, May, 1909; Colombo, May and June, 1909; Kandy, July, 1910; Jaffna, Aug., 1910; Colombo, Aug., 1910; sec. to Colombo Lake development scheme coman., 1909; jt. sec. to agric. banks comittee., 1909; sec. to opium coman., 1909; sec. to legis. coun. constitution coman., 1910; sec. to tuberculous diseases coman., 1910; secret. ast., July, 1910; sec. to ankylostomiasis comittee., 1910; hon. sec. to King Edward VII. memorial fund comittee., 1910; sec. to rlyw. traffic coman., 1911; sec. to educatin. comittee., 1911-12; sec. to Colombo Port Coman., July, 1913; sec. Prince of Wales war fund, Aug., 1914; hon. sec. Brit. Red Cross fund, Ceylon, 1915; hon. sec. wounded French Soldiers fund, Ceylon, 1916; sec. Colombo rivercraft comittee., Oct., 1916; hon. sec., French Red Cross fund, 1917; on war service, 1918-1919; joint sec. pesco celebrations comittee., July, 1919.

HINSON, CAPT. W.—Ed. St. Lawrence Coll. and Lycee Descartes, Tours; solr., 1904; 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt.; qualified as signalling instr., Aldershot, 1908; ast. dist. commr., G. Coast, 1911; dist. commr., 1916.

HISLOP FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; capt. territorial army res. (3rd Royal Scots); on war serv., 1915-18, Gallipoli, Egypt, France; Hythe instr. machine gunnery, 1918; cadet, S. Stiltns, 1919; attached, treas., Singapore, Deo., 1919; ag. ast. supt., govt. monopolies, June, 1920.

HISLOP, JAMES, M.V.O. (1920), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1870; entd. New Zealand civ. serv., 1885, New Zealand govt. rlyws., 1890; ast. priv. sec. to min. for rlyws. and postmstr. gen., 1900; priv. sec. to prime min., 1907; under sec. for internal affairs and clk. of wrks, 1912.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; ast. engr., water wks., Trinidad, 24th Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, 24th Sept., 1906.

HOAR, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1864; apptd. under prison commrs., England, after civ. ser. exam., 1892; gaoler, St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1896. to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. keeper of prisons, Leeward Is., May to Nov., 1897; ch. warder, Belize, Br. Head., Jan., 1899; ag. keeper of prisons, Br. Head., on numerous occasions, 1900-1912; keeper of prisons, 1st Oct., 1913.

HOBBES, GEORGE.—M. Inst. C.E.; ed. at Christ Coll., Finchley; Telford Premium, Inst. C.E., 1906-9; asst. engr., Castries Harbour, St. Lucia, 1895; res. engr., ditto, 1899; res. engr., St. John's harbour, Antigua, 1891-1896; asst. engr. to consulting engineers, 1896-1902; res. engr., Famagusta harbour, Cyprus, 1902-1906; res. engr., Sierra Leone harbour wks., 1906-1909; res. engr., Gold Coast harbours, 1909, including Sierra Leone after 1912.

HODDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo Kachcheri, April, 1913; Trincomalee Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. off. asst. to the gov't. agt., Ratnapura, May, 1914; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs, May, 1919.

HOBLEY, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1904).—Geologist to Impl. Br. E. Africa Co., Mar., 1890; 1st cls. asst. Uganda Prot., Aug., 1894; asst. dep. comntr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902; ag. comntr., 27th June to 1st Aug., 1904; assoo. mem. instit. of civ. engnr., Uganda mutiny medal; Nandi medal, 1900; M.L.C., E. Africa Prot., 1914.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1865; appkd., after compet. exam., elk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the comntr. of H.M.'s wrks. and publ. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1886; transf'd. to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. regtr., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff elk., first grade, 1911.
HOCART, ARTHUR MAURICE.—Archaeological comntr., Ceylon, May, 1919.

HODGE, LEONARD PRECIVAL, F.S.I., Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Taunton, and King's Coll., Lond.; honors construc., 1886; 2nd el. supt., P.W., Jamaica, 1891; 1st el. ditto, and attached to head off., 1892; dir. of roads, G. Coast, 1894, but did not take up apptnt.; col. engrnr. and surv.-gen., Dominica, 1898; draughtsman, P.W.D., B. Guiana, 20th Mar., 1901; asst. engrnr. and draughtsman, Apr., 1906.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Asst. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

HODGES, AERTHE HARRIS.—B. 1884; ednl. Impl. civ. ser., and apptd. to comptlr. and asst. gen.'s office, G.P.O., London, 1900; asst. asst.'s office, G.P.O. O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; asst. postmr.-gen. and junr. asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1910; treas. asst., 3rd grade, 1914; 2nd grade, 1918; 1st grade, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1920.

HODGINS, HON. FRANK EGERTON.—B. 1854; educ. Upper Canada Coll. and Trin. Univ., B.C.I.; called to the bar, 1879; K.C., 1902; prominent legal practitioner in Toronto for several years; pres., Ontario bar assoc., 1908-9; legal agt. for Dominion govt. at Toronto, 1890-1896; counsel for Ontario govt. on license investigation, 1907, and on other occasions; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1912; also local udge in Admiralty, Exchequer Ct.

HODGSON, SIR FREDERIC MITCHELL, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1898). Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1903).—B. 1851; entered sav. bank dept. of Impl. P.O. and apptd., after compet. exam., Feb., 1869; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the state, 1868 to 1870, and apptd. elk. in that office, Aug., 1870; apptd., after further exam., to the 3rd class (grade I) of the sec.'s office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was sen. capt. of the 24th Middlesex (post office), R.V.; postmtr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec. G. Coast, Sept., 1888; adminstr. the govt., June, 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893 to Mar., 1894; Apr. to Oct., 1896; and Dec., 1897, to March, 1898; major comdng. the G. Coast R.V., which he raised in 1892, 1892-1900; major comdng. Barbados volr. force, which he raised in 1901, 1901-1904; gov., G. Coast, March, 1898; gov. Barbados, Oct., 1900; gov. B. Guiana, 16th July, 1904; ret., 1911; mem., W. African lands comtees., 1912.
HODGSON, F. V.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914.
HODGSON, GERALD.—B. 1858; ed. Lancing Coll.; entd. crown agents' office, 13th Apr., 1878; head of correspondence dept., 20th Oct., 1892; supt., stock transfer off., 22nd Nov., 1905.

HODGSON, PAUL M.—B. 1886; solr., attorney and proctor of the supreme ct. of England, May 1905; ditto of the supreme court of Hong Kong, April, 1907; asst. crown solr., Sept., 1911; ag. crown solr., 18th March to 6th Nov., 1913, and from 28th Mar., 1914 to 16th May, 1916; proper offr. of the crown in prize, sup. ct. of Hong Kong, 5th Aug., 1914.

HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, C.M.G. (1922).—B. 1881; ed. Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war; mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. asst. comntr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. comntr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zitadotw, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; attchd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919; capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard.

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan. 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; asst. settlmt. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Order., June, 1915; on mil. duty, 1917; addtnl. asst. col. sec., Apl., 1919; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919.

HOFMETR, ANDRIES BRINK.—Clk., own lands off., Jan., 1889; mag.'s off., Paarl, 1893; Piquetherg, 1894; Hanover, 1896; chf. elk. to chf. of pol., Cape Town, 1896; inspr. and paymaster Cape pol., dist. II., 1896; partly, elk. and pvtc. sec. to colonial sec., 1897; prin. elk., C.O., 1898; asst. mag., Vryburg, 1898; spl. duty, relief supplies, 1899-1900; ag. civ. comntr. and res. mag., Vryburg, Apl., 1901, to Sept., 1902; civ. comntr. and res. mag., Murraysburg, Oct., 1902; spl. mission to N.W. dist. to inquire into distress and organise relief wks., 1903-04; divl. inspr., A.G.'s dept., May, 1904; organiser and in charge of immigr. and labour dept., 1904-05; civ. commr. and res. mag., Humansdorp, Oct., 1908; supt. porter-reformatory and Tokai convict sta., Apr., 1910; mem. pub. serv. resign. comm., 1910-12; mem. perm. pub. serv. comm., Union, Aug., 1912.

HOFMEYR, G. M., B.A., Cape.—B. 1877; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher for number of years; attorney, notary-public and conveyancer, Transvaal; regiar. and treas., Victoria Coll., 1908-1910; under-sec. for educn., Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. for educn., Jan., 1920.

HOFMEYR, GYSBERT REITZ, C.M.G. (1914), J.P. (civ. serv. law, 1893)—B. 1871; entered Cape civ. serv. in 1890; occupied several public positions including those of ag. civ. commr. and mag. at various stations up to 1897, when he became priv. sec. to col. sec. of Cape Colony (Dr. Te Water); clk. asst., Cape House of Assembly, 1897; clk. of first House of Assembly, Transvaal, 1907; Transvaal sec. to S. Africa National Convention, 1908-9 (Union medal) sec. to S. African Delimitation Comm., 1910; clk. of House of Assembly, Union of S. Africa, 1910; accompanied General Beyers, commandant general of Union citizen forces on mission to attend military manoeuvres and inspect military institutions in England, Switzerland, France and Germany, 1912; published for some years "Het Zuid Afrikaanse Jaarboek"; author of first sketch of practical plan for Union of South Africa, 1907; and "An Undivided White South Africa," 1916; edited for publication "Minutes of the South African National Convention," 1911; and wrote a critical memorandum on Sir E. H. Walton's "Inner History of the National Convention of South Africa, 1912; admstr., S. W. Africa Prot., 1920; admstr., S. W. Africa, civ. admstr., 1921.

HOGG, ALAN F.—B. 1888; ed. at Winchester and King's Coll., Camb.; 2nd cls. hist. trip., 1890; 2nd cls. law trip., 1891; prizeman of the coun. of legal educ. in common law and equity, 1891; Whewell scholar of international law, 1893; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1892; stip. mag., Entebbe, admstr. gen., prin. regiar. of documents and regiar. of companies, Uganda Prot., 6th Nov., 1908; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Jan., 1915; judge advocate gen., June, 1915.

HOHENKIRCH, LUDOVIC SMITH.—B. 1874; ed. in B. Guiana; ag. 6th cls. clk., govt. land dept., B. Guiana, 1890; ag. surv., 1896; 5th cls. clk., 1897; 3rd grade surv., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., 1904; ag. 1st cls. offr., 1901 and 1905; J.P. for the col., 1906; ag. warden, No. 3 mining dist., 1906, 1907 and 1909; 1st cls. offr., 21st Sept., 1908; ag. forestry offr., 1914-15; performed duties of prin. clk. in head off., 1916.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Aast. supt. of wks., S. Shilts, June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engr., Malacca, Dec., 1909; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, Jan., 1910; ag. exec. engr., Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1910; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, June, 1913; exec. engr., Malacca, Mar., 1915; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engr., Mar., 1920.

HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B. (1904).—B. 1856; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comm., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comm. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Lord Rothschild's comm. on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comm., 1900-1901; to royal comm. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comm. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, 1905-1908; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.
HOLLAND, J.—B. 1888; apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904; 1st cl. messenger, 30th Jan., 1912.
HOLLAND, J. S.—B. 1881; 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 29th June, 1909; 2nd cl. messenger, 3rd July, 1915; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1919.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. of S. Africa, 1906; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 1st Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Mishahe, 12th Oct., 1917.
HOLLIMAN, J. W., I.S.O. (1912).—Served in Impl. treas., entd. civ. ser., New South Wales, Mar., 1884; served as sec. to pub. ser. board; under-sec. for finance and trade, Jan., 1907.

HOLLINGSWORTH, A. H.—A.M. Inst. C.R. ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engr., Croydon; ast. engr., to borough and water engr., Croydon; ast. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engr., in charge of water and drain. wks., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engr., in charge of water and drain. wks., 1st Jan., 1902; J.P., 1907; semr. exec. engr., 22nd Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd astt. D.P.W. on several occasions; 2nd astt. D.P.W., 14th June, 1919; 1st astt., D.P.W., 17th Apr., 1920, and in addition D.P.W., 15th June to 21st Sept., 1920.

HOLLIS, ALFRED CLAUD, C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; ast. collr., R.A.P., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstr., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1; and Nandi expdn., 1906-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., 13th June, 1907; on special service to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 26th Jan., 1913; ag. govt., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915, and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admstr., German East Africa (Tanganyika Territory), 4th Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; chief sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; author of works on the Masai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLM, ALEXANDER.—M.R.A.S.K., F.H.A.S., S.E.A.C.; gen. manager, govt. experimental farm, Potchefstroom, Transvaal, 1908; gen. manager and principal, schl. of agric., Potchefstroom, 1909; under sec. for agric. (educat.), Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 1918.
HOLMES, HAROLD KENNARD.—Ed. All Saints, Bloxham; admitted solr., sup. ct., Hong Kong, Feb., 1900; ast. land offr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1919; J.P., Oct., 1919.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Arty., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921.

HOLMWOOD, A.D.—Supt., roads and local wks., Transvaal provl. admn.; previous serv. since 1891, engnrng. depts. Tasmanian govt. rly.; Impl. mil. rlys., Transvaal, Central S. African rly., and pub. wks. dept., Transvaal.

HOLT, THOMAS HERBERT.—B. 1861; ed. at Fulneck Sch., Yorks; entd. office of Messrs. J. and B. Freeland, Crown Agents' shipping agents, 1876; senr. partner, 1909; head of Crown Agents' shipping office, 1912.

HONEY, DR SIMONS MONTAGU, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919).—Served in B.S.A. Co.'s expdt. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admtn., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 5th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting, offr., 20th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admtn., 22nd Mar., 1907; res. comsmr., Swaziland, 1st Jan., 1917.
HOOD, HON. JOSEPH HENRY.—B. 1846; M.A.; puisne judge, Victoria, since 1890.

HOOD, THO., C.M.G. (1917).—Ed. City of London schl., Univ. Coll., and St. Bartholomew's hospital, London; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., London; ast. col. surg., S. Leone, 1897; served on staff during native rising, 1898 (W. African medal and clasp, 1898); comsmr. of Bandajuma dist., 1899; senior med. offr., 1902; ag. prin. med. offr., Oct. 1903, to Mar., 1904; J.P. for the colony; acted as P.M.O. and M.O.H. on three occasions during 1903 to 1907 for a total period of twenty months; transf. as sen. med. offr., Gambia, Mar., 1907; chmn., bd. of health, 1907; dep. P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1911; P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 12th June, 1913.

HOODLEES, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; ast. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., law schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCELOT.—B. 1876; ed. at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Sch. of Prop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in desps., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. apt., Penang vol.; ag. col. surg. res., Penang, 1905; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. ad supt. of prisons in Kedah since that date; adviser to govt. of Kedah, Supt., 1906, to pr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of indiam immigrants, Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kelah, in litigation, Nov., 1917; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Stltmtes, Jan., 1921.

HOPE, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1851; med. offr., convict estabmt., Fremantle; med. supt., invalid pdt.; med. offr., penal settlement, attntest Is.; and health offr., Port of Fremantle, Australia, July, 1884; dist. med. offr. and quarantine offr., Fremantle, 1895; major, Aust. d artillery.

HOPE, JOHN OWEN WEBLEY, C.M.G. (1918).—treasy. ast., E. Africa Prot., 17th May, 1899; ast. collr., 1st Apr., 1900; collr., 1st Apr., 1903; offr.-in-charge, Northern frontier dist., 1909; ag. prov. commr., Kenya, 1915; political offr., German East Africa, 1915.
HOPKINS, J. G.—Ast. dist. comsmr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINS, WALTER.—Cape postal serv., 1889; relieving offr., 1898; ag. inspr., 1901; clk., sec's. office, 1902; ag. prin. clk., 1903; ag. survr. and dist. engr., Northern dist., 1903; survr. and dist. engr., 1905; ditto, Eastern dist., 1908; ditto, Natal, 1910; survr. and divisional comm. supt., Natal (Union), 1912; contrlr. of stores, 1914; ag. ch. clk., June, 1919; ch. clk., Dec., 1919; ag. ast. under sec., Aug., 1920; ast. under-sec., Aug., 1921.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILIUS, D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical ast., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in desps., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling comsmr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."
HOPPER, HAROLD LANCELOT, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attld. Ratnapura Kach., Sept., 1921.

HORE, ERNEST HENRY.—Entered Impl. postal ser., July, 1871; transf. to Cyprus for special service, Oct., 1878; entered col. ser., Aug., 1882; as sub-postmr., Limasol; acted several times as ch. clk.; ch. clk., Feb., 1890; acted several times as Isld. postmr.; apptd. Isld. postmr., Apr., 1893.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN.—M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; served as civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904; certif. of London schl. of trop. med. (with distinction); awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; recl. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910; personal ast. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 24th July, 1912.

HORNYBY-PORTER, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. comsmr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1901; senior ast. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1902-1903; ag. col. sec., 1904; holds dormant comsm. to sit as pol. mag. and sat as such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1903; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1904, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. comsm., 1901; mem. of rly. comsm., 1901; pres. of cust. comsm., 1904.
HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER.—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schl.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. comsmr., 1st Apr., 1909.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

HORNE, HENRY HASTINGS, O.B.E., Mily. (1918).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; Brit. vice-consul, Mexico, 1900-01; S. Africa, field ntel. force, S. African medal; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 3rd Apr., 1903; dist. comsmr., 1st Apr., 1907; slavery
COMMISSIONER, Lamu, 1908-9; polit. offr. to G.E.A., exped. force, 1915-18 (mentd. in despatches); lent to Empire Cotton Comtece. for sp. rept., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-21.

HORSBURGH, B., C.M.G. (1921)—B. 1868; M.A. Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1889; attached to the Jaffna Kachcheri; ag. office ast., Jaffna, 1891; ag. off. ast., Calle, 1893; ag. ast. govt. agent, Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, 1896; ast. govt. agent, Mannar, 1896; ast. govt. agent, Hambantota, 1898; ast. govt. agent, Kalutara, 1904; off. of cl. II, 11th Mar., 1905; ag. chmn. mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, 1906; govt. agent, N. Cent. Prov., 1908; associated with Mr. Thurler, of the Madras Presidency salt and abkari dept. in introducing an excise system into Ceylon, Sept., 1909; seconded for service as excise commr., Sept., 1909; offr. of Cls. I., grade 2, and to remain seconded, Nov., 1910; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1910; govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1915; major (reserve) in Ceylon Arty. Volra.; ag. controller of rev., Apr.-Oct., 1919; food controller, May, 1919; ag. col. sec., Mar.-Sept., 1920; contr. of rev., Apr., 1920; has charge of a camp of Boer prisoners of war at Hambantota, and served on spec. duty at several pearl fisheries since 1891.

HOWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, Kt. Bath. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1896; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec., 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comm. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newf. centre of St. John Ambulance Assoctn.; chrmn. of comte. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newf. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comm. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1918.
HOSE, CHARLES, D.Sc.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; res. 2nd cl., 4th div., Jan., 1891; res. 3rd Div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

HOSE, EDWARD SHAW.—B. 1871; junr. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1891; ag. financial ast., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1891; ast. dist. mag., ditto, Nov., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Batang Padang, Oct., 1893; ast. sec. to govt., Perak, Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, May, 1903; offr. of cls. III, S. Settlements, Mar., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros.; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. commr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1904; chmn., sanitary board, Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1904; ag. commr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Sept., 1912; ag. and-gen., 1912; ag. controller of labour, May, 1913; ag. dir. of agric., Oct., 1915; offr. of cls. II, F.M.S., Mar., 1916; dist. offr., Lower Perak; ag. dir. food production, F.M.S., 8th Feb., 1919; dir. food production, S. Stltms., 17th July, 1919, in addition; contr. of lab., F.M.S. and S. Stltms. (offr. cls. Ia.), but continues to act as dir. of food production, Sept., 1919; dep. food contr., Malacca, in addition, Jan., 1920; food contr., Mar., 1920; ag. Br. rep., Selangor, Sept., 1920.
HOSKING, E.B.—Ast. dist. commr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913.
HOSKING, HON. JOHN HENRY.—B. 1854; barrister and solicitor; K.C., 1908; judge of sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914.
ROTHAM, COMMODORE ALAN GEOFFREY, C.M.G. (1919), R.N., H.M.S. "CHATHAM."—Commodore commanding N.Z. statn., and naval adviser to N.Z. govt.
HOUGHTON, H. E.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high commr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920.
HOUSTON, WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1846; formerly under-sec. for lands, N.S. Wales; commr. of lands, appeal ct. of N.S. Wales, and deputy admtr. of Norfolk Island, 1899.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical ast., Basutoland, 5th Oct., 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regis., 1st July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1919.
HOWARD, CHARLES GORDON, B.A.—B. 1891; ed., Mill Hill Schl., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 18th Aug., 1914; seconded, Tanganyika admin., 8th Oct., 1917.

HOWARD, HON. ERATUS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Troquois High Schl., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1896; gold medallist in maths. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist) 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 26th Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof., civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. ast. acctnt., army acctn. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. ast. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. ast. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supermy. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation commr. treasy., as ast. contr. of deptl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921.

HOWARD, JOHN CURRIE.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhbr.); called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1915; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1921.

HOWARD, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1872; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; Guy's Hosp., 1894-1898; B.A. Oxon. (1st cls. Physiology), 1894; M.A., M.B., B.Ch. Oxon., 1897; prize in pathology and treasurer's gold medal in clinical surgery at Guy's Hosp., 1897; F.S.T.M.H.; M.D. Oxon., 1906; ast. house surg. and house surg., Guy's Hosp., 1897-8; med. offr., Univ. Mission to Central Africa, Nyasaland, 1909; Cragg's investigator for London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1906; med. offr., Univ. Mission to Central Africa, Zanzibar, 1910-1818; govt. med. offr., Pemba, Oct., 1918.

HOWARTH, SAMUEL EDWARD JAMES.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War; Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; asst. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1906.
HOWATSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1861; dist. med. offr., Trinidad, 15th May, 1905.

HOWELL, ALLENNE GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk. correspndg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. commr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1905, and 1907.

HOWELL, E. H.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917.
HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919.

HOWELL, JOHN BRUCE.—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treas., Barbados, July, 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; ag. ch. clk., record branch, C.S.O., Sept., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., provost-marshal's office, Dec., 1897, to Apr., 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comct., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, May to October, 1900; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados, 1st Apr. to 6th July, 1902, and Jan. to Oct., 1904; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., June to Dec., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov., June to Sept., 1906, and from June to Oct., 1907; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Mar. to July, 1910, and to ag. gov., July, 1910 to Feb., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Mar. to July, 1911; ag. inspr. of pol., Sept., 1915 to Nov., 1918; J.P., 1918; ch. clk., col. secy.'s off., Aug., 1918.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., 22nd May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, 26th Mar., 1917; dist. mag., list judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, 1st Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and off. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, 18th July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb., 1921.

HOY, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.T. BACH (1916); C.B. (1918).—B. 1868; entd. serv. of Cape Govt. rly., 1889; representative of C.G.R. in Johannesburg, 1896; ag. ast. traffic manager, Bulawayo, 1897-8; Kimberley, 1898; ast. traffic manager, Port Elizabeth, 1899; traffic manager, Bloemfontein, Johannesburg, 1900-02; chief traffic manager, C.S.A.R., 1902; represented C.S.A.R. at rly. confce. at Washington; ag. gen. manager in 1905 and 1907; ast. gen. manager, 1909; gen. manager of rlys. and harbours, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORST.—Clk., control and audit office, Cape, 1896; examnr., 1896; ast. acctnt., 1903; ast. acctnt., contr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1916.

HUBER, JOSEPH.—B. 1866; entd. Malta civ. serv., 1892; in charge of govt. savings bank, 1892; acctnt., rec. gen. office, and in charge of savings bank, 1893; dep. rec. gen. and dir. of contracts, 1904; ast. treas., 1913; treas. and dir. of contracts, 16th Aug., 1916; also man. of savings bank since 1898; off. mem. exec. coun. and of coun. of govt., 16th Aug., 1916.
HUDSON, HAROLD.—Book-keeper, treas. dept., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1911; cashier, treas. dept., Aug., 1911; ast. treas., Mar., 1914; ag. deputy treas., June to Dec., 1919 and Dec., 1921 to Feb., 1922.
HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Apr., 1920.

HUGGARD, WALTER CLARENCE, K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honour-man and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. pulane judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. pulane judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan., 1922.
HUGGINS, CHARLES PELHAM.—B. 1860; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1882; stip. mag., Trinidad, 3rd June, 1912.

HUGGINS, HENRY DANIEL.—B. 1866; barrister-at-law, Mid. Temp.; entd. Trinidad service, 1873; clk. of the peace, 1876-80; mag. and warden, Mayaro, 1887; ditto, Cedros, 1894; stip. mag., county of Caroni, 1906.

HUGGINS, HORATIO CRICHTON, M. Soc. E.—1880; ed North London Collegiate Schl. and Polytechnic Schol. of Engng. (London); served three years articles under the Hon. W. Wrightson, M.I.C.E., C.M.G.; served in pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1st June, 1900 to 4th June, 1909; 1st grade ast. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 5th June, 1909; exec. engr., 2nd grade, 4th Oct., 1918; author of "Bridge Roads in the West Indies" (Trans. Soc. Engrs., 1907); "Reinforced Concrete in Nigeria" (Trans. Concrete Inst., 1913).

HUGGINS, JOHN, M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schol. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July 1st, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., 27th Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, 23rd May, 1920; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 7th July, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, 7th Oct., 1920; passed exam. in law, Mar., 1921; 4th ast. sec. to govt., 9th Mar., 1921; passed final exam., Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Kajang, 24th Aug., 1921.
HUGHES, D.B.B., M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—1st priseman, senior surgery; medallist, anatomy and pathology; ag. surg. in charge, col. hospt., Grenada, 1900; dist. med. offr., St. Vincent, Dec., 1900.

HUGHES, GEORGE ROBERT.—Served in lands and surveys dept., W. Australia; 1897-1903; ast. sec. for lands, Transvaal, 14th July, 1903; under-sec. for lands, July, 1904; sec. for lands, July, 1906; mem. land bd., investment bd., townships bd.; sec. for lands and irrigation, 1908; ag. sec. for lands, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; sec. for lands, 1st Apr., 1912; is a J.P.

HUGHES, LESLIE DONALD CHARLETON, M.C.—B. 1890; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; attached to chief censor's office, Apr., 1915; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, March, 1916; on military duty, 1917-19; asst. dir. of food prodn., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Nov., 1921.

HUGHES, BRIG.-GEN. ROBERT HERBERT WILFRED, C.B. (1921), C.S.I. (1918), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1916), Officer Legion of Honour (1917)—B. 1872; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1886-87; merchant service, 1887-1898; Ed. of T. master mariner's certif.; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1896; lieut., 1904; comdr., 1914; capt., 1919; served in S. African war, in S.A. Light Horse, Bethune's M.I., and Royal Navy, from 12th Dec. 1899 to 1st May, 1902; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 27th March, 1903; qualified in surveying at R.G.S. in 1906; elected a younger Brother of Trinity House, 1909; senr. marine offr., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. marine supt. and harbr.-mast., Lagos, 1st July to 10th Dec., 1906; commanded Nigerian govt. yacht "Ivy" as armed auxiliary under the R.N. in the Cameroons Expd., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1916; Trinity House pilot in N. Sea, Apr.-June, 1916; seconded to Army as Major, R.E., 19th June, 1916; lieut.-col., Sept., 1916; brig.-gen., May, 1917; dir. Inland Water Transport, Mespot. till Apr. 1921; demob., 13th Apr. 1921; dep. dir. of marine, Nigeria, Aug., 1921.

HUGHES, RT. HON. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to Ist H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., 27th Oct., 1915; ditto, 14th Nov., 1916; ditto, 17th Feb., 1917; ditto, 10th Jan., 1918.

HULL, HON. H. C.—B. 1860; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1879; left the civ. ser. and practised at the bar for many years; served in S. African war; treas., Transvaal; min. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912.

HUMPHREYS, JOHN LISSETTER.—B. 1881; ed. at Bromsgrove Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (senr. class. scholar, 1900); 1st cls. class, moda., 1902; B.A., 1904; cadet, S. Stltms., Oct., 1905; dep. collr. of land rev., and registr. of deeds, Singapore, May, 1906; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Malacca, Nov., 1906; passed final exam. in Malay, passed cadet, July, 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1910; seconded for serv. in Johore as collr. and mag., Batu Pahat, Feb., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1912; ag. dep. regiar., supr. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1913; also asst. supt. of Indian immigrts.; seconded for serv. in Johore as ast. advcr., Muar, Feb., 1915; ag. Brit. agt., Trengganu, Nov., 1915; British Adviser, Trengganu, May, 1919.

HUMPHRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treasy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit elk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit elk., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treasy., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit elk. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, ATLEE ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1864; ed. at Balmain pub. schl. and Sydney; Gram. schl.; entd. lands dept., New S. Wales, 1879; resigned in 1887 to study for the bar; admitted to the bar, 1892, and practised until 1900; priv. sec. to first prime min. of C. of A., Jan., 1901; sec. and permanent head of dept. of external affairs, C. of A., since 1901; attended Imp. Conf., 1907 and 1911; now arbitr., federal pub. service.

HUNT, EDMUND LANGLEY, C.M.G. (1902); L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Ireland.—B. 1868; cert. Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med.; civ. surg. with S. African field force, 1889-1902; med. offr. on personal staff of comdr.-in-chief (twice ment. in desps., 2 medals, 6 clasps); employed in Anglo-German bdry. comm. to Lake Chad; recd. thanks of German govt. and the Royal Order of the Crown of Prussia (3rd class); apptd. to W.A.M.S., Feb., 1905; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Accra, 1908; inspecting med. offr., Ceylon, Mar., 1912; asst. prin. civ. med. offr., Ceylon, Aug., 1915; suptg. med. offr., Jamaica, 1919.
HUNT, HENRY AMBROSE.—B. 1866; F.R.Met. Soc.; meteorologist, C. of A., 1906.

HUNTER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR ARCHIBALD, K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1896).—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Royal Lancaster, 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1889; col., 1894; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Egypt, and twice wounded; gov. of Dongola Prov. and commdt. Frontier Field Force, 1886-99; gov. of Omdurman, 1899; Egyptian medal and other orders; in command of 1st cls. dist. in India; lieut.-gen. on staff, commanded 10th div. S. Africa, 1900-01 (twice ment. in desps.); commanded in Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corps, India, 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, Indian Army, 1907-9; gov. and comdr.-in-chi f., Gibraltar, 1910-1913.
HUNTER, SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1921).—B. 1860; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1896-99, and sino 1911.
HUNTER, HON. GORDON.—B. 1863; ed. Brantford Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1888; removed to B. Columbia 1891; ch. just., sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1902.

HUNTER, JAMES BLAKE, B.A. (Univ. o Toronto).—B. 1876; clk., priv. coun. off., Canada 1899; transf. to P.O. dept., 1900; marine fisheries, 1901; pub. wks., 1902; ch. clk., 1907; deputy min. of pub. wks., 1908.
HUNTER, JOHN.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigne to the C.O., Apr., 1899; clk. to patronage comteee., Nov., 1907.

HUNTER, JOHN ADAMS.—B. 1890; ed. Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne and S John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (hist. trip cls. 2, I.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; ag. supe visor of cust., Port Dickson, Dec., 1915 to Aug. 1917; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Swettenham Aug.-Dec., 1917; in India studying Tamil, Jan. Sept., 1918; offr., cl. V, 26th Sept., 1918; ass. dist. offr., Klang, 5th Oct.-28th Dec., 1918; att. to chief sec.'s office, Jan.-Mar., 1919; seconded for serv. in food control dept., 26th Mar., 1919; ag. 3rd astt. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., 7th Oct., 1919; 4th astt. sec. to Govt., 3rd Oct., 1921.
HUNTER, K. L.—Astt. dist. comsrr., Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

HUNTER, LOUIS LUCIEN.—B. 1889; cad. local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; attach to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; addtl. pmag., Colombo, in addition to his own duti... July, 1915; addtl. comanr. of requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1915; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, in addition to his own duties, Jan., 1916; addtl. comanr. of requests and pol. mag., Panadure, Sept., 1916; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chilaw, Sept., 1919.

HUNTER, REGINALD JOHN.—Ck., audit office, Transvaal, 18th May, 1901; priv. ck., 1st June, 1901; ag. asst. auditor-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1905, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; ch. ck., control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, 1st Jan., 1912; dep. asst. auditor, 1st Apr., 1912; asst. cont. and audr.-gen., 16th Aug., 1916; ag. contr. and audr.-gen., 18th Feb., 9th Aug., 1921.
HUNTER, THOMAS GILBERT.—B. 1873; inspr. of mines, Ceylon, June, 1900.
HUSTLER, G. H., M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1879; ed. at Bradford Gram. Schl., Harrogate Coll. and Leeds Univ.; med. offr., Fiji, 1908.

HUTCHINGS, HUGH H.—Inspr. of govt. schls., Turks and Caicos Is., 1888-1906; N.W. Prov., 1906-19; ag. audr., 1908-13; audr., 1913-19; ag. comanr. and ag. judge of sup. ct., five occasions, 1911-19; ag. comanr. of Cayman Is., 1912-13; comanr., Cayman Is., June, 1919.

HUTCHINSON, SIR JOSEPH TURNER, KT. BACH (1896).—B. 1850; M.A., Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister, Mid. Temp., 1879; Queen's advoc. C. Coast, Apr., 1888; ch. just., Jan., 1889; ditto, Windward Is., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1898; ditto, Ceylon, 1906; retired, 1911.

HUTSON, SIR ERNEST, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), B.A.—B. 1864; 2nd ck. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1886; asst. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1886; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1898; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and reg. gen. Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comanr., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour comanr., 1903; col. sec., Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., educn. comanr., Apr., 1909; represented Fiji and Western Pacific high comanr. at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep.-gov., May and Oct., 1909, Sept., 1910, Mar., Sept., Oct., 1911, and June, 1912; comanr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov. of Fiji and high comanr. for Western Pacific, 13th Aug., 1915, to 25th May, 1916, and from 19th June to 9th Oct., 1918; gov'r., B. Honduras, 26th Nov., 1918.

HUTSON, JOHN, M.B.E. (1893), B.A. (Durham), 1879 (2nd cl. honours in cls.); M.B., C.M. (Edin.), 1888 D.P.H. (Cantab.), 1900.—B. 1859; res. surg. gen.; hosp., Barbados, 1886; sen., ditto, 1886-88; mem. Barbados quarantine comanr., 1903; chmn. of Glendairy prison comanr., 1908; del. for Barbados at W. Indian quarantine confce., 1904; del. for Barbados on cent. quarantine authority for W. Indies, 1907; poor law inspr., 1901; mem. of bd. of health, 1895, surg.-lieut., Barbados volrs., 1904; surg.-capt., 1906; med. assessor, 1906; ag. M.L.C., 1907; deleg. for Barbados at West Indian Tuberculosis Confce. at Trinidad, 1913; pub. health inspr., Barbados, 1913; surg.-major, Barbados Volr. Force.
