Aust., 1873; clk. cust., 1874; clk., audit offr., 1875; clk., cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measurer of ships, cust., 1889; correpdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comnr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correpdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; clk. ast. and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; dep. comnr. of taxes and stamps, Jan., 1896, to Mar., 1897; comnr., ditto, Mar. to Oct., 1897; dep. comnr., ditto, Oct., 1897, to Feb., 1911; also pres., marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosene, 1902; comnr. of trade marks and regir. of copyrights, 1904-7; also controller, outer harbour, Port Adelaide, Nov., 1907; also controller, ocean steamers wharf, Apr., 1909; also chief inspr. under Inflammable Oils Act, June, 1909; also supt. of life-saving service, Aug., 1909; also controller of harbours, July, 1911; chmn. of harbs. bd. comnr., since 1914.
SEARLE, HON. MALCOLM WILLIAM, K.C.—Puiane judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), June, 1910.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SELWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S. Stltms. civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. ast. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apl., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. ast. treas. and comnr. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Maeclesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv. with Cheshire Regt. from 20th Nov., 1914 to 23rd Jan., 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher div. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; res. clk., air miny., 31st Oct., 1921; seconded for serv. in C.O., as ast. prim., 9th Jan., 1922.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut. col. comndg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (deeps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkstone div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; partly, under sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of crownagents office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint partly sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1861; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 12th Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., 1st Jan., 1916; ast. censor, 1914; censor, 18th Apr., 1918.
SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ast. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, 1st Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., 1st Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., 1st June, 1917.
SEHEULT, RAUL M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1867; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 15th Feb., 1893; res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, 15th Oct., 1910.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yawa comnr., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regist. & office and clk. pub. market, 1892; ass't sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treas., 1895; sec., bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treas., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1911, and from Apr. to June, 1918.
SIGNORET, G. B.—Ck. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treas., 1893; ch. clk., registr.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treas., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1900), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1903), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn. Pacific cable comtce., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtce., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high comnstr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELDON, A. A.—Asts. dist. comnstr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.

SELLS, H.C., B.A. (Oxon.)—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898 to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postmr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909; offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. collr. of land rev. and regist. of deeds and regist. of Muhammadan marriages, Penang, 27th Mar., 1912; ast. censor in addtn. to other duties, 1st Jan., 1915; chmn., snty. bd., Kinta, 7th Aug., 1919.

SEMPEL, D. H.—Ck. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as ck., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, regist. of titles, regis. of friendly societies, and regist. gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916.
SENARATNE, CORNELIUS.—B. 1870; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; extra office ast. to govt. agk., S. Prov., Feb., 1921.
SENEMIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1893; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B. calos Kacheheri, Oct., 1920.

SENIOR, BERNARD, C.M.G. (1916), I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1866; clk. to the bindry. comn. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotch educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-sec. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1888; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Coast, Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuanaland, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; aud.-gen., O.R.C., Sept., 1902; recvd. thanks of S. of S. for war for services rendered in connection with audit of Boer refugee camp accnts. after the S. African war; mem. of comnstr. to inquire into law relating to mining for diamonds and precious stones in O.R.C., 1903, for which recvd. thanks of govt.; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.I.C., May, 1904; deleg. for O.R.C. at S. African freights confce., 1904-5; mem. of O.R.C. civ. ser. coman., 1905; chmn. of govt. tender bd.; dir. of National Bank of O.R.C.; col. auditor, Ceylon, 1st Mar., 1907; ag. treas. from 16th Nov., 1908, to 5th Feb., 1909; treas. and comnstr. of stamps, Ceylon, 6th Feb., 1909; is also ch. comnstr. of loan bd., a dir. of the W. and O. pension fund, chmn. of rly. advisory bd., and mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."

SENNETT, CEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; asst. censor, 1916; asst. regist., impts. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921.
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acutt., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot', 30th May, 1905.
SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1939.

SETH, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1913; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th May, 1904; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; ast. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907; ast. to atty.-gen., 15th Dec., 1910; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 6th Dec., 1912; collr. war tax, Singapore, 26th Mar., 1917; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; offr. ast. to atty.-gen., Singapore, Nov., 1918; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor in addition, May, 1919; has held numerous acting appts.

SEVERN, CLAUD, C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1869; M.A. Cantab.; temporary clk. in librarian's dept., foreign office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; ast. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; ast. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; ast. sec. to res. gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high comnstr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; sec. to high comnstr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admstd. govt., 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912, 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913, 6th Nov. to 8th Dec., 1916, 10th Nov. to 16th Dec., 1917, and from 12th Sept., 1913 to 29th Sept., 1919; L.L.D., Hong Kong; col. sec., Hong Kong, Jan., 1921; admstd. govt., 8th Apr. to May, 1921.
SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schi., and St. Andrews Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliva, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1920; ag. govt. ast., N.C. Prov., May, 1921.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CYRIL WILLIAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.N.A.F., 1918; ment. in des., 1919; demob., 1919; ast. dist. commr., Zanzibar, 1921.

SHANKLAND, WALTER CHARLES.—Clerical ast., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1886; engnr.'s ast., P.W.D., 5th Oct., 1887; 3rd cls. acctnt.'s branch, P.W.D., 23rd May, 1889; 4th cls. elk., ditto, 1st Apr., 1892; 3rd cls. offr. and elk. of wks., in charge of P.W.D., Essequibo dist., 9th Nov., 1895; student, Middle Temple, 1900; passed final bar exam., 1900; 2nd cls. offr. and elk., in charge of P.W.D., Essequibo dist., 30th Mar., 1906; ch. elk., local govt. bd., 1st Dec., 1906; 1st cls. offr. and elk. in charge of P.W.D., Demerara dist., 5th Sept., 1906; sec., local govt. bd., and inspr. of dists., 22nd Sept., 1908; attached to L.G.B., London, 1911; ag. stip. mag., 24th Oct., 1912 to 17th Feb., 1914, and from 15th Mar. to 12th Oct., 1914; apptd. by govt. to report on finances of town coun. of New Amsterdam, 1914; commr. to administer oaths to affidavits, 17th Apr., 1914; mem. bd. of agric., 1914; J.P. for the col., 1915; recd. thanks of L.G.B. on two occasions for special work carried out for the bd.

SHANNON, C. M.—B. 1869; clerical ast., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1888; 5th cls. elk., G.P.O., June, 1889; ditto, treasy., June, 1890; 4th cls. elk., ditto July, 1893; ag. acctnt., mines dept., June, 1893 to Mar., 1894; govt. offr. (mining regns.) and J.P., Apr., 1897; 3rd grade land survs., lands and mines dept., Aug., 1902; 3rd cls. elk., ditto, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. elk., audit dept., Aug., 1908; ag. acctnt., G.P.O., July to Sept., 1911; ag. paymaster of police, June, 1913 to Mar., 1914; 1st cls. elk., audit dept., Aug., 1913.

SHARPE, HAMNET HOLDITCH.—B. 1864; entd. R. Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tourmaline" and numerous other ships (Egypt, medal, 1882, and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to comdr.-in-chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N.S. Wales, May, 1903; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 22nd Nov., 1904.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. commr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy commr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as commr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; commr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; commr. commdr.-in-chief, and commr.-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.
SHARPE, H. B.—Temp. ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SHARPE, RICHARD.—B. 1882; ed. Sexey Schi., Somt. (Somt. county jur. and inter. scholarship) and hon. mention senir. scholarship); entd. Impl. cust. serv. by open compet. exam., and appld. to London, 1903; London Univ. cert. polit. economy with sp. distinc. in exchange, finance and taxation, 1907; seconded to govt. of Liberia as sub-inspr., cust., 1908-10; ch. inspr. cust. and finan. adviser, 1911-12; Br. receiver of Liberian cust., 1911-19; offr. recr., liquidating German property in Liberia, 1917-18; knighted comdr., Order of African Redemption, 1918; treas., Dominica, Feb., 1920; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., collr. of cust. and excise, regier. of shipping, chunn., quarantine bd., Impl. trade corresp.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and sen. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prize man in English prose; B.A., LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S., civ. ser., 1900; collr. of land rev., Krian, 1901; mag. 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr. of Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to War Office, directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919.

SHAW, WALTER SIDNEY.—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908, to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admstr. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911, to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., R. Honduras, 1912; puiana justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN, I.S.O. (1917).—Cik. at Clanwilliam, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. sec.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. of acctnts., 14th Apl., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895; acctng. offr., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. elk. and acting offr., 1st Mar., 1901; ast. under col. sec., 1st Apl., 1902; ag. undersec. for agric., Sept., 1907, to Mar., 1908; mem. of Rhodesian pub. serv. bd. of enquiry, Apl. to Sep., 1909; under-sec. for the Interior, Union of S.Africa, May, 1910; ag. sec. for the Interior, 28th Sept., 1914; sec. for the Interior, 1st Oct., 1919.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., Ferryland (Newfild.), 1889-93; again in 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; mem. of treasy. bd., 1898; mayor of St. John's, 1902; mem. exec. coun. 1904.

SHEARMAN-TURNER, PERCY.—B. 1874; ed. at Forest Schi. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1898; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Oxford Circuit, C.C.C.; recorder of titles, E.A.P., 1907 to 1910; ag. dep. prin. regier. of documents and admstr.-gen., 1908 to 1909; legal mem. of coun. and atty.-gen., Zanzibar, 14th Oct., 1910; ag. ast. judge, 7th Aug., 1913; ag. ch. judge, 7th Mar., 1919.

SHEEDY, FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—late capt., E.A.P. forces; ent. army, A.V.C. 1911; vety. offr., E.A.P., July, 1914; served throughout Great War, 5th Aug., 1914 to 22nd Sept., 1919 (E. Africa Mounted Rifles and E.A.V.C.); adjutant, E.A.V.C., 9th Jan., 1916; M.B.E. (mil.), 1918, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med.; dep. ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 31st Oct., 1919; ag. ch. vety. offr., May, 1920 to Mar., 1921.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian schls., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin.—Pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; 2nd Heut., R.F.A., Sept., 1917; demob., May, 1919; cadet, F.M.S.; Mar., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. adviser, Munar, Mar., 1921.

SHEDDON, HUGH FREDERICK, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Manch.)—Ed. Manchester Grain Schl. and Owen's Coll.; civ. surg., H.M. forces, and capt. and M.O., Health Border Pol., 1901-6; dist. surg. and M.O.H., Ladybrand, 1906-14; 37th Field Ambulance, France, 1915-16; regt. temp. maj., R.A.M.C., 1st Western Gen. Hosp., 1917; ast. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1920.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1889; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. vice-consul, Ghent, 1905; ag. vice-consul, Havre, 1906-7; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; ast. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; sec. to first minister, 1909-10; collr. (now dist. consnr.), Pembes, 16th Oct., 1912; ag. mag., 30th Aug., 1915; ag. ch. sec., 5th Mar. to 17th Nov., 1919; 4th cls., Order of the Aliyeh and 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar.

SHEPHERD, FREDERICK REZUS.—B. 1859; sup't of Skerrett's training schl., Antigua, Leeward Is., 1891-1905; curator and agric. sup't., St. Kitts, Nevis, Feb., 1904; ag. sup't., sugar cane experiments, Antigua, 1899-1904; deleg. to various agric. confrs. held by Impl. commr.; ag. sup't. of agric., Leeward Is., July to Nov., 1910.

SHEPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stithms, 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wesley, Aug., 1905; senior med. offr., Singapore, July, 1911.
SHERGOLD, F. Y.—B. 1900; app'td. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 10th Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castletown Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judg. olk., Nasaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1908, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; app'td. ast. to atty.-gen., Apl., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., E.A.P., 1913; ag. puisne judge, July, 1914.

SHERWOOD, SIR ARTHUR PENCEY, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G., M.V.O., A.D.C.—B. 1854; sup't. of Dominion pol., Canada, 1882; commr., Dominion pol., 1888; ed. commr. of pol. of Canada, 1911; res. 11th June, 1919.

SHERWOOD, MONTAGUE EARLE, M.B.E.—B. 1884; cadet, S. Stithms., 21st Oct., 1907; attached to col. sec.'s office, 1st Jan., 1908; attached to land office, Singapore, 23rd Jan., 1908; dep. collr. of land reg. and dep. regt. of deeds, Singapore, 31st Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, 29th Jan. to 16th Nov., 1909; attached to dist. office, Alor Gajah, 28th Dec., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, 1911; attached to land office, Malacca, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, 7th Apr., of Cls. V., 13th May, 1911; ag. dist. ms 15th Oct., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Alor Feb., 1914; ag. ast. to rec., Malacca 1913; ag. harbour-mast., Malacca, in other duties, 26th Jan., 1916; pri A.D.C. to gov., 1st Aug., 1916; offr. 16th Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to H.I. Feb., 1920; ag. sec. to high consnr., 1920.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. mily. service (France) 1914-18; on sts priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspecti Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—app'd., after compet. exam., 2nd di assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; mino 1911.
SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, M.B Med. offr., Nyassaland, 1908; transf'd. 1909; re-transf'd as med. offr., Nyass med. offr., E.A.P., 1912; dep. prin. Tanganyika Territory, 15th Oct., 1919.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (191 F.R.S.C.—B. 1869; ed. at Walkerton and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1885) and Glasgow and Edin. Univs. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885 prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908 consnr., Canada, 1898-1917; chmn. manuscripts publication board, Oct., on history, banking and economics.

SHUCKBURGH, JOHN EVLIAN, —B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; app'd. jnr. dele., I.O., Oct., 1900; transf'd. to pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint edit Office List, Oct., 1901; priv. sec. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. dept., Mar., 1906; editor, telegra Aug., 1917 to Nov., 1912; ast. sec., Oct., 1912; sec. polit. dept., Mar., ast. under-sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mr.

SHUFFREY, PAUL.—Ed. St. Paul ford; open sal., Lincoln 1908; B 1912; ast. dist. consnr., S. Leone, sec. to gov., 1914-15; ag. dist. consnr. 1916 and 1916-17; ag. priv. sec. to gov dist. consnr., N. Sherbro., Nov., 1919; dist. consnr., 1st Jan., 1920 consnr., Prov. 1921.
SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, JOHN WALLSEND, D.D.—B. 1867; fo principal of Wycliff Hall, Oxford, Amira, Nottingham, and hon. canon of consecrated, 1910.

SIFTON, HON. SIR CLIFFORD, (1915), K.C.—B. 1861, Co. Middle ed. London, Ont., High Schl. and Vi Prince of Wales, Medallist, 1890; c bar of Manitob, 1882; elec. to legis., 1888; atty.-genl. and min. of when codified laws relating to civil procedu had charge of negotiations with fo respecting the Manitoba Schl. Dominion, 1895; called to federal min. of the interior, 1896; elec. to scol. for Brandon; British ext. b'for Bndry. Tribunal, 1903; re-elec. to E and 1904; resig. from the cabinet. Canadian representatives at interna Washington on preservation of
SIMSON, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub-inspr., Beautoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; astt. commnr., 1918.
SIMSON, COLIN COAPE.—Chief med. offr., Papua, 19th Oct., 1908.

SIMSON, ERIC ANDREW.—B. 1895; ed. Wellington Coll. (schlr., 1900), and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mily. serv., R.F.A., 17th div., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1916; staff, 12th div., Feb. to Dec., 1916; capt., R.A.F., Jan., 1917 to June, 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, astt. prin., C.O., 3rd Aug., 1920.
SIMSON, H. B.—Temp. astt. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919.
SIMSON, J. A.—Ed. in England, Germany and Switzerland; astt. gen. in India for the British Protectorates in Africa.

SINCKLER, EDWARD GOULBURN.—B. 1856; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Nov., 1874; acted as clk. to lieut.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. correspide. branch, col. sec.'s office, 1879; ag. astt. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1890; 1st clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, and clk., courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; commnr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, astt. ct. of appeal, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. regis., friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. F, July, 1896, to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag., judge, and coroner, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apr., 1898; ag. coroner, St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. commr. of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist. E, Oct., 1901; discharged duties of pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, in addition to own duties, Sept., 1905; and from Aug. to Oct., 1907; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, 18th July to 23rd Nov., 1906; coroner, dist. E, 27th May, 1907; ag. commr. of probates, May to Aug., 1903, and May to Oct., 1908; ag. judge, petty debt. ct., Bridgetown, July to Oct., 1909; ag. pol. mag. and judge, Dist. "D," in addition to own duties, Aug. and Oct., 1910, and May to Aug., 1911; commr. of census, 1911; mem. and hon. sec. of historic sites comte., 1910; suggested Nelson centenary postage stamp, 1904; took active part in promotion of celebration of tercentenary of Barbados, 1905; compiler of first "Barbados Government Handbook," 1912, and compiler of handbooks for 1913 and 1914; man. of Barbados govt. savings bk., Aug., 1919.
SINCLAIR, SIR ARCHIBALD, BART., C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1880; major, 2nd Life Guards; personal mily. secy. to Mr. Churchill.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schlr.; mem. of swine commn. which visited Europe in 1909; unsuccess. cand., legis. ass., P.E.L., 1908; cl. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-e.l., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admn., 29th Dec., 1921.

SINCLAIR, JOHN HOUSTON, C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1871; apptd. to col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Dec., 1893; local auditor, E.A.P. and Uganda Rwly., Dec., 1895; H.M. vice-consul, Zanzibar, Apr., 1899; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1903, to June, 1904, Feb. to May, 1905; consul, 1st July, 1906; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Aug., 1906, to Feb., 1907, Mar. to Oct., 1911, and Feb., 1913 to Apr., 1914; ch. sec. Zanzibar Prot. 1st Jan., 1914; pres. of internat. bureau at Zanzibar for the suppression of the slave trade, 1914 and 1916; ag. res., Aug., 1916, to Apr., 1917, and from Mar. to Dec., 1919; also ag. high commnr., 8th Nov. to 2nd Dec., 1919; ag. high commnr. and res., 8th Aug. to 1st Oct., 1920; ag. res., 1st Oct., 1921; res., 10th Jan., 1922.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schlr., N.Z.; bar-rister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comsn., 1912.

SINGAPORE, LORD BISHOP OF RIGHT REV. CHARLES JAMES FERGUSON-DAVIE, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of St. Paul, Preston, 1896-99; dom. chap. to Bishop of Lahore, 1899-1902; S.P.G. mission at Rewari, 1902-1907; S.P.G. mission at Rawal Pindi, 1907-09; consecrated Lord Bishop of Singapore, 24th Aug., 1909.

SIROCOM, HAROLD SEBASTIAN.—B. 1878; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1904; ag. ass't dist. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1904; ag. ass't dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, May, 1907; ag. ass't dist. offr., Pekan, Nov., 1907; cls. V, Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; seconded, commr. of cust., Johore, 1913; cls. IV, Nov., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, Dec., 1917; cls. III, Jan., 1918; seconded, ass't adviser, Muar, Johore, June, 1918; cls. II, Nov., 1919; ag. commr. of lands and mines, Johore, June, 1920; ass't adviser, Muar, Apr., 1921.

SISNETT, HERBERT KORTRIGHT MCDONNELL.—Ed., at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902; and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. govr., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904, as res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904, for two and a half months in 1905, and from June to July, 1906; registr.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. commr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, 5th Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.
SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; ass't dist. commr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; ag. dist. commr., Ashanti, 1916-1918.
SKENE, RALPH RANGABE FELIX HENRY.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl., London, and Calvin's Coll., Geneva; ass't collr., E. Africa Prot., 5th Dec., 1897; collr., 1st Apr., 1903.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—4th clk., col. sec's office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apr., 1915; o.c. cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb., 1920; clk. to commr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec's. office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. ass't col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns., Leeward Is., Aug., Nov., 1921.
SKINNER, L. E.—Asst. supt. of pol., E.A.P., Apr., 1916.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; 1st cls. certif. of educn., Group I, 1894; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africs. and St. Helens, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's medal and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helens, Mar., 1903; sergt-major and drill inst., St. Helens volrs.; also sanitary inspr., firmmaster and inspr. of weights and measures, 1906; also schl. attendance offr., in charge of Zulu prison, and ass't clk. in cts. in prov.'s off., 1907-1909; transf'd. to Leeward Islands pol., and apptd. to Antigua in June, 1909, where sub-inspr. of pol., and mil. instr. to defence force and defence reserve; transf'd. to Montserrat in July, 1910, inspr. of pol., inspr. of weights and instr. reserve, mem. of Board of Health, a justice of prison; then offr. in charge discipline; transf'd. to Dominica in 1911, where inspr. of pol., mil. instr. to deft. offr. in charge of prison discipline, weights and measures, supt. of R brigade, offr. in charge of powder mag. mem. of Roseau town bd., and bd. o commrs.; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Is. mag., Dist. F, May-Aug., 1914; comd. Dominica defence force, 1914; comd. local forces, Dominica, 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) for B.W.I.R., at Barbados, June-July paying offr. and in-chr. recon. personal, B.W.I.R., Dominica, from 1917.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Temp. consent., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

SLADEN, ARTHUR F., C.M.G. (1911-1916).—B. 1866; ed. Haileybury Coll. Naval Coll., Greenwich; went to Car apptd. to gov.-gen.'s office, 1891; now to H.E. Lord Byng of Vimy, gov.-gen. similar capacity to five previous govs.; SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Jun. cl Jamaica, 1883; sen. ass't clk. cts., 1889; dep. clk. cts., Westmoreland, cls. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. regis., 1st Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with J. tinging (medal), 1897; ag. regis., 1899; passed exam. before judges equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., 1900; ag. commr. and judge of gr. Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the soc'y of Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. res. mag., 1908; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Isl.; ag. commr., Mar. to Oct., 1909; J.P. ton, Jamaica; stip. mag., Kingston, res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910, and in res. mag., St. Thomas, July, 1910; co edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1910.

SLATER, ALEXANDER RANSFORD (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. C. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangle Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. ass't W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd ass't, P.M. 1900; ag. 2nd ass't col. sec., D. on spec. duty at camp for prisons Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd ass't Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, judge, Badulla, Apr., 1906; addition sec. and clk. legis. couns., Apr., 1907; deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908 col. sec., Feb., 1909; ag.prin. ass't, col. 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, S joint commr. to report on salaries of Nov., 1911; prin. ass't. col. sec. 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec. 18th Aug., 1914; on special duty in Ist to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. govr., G. to Nov., 1916, and from Nov., 1916, to SLOAN, HON. WILLIAM.—B. 1864 residence in British Columbia in 188 Vancouver and Nanaimo; unsuccess H. of C., 1900; elec. to H. of C., Comox-Antl.; re-elec., 1908, but r favour of Hon. William Templeman, rev., who was defeated in Victoria legis. ass., B.C., 1916; min. of mines i cabinet, 1916.

SLOLEY, Sir Herbert Cecil, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moiros (medal with clasp, 1877-89); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1886; mentioned in despatches; sub-insp., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; insp., 1886; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. commr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. commr., 1898; ag. res. commr., Dec., 1900; res. commr., Sept., 1901.

SLYNE, D., C.B.E. (1919).—Barrister-at-law; Lord O'Hagan's medallist and David Lynch medallist, King's Inn, Ireland; entd. Imp.in rev. dept., 1880; rec-gen., Trinidad, 1903; commr. of currency, Trinidad, 1903; mem. of Port of Spain sewerage bd., and water authority, 1904; mem. of Port of Spain town bd., 1907; chmn. of comte., for liquidation of enemy businesses, 1914; chmn. of P.O.G. fund, 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1916; chmn. of bd. of management of agricultural banks, 1916.

SMALL, Robert.—Ed. at Univ. Coll. Schi., St. Thomas Hosp., King's Coll. Hosp., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; certif. of Lond. Schol. of Trop. Med.; L.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901; D.P.H. (Lond.), 1905; D.T.C. (Camb.), 1906; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901-1902; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 16th Feb., 1906; M.O.H., Mombasa, Apr., 1913.

SMALLEY, James Thornton, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr., and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O. Ra.; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, May, 1913; ag. asst. M.O.H., Feb., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, July, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital.

SMALLWOOD, Henry Armstrong.—B. 1869; ed. at King's schl., Canterbury, and Foster's, Stubbington; entered navy, 1885; on board H.M.S. "Victoria" at time of disaster, June, 1893; asst. commr., Colo West, and stip. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Ba and Yasawa, Apr., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, and priv. sec. to gov. Fiji, Apr., 1896; ag. sec. to W. Pac. high commr., May, 1897, to May, 1898; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Tavuni, Jan., 1899; island treas., Cyprus, Mar., 1900; passed lower standard Greek, Dec., 1900; ag. commr., Nicosia, June-Sept., 1904; treas., St. Lucia, Nov., 1907; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; nominated deleg. to Canadian Reciprocity Conference at Barbados, Jan., 1908; ag. col. sec., July 1908, to Jan., 1909; ag. admstr. and col. sec., Apr., 1909; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1909; treas., E.A.P., Oct., 1911; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; treas., F.M.S., 5th Apr., 1915; financial adviser to Palestine admin., 1st Oct., 1920.
SMART, L. M.—Aast. traffic manager, Uganda Rlyw., Apr., 1919.
SMARTT, Fitzpatrick Forbes Percy.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMARTT, Hon Sir Thom. Wm., K.C.M.G. (1911).—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A. Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; commr. of pub. wks., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1905, and 1906; attended Impl. confce., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Conventn. in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900; M.L.A. for Fort Beaufort and min. for agr., 1921.
SMITH, Alexander.—Treas. aast., E. Africa Prot., 19th Sept., 1904.

SMITH, Allan F., L.S.O. (1906).—Cik. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1895; col. postmr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900 to July, 1901; recvr.-gen., 1908; awarded Royal Humane Soc'y a vellum cert. for saving life, 1914.
SMITH, Charles Bernard, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; jur. supk., educ. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; attached Nigeria Rgt., 1917-18.
SMITH, Cecil Furness.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead schl. and St. John's coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; 2nd lieut., gen. res. of offrs.; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

SMITH, Sir Charles Holled, K.C.M.G., C.B.—B. 1846; ensign, 1863; lieut., 1869; capt., 1877; S. African war, 1879-1881; Zulu campaign; Transvaal cam., battles of Laing's Nek, Ingogo, and Majuba Mountain; despatches, Lon Gaz., May 3rd, 1881; Egypt expedit., 1882; at Ramleh, Tel-el-Mahuta; action at Kassassin; battle of Tel-el-Kebir, Lon. Gaz., Nov. 2nd, 1882; major (brevet), Nov., 1882; with Egyp. army, Jan., 1883, to Aug., 1892; Soudan expedit., 1884-5; lieut.-col., June, 1885; col., Dec., 1888; gov.-gen., Red Sea littoral, and comdt., Suakin, Sept., 1888, to Aug., 1892; Soudan, 1888-91; action of Gamsizah, Lon. Gaz., Jan. 11th, 1889; occupation of Handoul, Jan., 1891; major-gen. coming, Victorian mil. forces, Dec., 1894; several medals, clasps to bronze stars, and mentioned in numerous despatches.
SMITH, Emile Hanek, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 16th Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., 21st Dec., 1912.

SMITH, Ernest Gardiner, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; univ. prizeman in constit. law and hist.; hons. certifs. in civ. law, pub. internat. law and med.jurisprudence; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 29th Aug., 1908; asst. pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., 29th Jan., 1914; crown proscr., 10th Sept., 1914; ag. legal adviser, and mem., leg. coun., for various periods, 1914-19; crown counsel, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921.

SMITH, Francis Jago.—M.A. (Oxon); ed. Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. clas. mods., 1894; 2nd math. mods., 1894; 2nd lt. hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; office asst. to gov't. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1898; commr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; landing survr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901; 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1905; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Feb., 1908; dist. judge, Galle, Dec., 1911; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, July, 1912; on special duty, visiting post offices in Ceylon, Straits and F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1913; postmr.-gen., Oct., 1913; dir. of food production, Nov., 1922.

SMITH, F. M. Urling.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., Ist Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs., 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—B. 1870; ed. at Sherborne Coll.; called to the bar, In Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. “B,” Barbados, 1893; ditto, dist. “D,” Apr., 1894, to July, 1895; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., 1897; pol. mag., dist. “D,” Jan., 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. “A,” Nov., 1901, to Dec., 1902; apptd. pol. mag., dist. “A,” and Bridgetown, Dec., 1902; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal and mast. in chancery in 1906, and again in 1907; judge, asst. ct. of appeal, 1913, also inspr. of prisons, and regsr. of friendly societies, 1917.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, 16th Apr., 1878; elk., ch. sec’s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. elk., ditto, 1881; asst. to ch. sec., 15th Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, 16th Aug., 1886, to 14th Feb., 1887; elk., legis. coun., 1st Mar., 1886, to 24th Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., 15th May, to 16th Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comsrs., Papho, 25th Nov., 1891; registr. gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., 7th June, 1896; has served also as mem. of comttee. of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comttee., 1886; sec. to Queen’s Jubilee Memorial comttee., 1887; mem. of land registn. comsrs., 1891; pres. mun. comsrs., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. comsrs., 1895; mem. of mun. comsrs., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. British delegate of Evecaf, under conven. with Turkey, 20th Mar., to 25th Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; British delegate of Evecaf, 20th July, 1905; ag. recr. gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1909; col. sec., Mauritius, 10th June, 1910; administered govt., Apl. to Nov., 1911; gov., Nyasaland, 17th May, 1913; assumed govt., 23rd Sept., 1913.

SMITH, CART. GEORGE ECHLIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. Royal Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1909; asst. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub-inspr., R. Guiana police, Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, K.C.M.G., 1902-1904; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, K.C.M.G., 1904-1906; asst. dist. supt. of police, E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906; supr. of police, Apr., 1914; comdts., pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, May, 1920.

SMITH, GEORGE WHITFIELD.—F.L.S., elk., pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1879; 3rd treasy. elk., St. Lucia, 1882; sub-collr. of taxes, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Aug., 1882; rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Feb., 1885 (resigned); curator, botanic gardens, Grenada, Dec., 1890 (resigned, 16th Dec., 1893); trav. supt., Imperial dept. of agric., Dec., 1898; pol. mag. and asst. treas., N. dist. Grenada, and pol. mag., 3rd dist., St. Vincent, Apr., 1904; comsrs., Carriacou, Oct., 1904; ag. col. treas., Grenada, 1st Apl. to 4th Dec., 1913; comsrs. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., 1914; was engaged as asst. botanist to W. India, exploration comttee. of Royal Soc. during scientific exploration of St. Vincent, 1888-89; is a corrpdg. mem. of Royal Hort. Soc. of England.

SMITH, GERALD STANLEY WELLS.—B. 1873; graduate, Ontario business coll., Canada; supry. elk., regsr.’s office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; asst. elk., post office, Mar., 1890; 4th elk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. elk., col. sec.’s office, Oc. elk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit elk. thanked by gov. for report on paupers, 1905; has held numerous appointments, including ag. aud. of Wind 4th June to 31st Dec., 1906; 1st Jan., 1907; and from 18th Mar., 1908, to 24 ag. comsrs. of Carriacou and St. Vine Grenadines, 24th Jan. to 31st Dec. auditor, Windward Is., for six months colonial postmaster, Grenada, 9th colonial postmaster, B. Honduras, 13

SMITH, GERARD.—B. 1878; ed. coll.; cadet, S.S. Stltms., Oct., 1902 S.S. emig. depot, S. India, June, 190 exam. in Tamil, July, 1904; ag. 3rd Feb., 1905; passed cadet, July 19 offr., Nibong Tebal, Sept., 1906; a of Indian immgrs., Nov., 1907; let in F.M.S., 1909; offr. of cl. V., Nov., 1909; dist. offr., Dindings, Ja of cl. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1913; Butterworth, S. Stltms., 25th Sep sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, 26th ag. dist.offr., Butterworth, 12th June numerary offr. of cl. III., F.M.S., 1st mag., Ipoh, 18th Feb., 1919; offr., 1919; registr., sup. ct., K. Lumpur ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley SMITH, HENRY J.—B. 1859; exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., 12th 2nd cl. ditto, 1st June, 1897; Queen Apr., 1898; asst. office keeper, 22nd office keeper, 3rd July, 1915.
SMITH, H. M. BRICE.—B. 1884; lington Schl. and Queen’s Coll. Cam res., N. Nigeria 16th Jan., 1909.
SMITH, HERBERT FRANCIS.—B. Malvern coll. (class. scholar); Agents’ office, Feb., 1893; asst. c head of gen. stores dept., August 19.
SMITH, JAMES ALFRED, M.B.E 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., c div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to t 1891; promoted to higher grade, 190 col. regns. comttee., 1907-8; staff elk., 1

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. man, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, man, survey off., Sept., 1893; ward of water-wks. under St. George’s to 1895, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comsrs. of 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton ginn 1912; ag. supt. pub. wks., St. Vine 1913; col. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug. pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 19
SMITH, J. NOEL.—B. 1886; ed Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll., and H Oxford: 2nd lient., 3rd (Res.) Batt Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; secord as ast. res., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov.,

SMITH, LAURENCE.—B. 1876; ed. Schl.; elk. in accts. dept., Nyasalan 3rd ast. treas., Oct., 1902; 2nd Dec., 1908; 1st ast. treas., April, 1 treas., Dec., 1912; organising sec Vol. Res., 1912-13; inspr., enemy 1915; finance offr. in occupied territ Africa, July, 1916 to Sept., 1918; ag 1918 to Apr., 1919; treas. and men leg. couns., May, 1919.

SMITH, LINNISAY L.E.A.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to commr., Turka Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and elk. (coll. of customs, postmr., cashier and reg. of shipping) commr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and elk. (coll. of cust., postmr., cashier, and reg. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. ast. commr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

SMITH, MAXWELL HINLOP.—B. 1873; ent. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1892; ch. inspr. of rev., 1st Aug., 1912; formed and coupled 2nd batt., Trinidad Light Infy., Vols., 1914; seconded for serv. with B.W.I. Regt. in France and Belgium, May, 1917; major in comd. of 8th, 10th and 11th batt., B.W.I. Regt. in France, Belgium and Italy on various occasions; lieut.-col. in comd. of 8th batt., B.W.I. Regt., 1918; ment. in desps., 1918; demob., 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., Trinidad, July, 1919; ag. inspr.-gen., 1920; col. sec., Br. Honduras, June, 1920; ag. gov., Aug., 1920 to Jan., 1921.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings exhibitn.), 3rd cls. mods., 3rd Lit. Hum.; B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, 20th Oct., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., (north), Nov., 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., Apr., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. ast. coll. sec. and elk. of coun., 26th Aug., 1913; ag. ast. ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 19th Sept. to 1st Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1st Jan., 1914; ag. ast. postmstr.-gen., 19th Mar., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, May-July, 1914; ag. postmtr.-gen., Sept.-Oct., 1915; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1916; seconded for military service, 1916-19; ag. deputy reg. and appraiser, 10th June, 1919; ag. ast. supt., Victoria Gaol, 21st July, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 13th Aug., 1919, and 1st Jan., 1920; ag. ast. P.M.G., 24th Oct., 1919; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 6th cls., 17th Feb., 1920; qualified in P.kinese, 31st Mar., 1920; ag. supt., impts. and exports, 19th Jan., 1921.

SMITH, P. C., M.I. Munie.E. (Lond.)—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keates and Fosbrooks, archs. and surveys, Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as ast.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contr., Lond.; ast. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engr., Sheffield, 1902; town engr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engr., Barrie, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.

SMITH, HON. PETER.—Farmer, Perth County, Ontario, and prominent breeder of pure bred stock; warden of his County and for some years clk. of his municipality; elec. to leg. assm., Ontario, Oct., 1919; provincial treas. in U.F.O. gvt., Nov., 1919.

SMITH, RALPH SYDNEY.—B. 1873; entd. Navy, acctnt. branch, 15th July, 1892; ast. paymaster, 27th June, 1896; paymaster, 18th Jan., 1905; staff paymaster, 18th Jan., 1906; served in H.M.S. "Theta" during Cretan troubles, 1898-99, and in blockading squadron at Delagoa Bay during S. African War, 1898-1900 (medal and gratuity); retired from Navy, 8th May, 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1st June, 1907.
SMITH, RICHARD MONTAGUE BOSWORTH.—Sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted police, 1895; inspr., 1905; ast. commr., 1909; govt. sec., 1920.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. elk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, HON. SYDNEY.—Formerly M.P. for Macquarie, New South Wales in the Federal parlmt.; postmr. gen., Commonwealth of Australia, in Reid-McLean ministry, 1904-05.

SMITH, THOMAS SERCOMBE, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.).—Hong Kong cadet, 1882; attached to C.O., 1883; passed cadet, 1886; ag. ast. regis. gen., 1886-90, except for five months as ag. pol. mag.; ast. regis.-gen., 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1893; ag. puisne judge, various dates, 1895-7, and in 1900, 1901, 1902 and 1903; treas., Dec., 1896; pol. mag., July, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1898-9, for 3 months in 1901, and from Aug., 1905, to Dec., 1906; puisne judge, S. Stiths., 1907; ag. chief judicial commr., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Apr., 1915; honorary extra legal adviser, C.O., June, 1915.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK HATTON, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1863; solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1865; atty.-gen., Oct., 1874; admtd. the govt., Apr. to Sept., 1884; and in 1887; gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1888; gov., Bahamas, 1895; high commr., Cyprus, 1898-1904.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. at Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb., Rustat school, 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class tripso, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; elk. col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; ast. audr., Nyassaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr. N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. elk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished commn. owing to wounds, 29th Sept., 1918, and returned to col. audit dept.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITH-DORRIEN, GEN. SIR HORACE LOCKWOOD, G.C.B. (1913), G.C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1886); grand offr., legion of honour; A.D.C. gen. to the King, 1910-14.—B. 1858; served in Zulu war, 1879; Egyptian war, 1882; Soudan campaign, 1885; Soudan field force, 1885-6, 4th cls. Medjidie; 4th cls. Osmanieh, Chitral expedit., 1895; N.W. Frontier, India, 1897-98; Tirah exped. force; Nile expedit., 1898; S. African war, 1900-01; European war, 1914-15; adjt.-gen. in India, 1901-3; commdg. Quetta div. of army in India, 1903-7; commdg.-in-chief, Aldershot, 1907-12; commdg.-in-chief southern commd., 1912-14; commdg. 2nd army corps and 2nd British expedit. force in France, 1914-15; commdg. E. African expedit. force, 1915; gov., Gibraltar, 9th July, 1918; assumed govt., 7th Sept., 1918.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripos, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1888; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comm. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comte. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, Union of S. Africa, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defense, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confere. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa since 1919.

SMYLY, Sir Philip Crampton, K.T. Bach., (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch.just., S. Leone, 1901; ch.just., Gold Coast, 1911.

SMYTH, Herbert Washington, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1867; ed. at Westm. and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A., LL.M., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., M.I.M.M.; barr-at-law; advoc. of sup. ct., Transvaal; unpaid asst. to mineral adviser to office of woods, 1891; sec., dept. of mines, Siam, 1891; director-gen. of mines and geological survey, Siam, 1895; sec., Siamese Legation, 1897 (order of White Elephant, 3rd cls.); Murchison award of R.G.S. for travels in Siam, 1898; sec. to mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; M.L.C., Transvaal, 1906; mem. exec. coun. while ag. comm. of mines, 1906; sec. for mines, 1907; ag. sec. for mines, Union of S. Africa, and comm. of mines, Natal, 1910; ch.insp. of factor es, 1918; author of "Five Years in Siam," "Mast and Sail in Europe and Asia," papers on Indo-China, etc., etc.

SMYTH, James Lloyd.—B. 1871; served in R.I.C. from 1889 to 1897; transfd. from detec. dept., Belfast, to Jamaica constab., Sept., 1897; ag. supt., M. and S. co. gaol, Sept., 1900, to Feb., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Br. Hond., July, 1906; J.P. for colony; ag. supt. of pol., 20th Sept., to 26th Oct., 1906; vis. just. to Corozal and dist. pris., Dec., 1906; ag. supt. of pol., 4th Apr., 1907, to 3rd Mar., 1908; vis. just. to Belize prs., June, 1907; ag. dist. commr., Corozal, Dec., 1908, to Feb., 1909.
SNELL, Harold Emley, B.A. (1905).—B. 1888; cl. exhib., Wadham Coll., Oxford, 1905; cadet, Fiji, 1911; dist. commr., Ba, 1915; offl. sec. to gov., 1920.
SOLOMON, Michael Claude.—Ag. 3rd cl. clk., island med. dept., Jamaica, Mar., 1885; 3rd cl. clk., Oct., 1888; 2nd cl. clk., July, 1891.

SOLOMON, Hon. Sir William Henry, K.C.M.G. (1913) KT. Bach. (1907). M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town and Cambridge Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1876; raised to the bench, Kimberley, 1887; afterwards transferred to Eastern Districts Court in same capacity; puisne judge sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appellate div. of sup. ct. of South Africa, May, 1910.
SORZANO, Leonardo Joseph.—B. 1863; entered civ. serv., Trinidad, 20th Apr., 1882; sub-revvr., sub-collr. of customs and postmr., Tobago, and harmbr., 1st Apr., 1913; mag. warden, Tobago, Jan., 1919.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1st Baron (creat. 1917), Rt. Hon. Sir Francis John Stephens Hopwood, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec. rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comtees. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comm., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comm., 1906; mem. of comm. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings" 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comm. on electoral reform, 1909; registr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comm., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, Wilfred Thomas.—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; offl. asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1906; asst. gov't. agt. Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919.

SOUZA, J. S. de.—B. 1879; ed. Luso Braziliro Coll. and Lycee, Lisbon, Bombay Univ. and Royal Infirn., Edin.; L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); public vaccinator, Zanzibar govt., 1st June, 1903; asst. med. offr. since 1st Sept., 1903; temp. ag. asst. collr., Nov., 1911.

SOUZA, Simon Isidoro de.—B. 1863; ed. at the R. Catholic gram. schl., Lagos; 1st despatch clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Lagos, June, 1895; acted as clk. of the legia. coun., 1900; reg. of correspondence, June, 1900; conf. clk. to gov. and clk. of leg. coun., July, 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov., and clk. to exec. coun., Feb., 1902; in charge of Ibadan residency, Nov., 1903.

SPALDING, Col. Warner Wright, C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1844; lieut., Royal Marines, 1862-1869; carried colours in operations at Simonosekile, Japan, 1864; N. S. Wales artill., 1871-1896; 2nd in cmd. of N.S.W. Sudan contng., 1885; ch. mag. Norfolk Is., 1896-1898.
SPEARMAN, Baruch, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Camb.), D.T.M. and H.—B. 1877; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1912; capt., Uganda med. surv., Sept., 1916; med. offr. of hth., Zanzibar, Nov., 1920.

SPEED, Sir Edwin Arney, KT. Bach. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripos, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. commr., G. Coast, 1893; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.

SPENCE, R. O. H.—B. 1867; Cler. asst., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 16th Aug., 1886; ag. 2nd clk., P.W. dept., Aug., 1889; ag. 3rd clk., treas'y., Jan., 1890; gov. offr., govt. ld. dept., Apr., 1890; clk., govt. ld. dept., May, 1890; sec., Bartika commrs., June, 1891, to May, 1893; clk., dept. mines, Oct., 1892; ag. 3rd cl. clk., dept. mines, Apr., 1893; gort offr., dept. mines, July, 1893; J.P., May, 1894; comnr. to admnr. oaths, June, 1894; dist. gov. offr., dept. mines, Aug., 1896; warden, dept. mines, Nov., 1896; obtnd. certif. as sworn land surv., June, 1896; ag. ch. clk., dept. mines, Feb., 1900; obtained 1st cls. certif. in assaying and mine surv., Camborne schl. of mines, 1902; elected assoc., Inst. of M. and M., Lond., Apr., 1902; 1st cls. offr., dept. of lands and mines, Apr., 1903; ag. ast. comnr. of lands and mines, Apr. to Dec., 1904; 1st cls. clk., dept. of lands and mines, July, 1905; ch. clk., ditto, Oct., 1905; ag. comnr. of lands and mines, 1906, 1911-12, 1914-15, and 1916; ast. comnr. of lands and mines, 1st Jan., 1916.

SPENCER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legia. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comnr., on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comdt., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in cmd. of diva. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of prs. and ast. to dist. comnr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comnr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf. to H. East Africa, as ast. dist. comnr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comnr., Njombe, from July, 1907; dist. comnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1912; senr. ast. sec. secretariat, Jan., 1919; elk. of councils, Jan., 1919; ag. ast. chief sec., Jan., 1919.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. ast. registr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros. Mag., 1905; ag sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908; ag. registr., supreme ct., Penang, Dec., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1909; solr.-gen., Apr., 1911; ag. judel. comnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1912; solr.-gen., May and Oct., 1912; ag. temp. judge, sup. ct., July-Sept., 1912, and Nov., 1912; puline judge, Dec., 1913; prs. toddy comnr., Selangor, Oct., 1916; ag. ch. judl. comnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., S. Sttlmtn., Oct., 1920; ag. senr. puline judge, Feb.-Apr., 1921.

SRESHTA, MAURICE SALVADOR.—B. 1873; B.A., Madras; Barrister-at-law; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. ast. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1898; Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1898; adill. pol. mag., Tangalla, Sept., 1900; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1900; Avisawella, Dec., 1901; Galle, Sept., 1905; comnr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Jaffna, Jan., 1911; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1915; ditto, Karunegala, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, May, 1921; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Aug., 1921; dist. judge, Jaffna, Oct., 1921.

STACE, WALTER TERENCE.—B.A., Trin. coll., Dublin; B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 29th oct., 1910; attached to the Galle Kachcheri, 1st Dec., 1910; office ast. to govt. agt., Galle, March, 1912; pol. mag., Chilaw, May, 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; priv. sec. to gov. and extra ast. col. sec., Sept., 1915; ast. censor, Dec., 1916; censor, May, 1918; dist. judge, Negombo, Apr., 1920.

STACK, ALAN EDWARD, O.B.E. (1919)—B. 1878; entrd. navy, 1896; ret. as paymaster, comdr., 1919; served on Mediterranean, N. America and W. Indies, Cape, E. Indies, and Atlantic stations; sec. to rear-admiral commanding 3rd and 4th divts., Home Fleet, Portsmouth, 1910-11; sec., British naval mission to Turkey, Mar., 1912 to Sept., 1914; sec. to vice-admiral and senr. naval offr., Malta, Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1916; British sec. to confee. of allied admirals, Malta, Mar., 1916; ast. sec. to provisional admstn., German E. Africa, Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. sec., Feb.-June, 1919; ast. ch. sec. Tanganyika Territory, June, 1919; ag. ch. sec., Mar.-Aug., 1920; S. Africa medal; Coronation medal; Medjidie, 3rd cls., chevalier, Legion of Honour; offr., Order of the Crown of Italy.
STAINER, COURTENAY EDWIN, LIEUT.COMDR., R.N.—B. 1875; ast. mast. attendant, Colombo, Nov., 1913; ag. mast. attd., in addition to his own duties, Apr., 1914; mast., attld., Colombo, Apr., 1915.

STANFORD, COL. THE HON. SIR WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, K.B.E. (1919), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1892), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1850; mag. & clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal); mem. native laws and customs comnr., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1; on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs comnr., Sept., 1903, to Jan., 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; mem. of senate, U. of S.A.
STANLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR LYULPH, K.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1875; ed. Oxford Univ. (B.A.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; M.P. for Eddisbury div. of Cheshire, 1906-10; gov. of Victoria, 1914-20.

STANLEY, HERBERT JAMES, C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (B.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; ast. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov. gen., 1913; resident comnr. Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comnr. for S. Africa, 1918.

STANLEY, W. B., M.B.E. (1918).—Served with 1st Border regt., occupation of Crete 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (medal and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjut., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (medal and clasp); ag. trav. comnr., Gambia, May and June, 1901; trav. comnr., Gambia, Aug., Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. engineering, Chatham, 1905; resign. comnr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comnr., S. Leone, 1910; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1918, to May, 1919, and from Oct., 1919, to Jan., 1920.

STANTON, AMBROSE THOMAS, M.D., Toronto: M.R.C.P., Lond.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; M.C.P. and S., Ont., D.T.M. and H., Camb., D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1875; sent. house surg., regiar. and demonstr., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., 1905-07; ast. at Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1907; bacteriologist, I.M.R., 1908; ag. dir., I.M.R., 1910; on sp. duty in Siam, Indo-China, and Netherlands E. Indies, 1915-16; dir., govt. labs., F.M.S., 1920; author of "Etiology of Beri-Beri," "Malayan Anophelines and their Larvae," "Malaria in the Oriental Region," "Distribution of Stegomyia Fasciata in Far Eastern Ports," etc., etc.

STARK, WALTER JOHN KIRKPATRICK.—B. 1887; ed. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and at Aberdeen Univ. (M.A.), 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Mar.-Apr., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May-July, 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1912; passed cadet, Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imgrts., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr. of lab., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar., 1913; offr., cl. V, Dec., 1913; asst. contr. of lab., Seremban, Apr., 1914; ag. mag., Seremban, in addn., Feb.-Mar., 1915; asst. contr. of lab., Klang, Sept., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Sept., 1917 and June, 1918; dep. contr. of lab., Klang (offr., cl. IV.), 1st Jan., 1919; emigr. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Cocoonada, Sept., 1920; ag. emigr. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Madras, 9th to 24th Oct., 1920.
STARNES, CORTLANDT.—B. 1864; served in N.W. rebellion, 1883; ag. adjutant, 65th Montreal; Insp., R.N.W.M. Pol., 1st Mar., 1886; adjutant, 1891-97; supt., 1909; asst. commr., 1st Dec., 1919.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS.—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbrmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; regis. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, 1st Jan., 1919 to 31st Mar., 1920; refugee commr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; regis. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1st Oct., 1921.

STEAD, ROBERT J.C.—B. 1880; asst. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of imgrts. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.

STEEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll.; articled to borough engr. and surv. of Dorchester, 1895 to 1898; asst. to same, 1898 to 1903; on staff of superintending civil engr., H.M. Breakwater, Portland, 1903 to July, 1905; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 27th July, 1905.

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 26th Apr., 1899; admitted (Grenada bar), 7th July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, 16th Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regisr. gen., dist. F., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920.
STEEL, WALTER MATTHEW.—B. 1868; served in treas., B. Guiana, 1884-1896; govt. lands dept., 1896-1904; treas., S. Nigeria, July, 1904; prov. treas., May, 1906; asst. treas., Nigeria, Jan., 1914.

STEEL-MAITLAND, SIR A.H.D. (great, 1917).—B. 1876; ed. Rugby (exhibitioner); Balliol Coll., Oxford (scholar); Eldon scholar, 1899; 1st mods., 1897; 1st cls. class. schls., 1897; final law schls., 1900; sec., junr. treas., Oxford Univ. Soc.; special comm. comm. on Poor Laws, 1906-7; M.P., Birmingham, since 1910; chrmn. of party organisation, 1911; partly un. state for the colonies, 31st May, 1916; additional under-sec., B. of T., in charge of the dept. of overseas trade, 1916.
STEERE, CHAS. JAS. LEE.—B. 1868; clk., col. sec.'s off., W. Aust., Feb., 1886; Aug., 1886; ag. clk., gov't. of, clk. of legia. comm. und. clk. of parl., 1890, to June, 1901; clk. of leg. ass. 1901.

STEPHEN, GUY NEVILLE.—Ed. at Seville, London; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1881; of medicine, France, 1882; ag. house sur. co. hosp.; ditto, asst. med. offr., Military asylum (Colney Hatch); surg. to the c. Norway and Sweden, Marseilles, 1888; the Br. Consulate and Seamen's Home, 1882; dist. med. offr., Nicosia, Cyprus, offr., central prison, gen. and ophthal. med. offr. of health, Nicosia; pres. of cipality, Nicosia, 1889; ag. ch. med. and 1890; surg. to Smyrna Hosp., 1890.

STEPHENS, FRANCIS TRANT.—Se African war, 1900-02; joined B.; Rhodesia, 1903; commd., 1907; se Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for S. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in d. commr. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—Ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Rova Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Te mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, 4th Jan., mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of 2nd Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, 1915 to 20th Apr., 1916; and from 2nd 5th Aug., 1918; editor-in-ch. of "The Naval Law and Court-martial proceed. edtn."); asst editor of 11th edtn. of "On Contracts"; author of works on "range," "Freight," "Charter parties," tributor to the Encyclopaedia of th England, Journal of Comparative I etc.

STEPHENSON, SIR ALBERT K.C.M.G. (1921); C.M.G. (1914).—clk. E. and A. dept., Feb., 1884; loc. Lagos, Aug., 1888; also ag. locala. a Coast, Dec., 1888, to Aug., 1889; r. E. and A. dept., and apptd. to branch, Jan., 1891; clk. in charge May, 1893; asst. sept., Aug., 1897; clk. on amalgamation of col. audit by E. and A. dept., 1st July, 1906; dir. audit, C.O., 1910; mem. of financial in comtee., Malta, 1912; major (ret.), 4th Surrey Regt., V.D. (1910).

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERRARD.—Ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ.; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st mods., 1914; on military service, 2nd 1914, to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (w tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Se

STEVENSON, PERCIVAL, Assoc., M.I.C.E.—B. 1857; asst. engr., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1874; dist. engr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. engr., in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiacara rlys., 1879; lat. asst. engr., P.W.D. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as ass't dir. of P.W.D., 1885, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engr., N. div., P.W.D., Jan., 1892; 1st engr., in charge of rds. and bridges, P.W.D., Jan., 1894; engr., in charge of rds. and bridges, rds. and bridges, dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. of pub. wks., and sen. div. engr., Jan., 1897; inspr. of mines, 1st Apr., 1914; has acted as D.P.W. with seat in leg. coun. on several occasions, 1894 to 1907.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; B.A., Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; attached to Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S., Prov., Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918.

STEVENSON, SIR JAMES, BART., G.C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1873; dir. of area organization, miny. of munitions, 1915-17; vice-chmn., miny. of munitions advisory comtee, 1917; mem., cent. reconstruction comtee., mem., munitions council for ordnance, and chmn., coun. comtee., on demobilization and reconstruction, 1918; mem. of army coun., 1919; surr. gen. of supply, W.O., 1919-21; mem. of air council, 1919-21; vice-chmn. of advisory comtee., on civil aviation; personal commercial advisor to Mr. Churchill, 1921.

STEVENSON, MALCOLM C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1878; ed. Trim. coll., Dublin (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Mar., 1902; Badulla, May, 1904; addl. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and addl. dist. judge, &c., Mannar in connection with the Pearl Fishery, Feb., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Apr., 1906; Mannar, Aug., 1908; asst. land attm. offr., Feb., 1910; 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1911; attached to col. sec.'s office for special duties, Oct., 1911; temporarily employed in C.O., Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to govt., Ceylon, Oct., 1913; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1914; priv. asst. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1915; ch. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 14th May, 1917; admstd. govt., Nov., 1918 to 24th Aug., 1920; high commnr., Cyprus, 23rd Aug., 1920.

STEWARD, MAJ.-GEN. EDWARD HARDING, C.M.G. (1887)—B. 1835; entered army, Roy. Engnr., Oct., 1854; employed from 1865 to 1879 in the fortification branch of the W.O.; services lent in 1866 to 1869, and in 1877 to the S. Australian govt., and in 1877 till 1879 to the govt. of Victoria. In charge of the defence of the Natal frontier in 1879, and comdt. of Natal and mem. of exec. coun. of the col. in 1880; services lent to govts. of Victoria and N. S. Wales in 1883; and in 1884, on retirement from the army, became military adviser to the agt. gen. of Victoria, N. S. Wales, Queensland, S. Australia, N. Zealand, and Tasmania.

STEWARD, MAJOR GEORGE CHARLES THOMAS, C.M.G. (1909).—B. 1866; clk., Tasmanian govt. rly., June, 1892; ch. clk. and acctnt., educ. dept., July, 1893; sec. to premier of Tasmania, Aug., 1894; clk. to ex. coun., Jan., 1895; and sec. to defence comtee., June, 1895, in addition; under sec. for Tasmania, Jan., 1896; and ch. inspr. of explosives and magazine, 1st Sept., 1897, in addition; raised and commanded first company of mtd. infantry in Tasmania, and holds rank of captain; ch. clk., dept. of external affrs.; Commonwealth govt., 18th May, 1901; off. sec. to govt. gen., and sec. to fed. ex. coun., 24th Dec., 1902; apptd. to command No. 5 squadron, Aust. Light Horse, 29th Jan., 1907.

STEWARD, THOMAS ALFRED FRANK.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African war with Army Ser. Corps.; ch. clk. in staff office for payment of mil. receipts, O.R.C., 1902-1903; regis. of war claims, Kronstad and Heilbron districts, O.R.C., 1903-1904; clk., Swaziland adminstr., July, 1905; principal clk., Apr., 1910; also regis. and master of the special ct. of Swaziland and sheriff of Swaziland, Apr., 1907; passed Cape Univ. civ. ser. lower law exam., 1906.

STEWART, HON. CHARLES.—B. 1868; el. by accl. to legis. ass., Alberta, g.e., 1909 and 1913; min. of mun. affrs., 4th May, 1913; re-el. at bye-el., 1913; min. of pub. wks., 28th Nov., 1913; re-el., g.e., 1913 and 1917; el. to H.C., 1921; min. of int., supt.-gen. of Indian affrs., and min. of mines in King adminstr., 29th Dec., 1921.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY.—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apr., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; ag. ch. clk., native dept., Jan.-Dec., 1908; ag. 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1909; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and govt.'s consmr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1915; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1911; ag. clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to administrator, June, 1912; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examms., Fijian language, Dec., 1913; asst. native consmr. and govt.'s consmr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1915; asst. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1916; 2nd lieut. Fiji defence force, 21st July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; 1st lieut., Fiji defence force, Aug., 1918; ag. dist. consmr., Rewa, and prov. consmr., Rewa and Nalasari, 1918; asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; prin. asst. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portamth.; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROT. PETER.—Exhbtrnr., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1892-93 and 1893-94; clk., immigr. dept., 1894; seconded to govt. secretariat, 1897-98, and 1900-02; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, July, 1903; immigr. agt., Dec., 1906; ag. sen. immigrn. agt., 1908, 1909, and 1913; senr. immigrn. agt., Feb., 1914; mem. bd. of examms. in Hindi, etc., 1914; ag. immigrn. agt.-gen., June, 1916.

STEWART, HON. WILLIAM DONIK, L.L.B.—B. 1878; ed. Otago High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. parl., 1914; min. of internal affrs. and min. of cust., 1921; served in France with N.Z. Rifle Brigade.

STIEBEL, HERBERT CECIL.—B. 1876; Boer War, 1899-1902 (King's and Queen's meda.); Transvaal civ. serv., as sub-native comsnr., 1902; prot. of natives, 1907-16; E. Africa campaign, 1916; polit. dept., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; 1st grade administrative offr., 1st Apr., 1920.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON.—2nd clk. to res. comsnr. for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and registr. to res. comsnr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899, to May, 1900; clk. of ct. and clk. to asst. comsnr. Gaberones, 1902; J.P., Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Feb., 1904; 4th Dec., 1905, to 28th Feb., 1906, and 11th May, 1906, to 29th Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag. for Southern dist. Bechuana land Prot., 15th Jan., 1907; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; seconded for ser. as res. mag., Ngamiland, 1910.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), O.B.E. (1918), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres., legis. coun., since 1901.
