STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1883; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912.
STIRLING, RIGHT REV. W. H., D.D.—Bishop of the Falklands.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; Holmes exhibn., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.), 1904; M.A., 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on special service to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to West Indian agricultural conferences, 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem. of agric. sch. comsn., 1909; mem. of banana comsn., 1910; on special service to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec. of tobacco comtece., 1911; comsnr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem. of coun. of govt., 1913; regisr. co-operative credit societies, July to Oct., 1913; mem. of bd. of directors, Mauritius Institute, 1914; mem. of irrigation comtece., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; regisr. co-operative credit societies, dir. of agric., Ceylon, 1916; vice-pres. agric. socy. and comsnr. local loans and development, 1916; mem. Machadaa colonization comtece., 1917; mem. cattle breeding comtece., 1918; mem., prison attitns. comtece., 1918; chmn., state bank comtece., 1919; del. to Imp. Entomological Conf., London, 1920; mem., statistical coun., 1920; author of several reports and articles relating to tropical agriculture.

STOCKENSTROM, SIR AUDREY, B.A., B.A. (Cantab.)—Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; sec. law dept., S. African Repub., 1895; chmn. criminal divn., atty.-gen.'s dept., S.A. Repub., 1898; chmn. of comteces., leg. assem., Transvaal, 1907-10; chmn., pub. serv. comsn., 1912; asst. chmn. of comteces., Union H. of A., 1916; rly. and harbour comsnr., 1920.

STOKER, WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temp.; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Mar., 1898; rum. fed. and island ex. and leg. couns.; ag. int. police judge conjointly with atty.-gen., Nov. to Dec., 1901; atty.-gen., Barbados, July, 1902; chmn., Barbados quarantine comsnr., 1902-3; K.C., Barbados, 3rd Apr., 1903; M.L.C., Apr. to Oct., 1903; ag. pres. of educn. bd., May to June, 1903; ag. ch. just., 1st Aug. to 30th Oct., 1903; deleg. for Barbados, and elec. pres. at Brit. W. India conf. on quarantine, Apr. to May, 1904; M.L.A. for St. Michael's, 4th July, 1906; re-elected, 23rd July, 1906; chmn. of spec. comtece. on liquor licensing system, and as to tobacco industry, 1906-7; pres. of W. Indian cent. quarantine authority, May, 1907; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, 31st Aug., 1907; ag. chief just., E. and C. Provs., Sept., 1907, to 31st Mar., 1908; ag. atty.-gen., 1st June to 14th Sept., 1908, and in Nov., 1909; ag. ch. just., 1st Dec., 1909; ret., Sept., 1914; apptd. by min. of mins. chmn. of general munit. tribunals, N.E. Coast Div. of England; mem. and chmn. of Appeal Tribunal under Military Service Acts for London; one of the arbitrators for the Bd. of Trade and Ministry of Labour in labour disputes, 1916-19; a chmn. of the interim ct. of arbitn. wages (tempy. regulation) acts, Aug. to Nov., 1919.
STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1911; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1914.
STONE, THOMAS.—Sub-dist. commandant, S. African constab., 1901-8; King's S. African medal with five clasps; King's police medal, 1909; asst. comsnr. of police, N. Nigeria, 1st Oct., 1908.

STONOR, OSWALD FRANCIS GERARD.—B. 1872; ed. Oscott and Stonyhurst; junr. offr., Selangor civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1890; ag. priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1891; held various posts in Selangor and Perak; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 6th May, 1896; max. Kuala Lumpur, 26th May, 1899; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 5th July, 1900; ag. treas., Selangor, 23rd Sept., 1901; ag. regisr. of titles, 14th Mar., 1902; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 10th Mar., 1904; ag. regisr. of titles, 1st Jan., 1907; dist. offr., Batang Padang, 25th Mar., 1909; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 25th Apr., 1912; sec. to res., Selangor, 1st Jan., 1914; ag. British res., Selangor (in addition), from 8th June, 1919; under sec., F.M.S., 24th July, 1919; ag. comsnr. of trade and cust., F.M.S., 7th Mar., 1921; ag. Br. res., Perak, 25th Apr., 1921.
STORDY, ROBERT JOHN, D.S.O. (1915).—Uganda transport service, 1st Jan., 1898; chief veterinary offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1st Apr., 1901.
STORRS, FRANCIS JOHN TOWNSEND.—Apptd. asst. collr., E. C. Africa Prot., July, 1899; 2nd cls. dist. res., Apr., 1906; res., 1st grade, Dec., 1917.

STOUT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1879; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educn., 1884-87; fellow and chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1889; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.

STRACHEY, CHARLES, C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cls. jun. clk., 1st Nov., 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtece., 10th Sept., 1885; 1st cls. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; principal clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; citto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920.

STRADBROKE, (3rd Earl of) (cr. 1821). GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, Baron Rous (cr. 1736). Viscount Dunwich (cr. 1821), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.; B. 1892; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 27th brig., R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 4th Oct., 1920.
STRANGE, HUBERT PINCKENRY COOTER.—B. 1863; inspr. of schls., Br. Honduras, 1895; dist. commr., Br. Honduras, 1898; stip. mag. and commr., Tobago, 1913; warden, Victoria, Jan., 1919.

STRANGE, WM. LUMSDEN, M.I.C.E.—B. 1837; served in P.W.D., Bombay, 1879 to 1901; under-sec., P.W.D., Govt. of India, July, 1901; supting engr., 2nd cl., Dec., 1906; seconded as dir. of irrigtn. and water supply, Transvaal, 25th Apr., 1903; author of "Indian Storage Reservoirs with Karthen Dams"; recd. Telford premium, Inst. C.E., 1897.

STRATHAIRN, DONALD BRITH.—B. 1884; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; passed law agent's exam.; capt., 6th Black Watch; Hythe certif. musketry, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1912; ag. dist. commr., Jan. to July, 1913, July, 1914, to April, 1915, Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1916, Apr. to Oct., 1917; dist. commr., 9th Apr., 1918.
STRATHAIRN, GEORGE CECIL, M.B. Ch. (Edin.).—Med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 29th July, 1903.
STREET, PHILIP WHISTLER.—Puiseux judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907.

STRETCH, CHARLES KINGSLEY.—B. 1880; assoc. M.I.E.E.; assoc. M.I.M.E.; served articles under Messrs. Mather and Platt, Ltd., Manchester; asst. electrical engr., P.W.D., Lagos, 1905; ag. chief electrical engr., Sept.-Dec., 1908; mech. engr., S. Nigeria, July, 1908; invalided from W. Africa, 1913; supt. of pub. wks., Antigua and Montserrat, 1914; M.I.C.E., 1914; J.P. and visiting just. to H.M. prison, Antigua, 1915; commr., St. John's city commr., No.: 1915-Feb., 1918; supt. of telephones, Antigua, Jan.-Mar., 1918 and Apr., 1919 to June, 1920.

STRICKLAND, Sir Gerald, Count della CATENA, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law trip; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera commr., Malta, 1847; thanked by govt. for obtaining from Leo XIII. recognition of Imp. veto of appts. to See of Malta; unoffic. mem. ex. coun. and ag. asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comtee. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., comm. of Malta univ., 1900; comm., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtee. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and commr. in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engns.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engns., N.S. Wales; held dormant commn. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917.
STRINGER, Hon. THOMAS WALTER.—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914.

STRONG, ARTHUR NESBITT.—B. 1890; M.A., Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 13th Dec., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Jan., 1914; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1914; addtl. commr. of requests, Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; on military duty, 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1919; landing surv., Colombo cust., Nov., 1921; dep. food contr., July, 1921.

STRONGE, HERBERT CECIL.—B. 1875; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; adm. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., 2nd July to 4th Aug., 1914, 3rd Sept. to 3rd Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just., Tonga, 1917.

STUART, ALAN LENNOX CONYNGHAM.—B. 1863; ed. Corrig Schl., Kingstown, and Royal Univ. of Ireland; L.I.D., barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin (hons.); dist. commr., G. Coast, 1898; sp. commr. to enquire into the admn. of Akwapim, 1899; ag. atty.-gen., 1899; sp. serv., Ashanti Field Force, with W. Akim levies, 1900 (ment. in desps., med., thanks of govt.); sp. promoted 1st cls. dist. commr., united districts of Secondee, Tarkwa and Dixcoe, 1901; commr. for rly. and mines (Tarkwa), 1902; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1902; pres. of mun. commr., Paphos, 1903-5; acted on various occasions as King's advocate; recd. letter of thanks from S. of S. re performance of addnl. duties (1914-16); puiseux judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, 1919.

STUART, ALEXR.—B. 1861; ed. St. Andrew's Univ.; registr. of impts. and expts., Singapore, Sept., 1890; tempy. attached to H.M. customs, and trade marks branch of the patent office, London, 1897-8; comml. correspondent for S.S. and F.M.S. to intell. branch of B. of T., 1903.

STUART, Hon. CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1864; ed. High schl., Strathroy, Ontario, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1898; elect. to first prov. legis., Alberta, 1905; judge of supreme ct., Alberta, 1907; chancellor of univ. of Alberta, 1908.

STUART, WALTER LESLIK.—3rd associate to judges, sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, S. Australia, 1898; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1900; 2nd associate and clk. of arraigns, Nov., 1905; judge assoc., chief clk. of sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, Feb., 1908; also a solr. to lands titles office, 1911; master of sup. ct., registr. of probates, regist. in admiralty, reg.r. of companies, trades unions and industrial and provident societies, and dist. reg.r. of high ct. of Australia, Jan., 1913, after acting in these offices from 1911.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cl. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Stlmts., F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comte., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; offr. administering the govt., Jan. to Oct., 1913; Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed govt., 30th Sept., 1919; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT.—A.I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, KT, BACH. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1873; for some years was sen. impr. and probability offr., Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-86; supt. and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comte., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20.
STURGESS, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1871; M.R.C.V.S.; govt. vet.surgeon, Ceylon, Mar., 1895.

STURMAN, MAJOR EDWARD ALBERT, C.B.E. (1918).—Entd. Impl.ser., C.T.O., 19th Apr., 1879; transf. to engineering branch, 1885; transf. to Cape telegraph serv., 22nd Feb., 1889; telegraph construction, Apr., 1891; elk., inland mails branch, 1st Dec., 1891; foreign mails branch, 1st Oct., 1892; prim. elk., staff branch, 1st July, 1897; temp. attached to agt.-gen.'s office, Mar. to Sept., 1901; prim. elk., telegraph branch, 1st May, 1902; chief elk., 1st Feb., 1908; ag. asst. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1910; ag. 1st asst. under sec., posts and telegraphs, Union of S. Africa, 1910; 1st asst. under sec., 1st Apr., 1912; dir. of army postal serv., 1914; under-sec., 18th Dec., 1919; ag. P.M.G., 13th Aug., 1920.

STURROCK, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902 (hons. class.); cadet, F.M.S., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 1st May, 1903; passed cadet, 26th Oct., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 17th Feb., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, 9th Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Gopeng, 14th Mar., 1910; seconded for service in Kelantan, 23rd Apr., 1910; judge, high ct., and ag. supt. of cust., 13th May, 1910; dist. offr., Batu Mengkabang, 28th Dec., 1910; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1st Jan., 1913; superny. mag., Ipoh, 5th Mar., 1915; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 19th May, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, 28th Oct., 1915; superny. offr., cls. II., 14th Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1st July, 1921.

STUTCHBURY, SAMUEL.—B. 1882; supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1909, and served in different depts. of the P.O.; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Singapore, Feb., 1913; supt. of mails, P.O., Penang, Jan., 1915; senr. supt. of mails, July, 1918; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Jan., 1918; supt., grade I., Jan., 1919; ag. accont., G.P.O., May, 1919.
SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Karunegala Kach., Sept., 1921.

SULLIVAN, CHARLES ERIC EUGENE, B.A. (Oxford).—Asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 7th Dec., 1909; temp. capt., intell. dept., E. African Forces, 1916; ag. 1st asst. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1918; ag. asst. chief sec., July, 1918; ag. regisr.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Sep., 1918.

SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—B. 1870; end. crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clk., agri. off., May, 1892; chief elk., July, 1902; ag. sec. to min. of agri., Feb., 1910; sec. to ditto, July, 1911; also sec. to min. of industry and sec. to min. of marine, 1915.
SUMNER, WILSON GEORGE HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Chigwell Sch.; Colonial Fellow of Surveyors' Inst., 1916; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.
SUNTHARALINGHAM, CHELLAPPAH.—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Badulla Kach., Jan., 1921.
SUNTHERAM, CANDIAH.—B. 1864; entd. customs dept., Ceylon, May, 1888; chf. appraiser, June, 1907; apptd. to class V., Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1913.

SWRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; asst. sec., ch. eecs. office, Cyprus, 16th Mar., 1918; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local comdt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. commr., Larnaca, June, 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. asst. sec., Oct., 1920.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univs.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-06; lectr., law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice repts., Queb., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages): pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francoise, Montreal.

SUTCLIFFE, EDWARD, M.R.C.S., Eng. L.R.C.P., Lond. (1907).—Med. offr., Virgin Isds., 1910-11; poor law med. offr., and vaccination offr., Holbeach Union, Lincs., 1912-20; lt., R.A.M.C., 1915-16; served in France; med. offr., dist. A., Dominica, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, ALEXANDER CHARLES, M.A., M.C.F. (Melbourne Univ.), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1869; astt. mine survr., Transvaal, 1921; mine survr., Transvaal, 1903; mine survr., govt. mining engr.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SUTHERLAND, A. R. — M.A., Aberdeen Univ.; Science mast., Glen Urquhart H.G. schl., 1903-5; astt. mast., Campbelltown gram. schl., 1905-6; astt. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-10; ag. astt. master, pol. schl., 1909 and 1912; headmaster, Victoria British schl., 1913; lieut., R.G.A., on active serv., 1914-19; astt. mast., cls. I., 1st Jan., 1920.
SUTHERLAND, GEORGE.—B. 1877; astt. man., Barbuda, Leeward Is., 1st Oct., 1908; astt. man. from 15th June, 1910.

SUTHERLAND, HON. ROBERT FRANKLIN, K.C., B.A.—B. 1859; ed. pub. and high schls., Newmarket and Windsor, Ontario, and Toronto and Western Unis.; called to the Bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900; re-elected 1904 and 1908; speaker of H. of C., 1905-1906; judge, High Ct., Ontario, 1909.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B. (mil.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1882; entd. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1900; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig.-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expdn., 1883-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaiss. in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing Pho country, Nov., 1895, to Mch., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay govt., and appreciation by govt. of India); Gildess affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay govt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E. Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in desps., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by govt.); organised 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expdn., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in desps.); apptd. commnr., consul-gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italian deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 1906-13; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by govt. of India) 1908; spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.
SWEETIE, J. W.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., Aug., 1898.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon.; exhibitioner, Worce. Coll., Oxon., June, 1876; 2nd class classical mod., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1891; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admstl. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admstl. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1895; again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Sevchelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-chief, Oct. 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906; gov., Fiji, and high commnr., W. Pacific, 11th March, 1912; assumed govt., 25th July, 1912; ret., 1918.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHLETANE, K.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1860; cadet, S. Stltmts., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and commr. et. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. commr. Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; commr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. commr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, Jan., 1890; admstl. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909.

SWETTENHAM, SIR J. ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll., Camb.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service, 1868; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. mag., Kayts, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; ag. landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; asst collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to continue to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1873; 2nd asst. col. sec., and clk. of the legis. coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audr. gen., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admstl. govt., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1898, and from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov., Br. Guiana, 1901; gov., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; resign, 1907.

SWINBOURNE, MAJOR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt.rlys., May, 1900; liq., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; passed with honours in topography and range-finding, Sydney, 1908; clk., pay and qtrmr., with rank of sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 6th Oct., 1909; capt., Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; comdg. offr., Fiji cadet corps, 13th Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, 13th Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., 16th Apr., 1911; qualified rifle course, Hythe, May, 1914; qualified (distinguished) machine gun course, Hythe, June, 1914; attached 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fiji 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., 1st Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, 13th Sept., 1915; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, 30th Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., 6th Oct., 1916; mentioned by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active service, July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., 30th Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., 25th Jan., 1918.

SWINBURNE, UMPREVILLE PERCY, A.R.S.M., F.G.S., M.I.M.M., M.I.M.E.—B. 1868; commr. of oaths; inspr. of mines, Transvaal, 1902; ch. inspr. of mines, Union of S. Africa, 1912; S. African War (despatches, medals, 3 clasps); European War, 1914-17; major, 8th serv. battn. Seaforth Highlands; wounded in France, 1915; released from mil. serv., 1917.
SWINDELL, REV. FRANK GUTHRIE, M.A. (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; col. chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. chap., Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908.

SYDENHAM OF COMBE, LORD (1ST BARON, 1913, U.K.). SIR GEORGE SYDENHAM CLARK, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1906), G.C.S.I. (1911), G.C.I.E. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1889; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expedition, 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comitee, 1885-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comitee to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comitee, 1904; gov. of Bombay, 1907-1913.

SYDNEY, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF MOST REV. JOHN CHARLES WRIGHT, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of Kilworth-Pesachamp, 1885-1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1889-1893; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1905; St. George's, Leeds, 1896-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to Bishop of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop of Sydney 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYER, WM. CHEVALLIER.—B. 1873; ed. at Eastbourne; lieut., 1897; capt., 1900, Jamaica Militia; qualified at schol. of musk., Hythe, Feb., 1901; asst. dist. comanr., S. Nigeria, June, 1901; ag. dist. comanr., Degema, Jan. to Oct., 1902; asst. transport offr., Aro field force, No. 2 column, 1901-1902 (medal with clasp); ag. dist. comanr., Degema, July, 1903, to Jan., 1906; polit. offr., Ekpaffie field force, No. 2 column, Oct. Dec., 1904; promoted dist. comanr., 1st Apr., 1905; dist. comanr., Akpiko, Aug. to Oct., 1905; polit. offr., Abakaliki, Oct., 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. comanr., Degema, Jan. to May, 1906; ag. comanr., Abeokuta, May to Sept., 1906; dist. comanr., Sapele, Feb. to June, 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. prov. comanr., Cent. Prov., June to Oct., 1907; dist. comanr., Sapele, Oct. to Dec., 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. prov. comanr., Cent. Prov., Dec., 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. prov. comanr., E. Prov., Jan. to May, 1908; ag. sen. asst. col. sec., 7th to 31st Dec., 1908; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1st Jan. to 30th Apr., 1909; ag. res., Ibadan, 1st May to 18th Oct., 1909; ag. prov. treas., W. Prov., 19th Oct. to 7th Dec., 1909; dist. comanr., Warri, 14th May to 12th June, 1910; ag. chief asst. sec. and ag. prov. comanr., W. Prov., 14th June to 29th Sept., 1910; dist. comanr., Onitsha, 3rd Oct., 1910, to 15th May, 1911; polit. offr., Onitsha escort, Orlu patrol, 22nd Nov. to 31st Dec., 1910; ag. chief asst. sec., 28th Oct., to 31st Dec., 1911; comanr., Abeokuta, 1st Jan. to 7th July, 1912; dist. comanr., 1st grade, 6th Mar., 1912; dist. comanr., Onitsha, 7th July to 5th Nov., 1912; ag. asst. prov. comanr., Eastern Prov., Apr. to July, 1913; dist. comanr., Calabar, July to Dec., 1913; ag. prov. comanr., Eastern Prov., 21st Dec., 1913; comanr., 2nd cls., 1st Jan., 1914.

SYKES, GEORGE ROGNAUL.—Cadet, S. Stltima, Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, 1914; attached censor's office, Singapore; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1916; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apr., 1917; ag. asst. regis. imports and exports (war trade dept.) Singapore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept.; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies and asst. prot. of Chinese, Malacca, June, 1921.

SYMONT, SIR JOSIAH HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1846; mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1881-1887; atty.-gen., 1881; Q.C., 1881; representative to Aus. fed. conven., 1897-8, and chmn. of its judiciary comitee; pres. of fed. league of S. Aust.; pres. of Commonwealth league; senr. for S. Aust., Commonwealth parl., 1901-1913; atty.-gen., C. of A., 1904-5; has ret. from politics.

SYMONS, CLAUDE TREVINE.—B. 1879; B.A. Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, June, 1912; ag. govt. analyst, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1916; govt. analyst, Sept., 1919.

TACHE, JOSEPH DE LABRONCHIERE.—B. 1858; ed. St. Hyacinthe Sem. and Laval Univ.; notary public, 1881; priv. sec. to Hon. J. A. Chapleau, premier of Quebec, 1880-82; priv. sec. to lieutenant-gov. Angers, 1887-92; proprietor and editor of "Le Courier," St. Hyacinthe, 1902-14; mem. of prov. coun. of agric., 1890-91; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., bye-elec. and g.e., 1904; apptd. King's printer of Canada, Mar., 1914; jt. lib. of parl., June, 1920.

TAIT, HUGH NIMMO.—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripos, Parts I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripos, Part I.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st class clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian-Coman., 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

TAITT, ALFRED.—Supernumerary clk., Trinidad, Mar., 1898; extra clk., savings bank dept., 1st Sept., 1890; 3rd clk., royal gaol off., 1st Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., educn. off., Apr., 1891; 3rd asst. clk. of the peace, Port-of-Spain, 1st June, 1891; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 12th July, 1892; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., 16th Aug., 1897; confid. and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., and clk. to leg. coun., Nov., 1895; June, 1897, to Nov., 1898; and Nov., 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., July to Oct., 1906; sec. to the col. coun.; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir F. Napier Broom; sec. to the municipal inquiry comm., 1896; clerical asst. to comm. apptd. to inquire into admin. of just. in Trinidad; sec. to industrial educn. comm.; priv. sec. to Sir H. E. H. Jerningham; asst. priv. sec. to Sir A. Moloney; priv. sec. to Mr. Hugh Clifford; priv. sec. to Sir H. M. Jackson; priv. sec. to Sir G. T. Carter; priv. sec. to Mr. S. W. Knaggs; ag. asst. col. sec., Mar. to May, 1908; ag. chf. clk., May to Dec., 1908; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Oct., 1909; warden of Couva and Chaguanas, 1st Aug., 1915; warden, Caroni, Jan., 1919.

TAITT, HORACE VIBERT.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd cls. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offir., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd asst. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1914, to Apr., 1915; June-Sept., 1915; July-Oct., 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. off., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. classical master, Queen's Coll. Apr.-July, 1917.

TALBOT, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR REGINALD ARTHUR JAMES—K.C.B. (1902); C.B. (1885); b. 1841; ed. Harrow; formerly A.D.C. to Queen Victoria; M.P. for Stafford, 1869-74; serv. in Zulu War, 1879; Egyptian Campaign, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-85; lt.-col. com. 1st Life Guards, 1886-88; mil. attaché, Paris, 1889-95; comg. cavly. brig., Aldershot, 1896-99; comd. army of occupation, Egypt, 1899-1903; gov. of Victoria, Feb., 1904, to July, 1908.
TALBOT-SMITH, L., Dist. commr., E.A.P., 27th Feb., 1908.

TALMA, EDWY L.—B.A., Christ's Coll., Camb., 14th Wrangler, math. tripes, 1895; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. astt. prot. immigr., Penang, Aug., 1897; to May, 1898; and from Oct., 1898; confirmed Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, Feb., 1899; ag. astt. P.M.G., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd ditto, Nov., 1903; astt. supt. of Indian immigr., in addition to other duties, Nov., 1903; dep. reg. sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; also astt. supt. of Indian immigr., Singapore, June, 1907; ag. reg. sup. ct., Singapore, Oct. to Dec., 1908; promoted to Cls. III., Aug., 1911; passed final exam., comm. of legal ed. (cert. of honour, Inner Temple), Trinity, 1912; ag. 2nd mag., July, 1912; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., May, 1913; ag. reg. sup. ct., Mar., 1914; ag. tempy. dist. judge, Feb., 1915; ag. dist. judge, and 1st mag., Apr., 1915; ag. dist. judge, Dec., 1915; ag. 2nd mag., Feb., 1916; offr., cls. II., Mar., 1916; ag. collr. land rev., Mar., 1916; on sp. duty, Somerset House, May-Aug., 1917; temp. attached, war tax office, Penang, Oct., 1917; collr., war tax, Nov., 1917; collr., gen. war tax, Feb., 1918; ag. treas., May, 1920.

TANNER, B. AST. mast., Diocesan schl., Hong Kong, 1898; astt. mast., Queen's Coll., 1900; sen. grade, 1901; normal mast., 1904-6 and from 1908; lecturer, technical institute, 1908-18; ag. 2nd mast., 1916; 2nd and normal mast., 1917; head mast., 1918.
TANNER, WILLIAM HUGH, F.S.I., Ed. at Merchant Taylors' schl.; astt. dir. pub. wks., E. Africa Prot., 6th June, 1903; ag. D.P.W., 1903 to 1906, and in 1907 and 1909; dep. D.P.W., 1913.

TAPLIN, CAPT. B. B. M.—B. 1883; astt. dist. commr., G. Coast, 31st July, 1909; staff offr., H.Q.C.L.C., 1917; Capt., Chinese labour batt., 1917; staff capt., Weihsai, 1917-18; capt., Chinese labour batt., 1918-19; dist. commr., Gold Coast, 23rd Feb., 1911; resumed duty, 1919.

TARRANT, HUMPHREY MILNER.—Civ. servant, dept. of civ. instr., N. Wales, 1892-96; clk., Uganda Prot., 1897; in charge of transport at Wakoli, Bunoga, 1898; in charge of Luba's station, 1899-1900; ag. coll., Bunoga and Bukedi, 1900; ag. collr., Toro, 1901; astt. treas., 1902; 2nd astt. treas., 1904; 1st astt. treas., 1909; ag. dep. treas., Uganda Prot., from June, 1909, to Jan., 1910, May to Dec., 1910, and from Apr. to Nov., 1912; dir. of customs, reg. of vessels and port officer, 1st Apr., 1913; comm. correspondent (for Uganda Prot.) to the B. of T., 1st Apr., 1913; mutiny medal with 2 clasps (Uganda, 1897-98).
TATE, FRANK, C.M.G. (1910). I.S.O. (1903).—I., 1863; dir. of educn., Victoria, Apr., 1902.

TATHAM, Hon. Frederic Spence, D.S.O., V.D., K.C., Col., S. African Defence Force; hon. major, British Army.—B. 1865; ed. Bishop's Coll., Natal; deputy speaker, Natal parlmt., and chmn. ways and means, 1897-1907; called to the bar, 1886; served Basuto war, 1881; Boer war, 1899-1902; European war, 1915-1918 (five times ment. in des., D.S.O.); judge of supreme ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Divn.), 1918.
TAUBMAN-GOLDBIE, P.C. (1898). THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE DASHWOOD, K.C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1846; founder and dep. chmn. of the Royal Niger Co.; chmn., 1885.

TAVERNER, Hon. Sir John William, K.C.M.G. (1913). KT. BACH. (1909).—B. 1854; mem. of Swan Hill Shire coun., Victoria, 1879; twice pres. and resig. in 1889; M.L.A. for Donald and Swan Hill, 1898 to 1904; mem. of first rlyw. standing comte., min. of agric., commr. of pub. wks., and vice-pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1894-1899; min. of lands and agric., and pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1902 to 1904; agent-gen. for Victoria in London, Feb., 1904; re-apptd. ditto, Feb., 1907; retired 1912.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; elk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. astt., Uganda, Jan., 1909; astt. treas., civil admn., German East Africa (occupied territory), Jan., 1917; treas., Seychelles, May, 1919.
TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918, France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 1921.
TAYLOR, E. R. S.—Asst. supt. of police, E.A.P., May, 1912.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLULUDE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.

TAYLOR, FRANK WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Newbury Grammar School and Oxford, Non-Coll.; 3rd cls. mod. hist., 1910; bd. of educn. certif.; certif. in geog., 1911; astt. mast., Magdalen Coll. School, Brackley, 1911-14; jun. supt. of educn., N.P. Nigeria, 17th June, 1914; capt. 4th Batt. Oxf. and Bucks. Lt. Infv. T.F.; served in the Northern Cameroons, 1915-16; and attached to the 1st Training Centre, W.A.S.B., 1917-18; author of "A first grammar of the Fulani language."

TAYLOR, FREDERICK EDWARD.—B. 1877; ed. Lancaster Gram. Schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb., 1st cls. class. trip., 1899; B.A., 1899; cadet, F.M.S., 1900; passed cadet, Oct., 1902; offr., cls. V., July, 1906; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1912; supernum. offr., cls. III., Jan., 1918; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Mar., 1918; supernum. offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; chmn. sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Larut, Mar., 1921.

TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practiced in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., 1st Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc., 1917; just., ct. of King's Bench, Sask., 1st Mar., 1918.

TAYLOR, GEORGE WILSON.—B. 1866; ed. in Toronto; apptd. to excise branch, inland revenue dept., 1888; engaged in spec. organization work, Quebec, 1898-1900; astt. sec., inland rev. dept., 1900; sec., ditto, 1913; astt. deputy min. and sec., 1914; astt. commr. of customs and astt. deputy minister of inland rev., Oct., 1919.

TAYLOR, HON. HENRY L.—B. 1862; ed. St. John pub. and grammar schls., Univ. of N.B. (B.A. 1882); Univ. of Edin. (M.B., C.M. 1898); physician; min. without portfolio in Murray admntr., New Brunswick, 1917.

TAYLOR, JOHN, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1844-5 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize priz., B. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; offr. reer. in bank., and offi. administr., 23rd Oct., 1908 to 22nd May, 1909; ag. dist. commr. on numerous occasions, 1905 to 1914; dist. commr., Toledo dist., 18th May, 1914; J.P. for the colony, 1908.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schol. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown att's. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, 6th Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, 11th July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 13th Feb., 1920.
TAYLOR, S. F., Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

TAYLOR, Sir W. T., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr. gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. commr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recvr. gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr. gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apl., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settmts., June, 1901; admntd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apl., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. recr.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; ret., 1910; in charge of F.M.S. development agency, London, Oct., 1910; mem. W. African lands comtce., 1912.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. gov't. chemist, Leeward Is.; 1903; ag. gov't. chemist and asst. agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; gov't. chemist and asst. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; offl. mem. legia. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees, Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem. Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dire., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; author of numerous papers and reports on agric. chemistry and tropical agric.

TENNANT, MATTHEW PETTIGREW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Montrose Academy; ch. ast. engr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1905; ag. loco. supt., ditto, July, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ag. ch. engr., Mar.-Nov., 1913; senr. inspgr. engr., P.W.D., Jamaica, Apr., 1914; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., Dec., 1918 to Sept., 1921; ag. dir. of pub. wks., July-Oct., 1920.

TENNETT, JAMES HALL.—B. 1889; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch. B., 1911; sen. house surg., Edinburgh Royal Infirmary; house physician, Cumberland Infirmary, Carlisle; house surg. and asst. health offr., Penang and Singapore, S. Settmts., 1912-1914; seconded for service in Kedah, 1914; resumed duty in S. Sttltms., 1918; ag. surg., native hosp., Singapore, and lectr. in clinical and operative surgy. to the King Edward VII. med. schl.; ag. senr. surg., Penang, 1919.

TENNYSON, Rt. Hon. LORD, 2ND BARON (U.K., crest, 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.M.G. (1899). HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded 1892 (on the death of his father), Alfred Lord Tennyson, poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1852; ed. Marlborough Coll., Trim. Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; J.P. for Hants; mem. of Marlborough Coll. exec. coun.; prepared his father's memoir, published in 1897; gov't. S. Australia, 1899; gov't. gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1902-1904; chmn. of comtce. for sttlmt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917.

THAINE, ROBERT NIEMANN.—B. 1875; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; offl. asst. to gov't. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Cent. Prov., Aug., 1900; commr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, June, 1901; offl. asst. to gov't. agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, May, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landing surr. cust., Colombo, 1904; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, Aug., 1906; dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908; commr. of requests Colombo, Jan., 1911; asst. gov't. agent, Mannar, Sept., 1911; ag. gov't. agt., Province of Sabaragamawela, Feb., 1913; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1913; commr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1917; censor, 18th Apr., 1917; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, May, 1918; ag. prim. collr. of cust., Apr., 1919; gov't. agt., Prov. of Uva, June, 1920; ag. prim. collr. of cust. and food contr., Nov., 1921.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. schl.: examr. of loan accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 1st Jan., 1904; examr. of accts., ditto, 1st July, 1904; provincial auditor, O.F.S. Prov., 16th Apr., 1912.
THEODORE, HON. E. G.—Treas. and sec. pub. wks., Queenslands, 1915; premier, 1919.

THIBOU, ALFRED EARLE.—B. 1885; junr. clk., audr.-gen.'s office, Antigua, Feb., 1906; 3rd indoor offr., treasy., June, 1909; ag. 2nd indoor offr., Aug.-Oct., 1909, and from Apr., 1911, to Mar., 1912; ag. 1st indoor offr., July-Sept., 1912; ag. 2nd indoor offr., Jan., 1913; ag. chief audit clk., federal acctnt., and clk., P.O.G. Fund, July, 1915; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts, Nov., 1915 to Apr., 1916; ag. 1st indoor offr., treasy. and cust., Antigua, Aug.-Nov., 1916; sub-treas., Portsmouth, Dominics, Nov., 1916 to May, 1917; ag. supt. of charitable instittns., Antigua, Aug., 1917; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.
THIBOU, CLEMENT GARRY.—Foreman of wks., P.W.D., Antigua, Mar., 1915; asst. town clk., St. Kitts, Apl., 1916; ag. supt. of pub. wks., Aug., 1916.
THOM, M. ST. C.—Asst. supt. of police, E. Africa Prot., 1911.
THOMAS, CAPT. G.—Ed. Mulborough Coll.; solr., 1908; 7th batt. Welsh regt., Aug., 1914; capt., June, 1916; served with M.G.C. in France and Italy, 1916-1918; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 1919.

THOMAS, HON. JOSIAH.—B. 1863; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected in 1903 and 1906; P.M.G., C. of A., Apr., 1910; min. for external affairs, 1911-1913; now federal senator.

THOMAS, THOMAS SHENTON WITTELEGGE, O.R.E. (1919).—Ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons., 1901; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd asst. sec., Apr., 1911; sen. asst. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. asst. ch. sec., Aug.-Oct., 1913, Aug.-Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917-Jan., 1919; chmn. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917-19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. comn., 1918; asst. ch. sec.

UGANDA, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development commn., 1919; gov't. dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prin. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911.

THOMPSON, HENRY BURFORD.—B., 1880; cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Apr., 1905; sectar., ditto, Apr., 1906; ag. 1st treas. offr., Montserrat, Aug.-Nov., 1906; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Antigua Dec., 1906; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1907 to Mar., 1908; 3rd outdoor offr., treas., Jan., 1908; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and from Apr. to Sept., 1912; student, Middle Temple, 1912; cashier, treas., May, 1914; ag. 1st clk., regis.'s office, May, 1915; commr. to admstr. oats, Antigua, July, 1916.

THOMPSON, HERBERT W. B.—1889; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920.
THOMPSON, J. H.—M.B. Bac. Surg.; med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B., 1892; matric., 1912, and diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; app't. after compet. exam., ast. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., app'td. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 27th Aug., 1914.

THOMPSON, J. V., B.A.—B., 1872; ed. Dulwich Coll., Blair Lodge, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; B.A., Cantab., 1893; English and math. master, Idadi schl., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1899; headmaster, Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1906; inspr. of schls., 1907; J.P. for the Colonies, 1909; represented Fiji at imp. conf. on educ., 1911; English educn. offr., Johore, 1916.
THOMPSON, PETER G.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1891; dist. commr., Lagos, 1904; has acted on several occasions as Crown prosecutor and as pol. mag.

THOMPSON, ROBERT REGINALD, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.I.M.E., A.I.M.M., A.I.P.T.—B., 1882; silver medallist, City and Guilds; field gen. man., Anglo-Persian Oil Coy.; ment. in dep., 1915 and 1918; dir. of lands and mines, and sub-intendant of crown lands, Trinidad, 26th Dec., 1920; author of prize papers before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM AUSTIN.—2nd cl. supervsr., G. Coast, Apr., 1894; ag. ast. treas., Cape Coast Castle, Mar., 1895; cashr., Accra, Mar., 1896; ag. ast. treas., Apr., 1896; 1st cl. supervsr., Sept., 1897; sent on serv. in hinterland, Feb., 1898; invalided and ret'd. from West Afr., Feb., 1900; treas., etc., Falklands, July, 1901; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., in addition to duties as treas., May to Nov., 1902, Apr., 1903, o Jan., 1904, July to Sept., 1905, June to Nov., 1909, and from Jan. to June, 1911.

THOMPSON, DONALD C.—B., 1874; ent. treas. dept., 1894; priv. sec. to various ministers since 1901; sec. to prime min., 1912; clk. of exec. comm. and sec. of cabinet since 1919; mem. of secretariat, Br. Empire delegation, Peace Conf., Paris, 1919; mem., Imp. Conf. secretariat, 1917, 1918 and 1921.

THOMSON, SIR GRAEME, K.C.B.—B., 1875; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; higher div. clk., Admity., 1900; ast. dir. of trans., 1914; dir. of trans., 1914; col. sec., Ceylon, Sept., 1919; offr. admtrg. govt., 8th-18th Jan., 1920 and Mar.-Sept., 1920.

THOMSON, HENRY WAGSTAFFE, B.A. (Oxon.)—B., 1874; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Selangor, F.M.S., Nov., 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1897; passed cadet, Dec., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Feb., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1898; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Dec., 1898; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1899; ag. ast. dist. offr., Serendah, Apr., 1900; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1900; seconded for service in Kelantan, May, 1903; resigned F.M.S. serv., and in the serv. of the Kelantan govt., May, 1906 to July, 1909; offr., cls. III., F.M.S., performing duties of ast. adviser, Kelantan, July, 1909; ag. dist. offr., Klang, 1910; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regis. of titles, Selangor, Feb., 1914; ag. regis. of titles, Perak, Oct., 1914; ag. supt., convict estab., Taiping, in addn., Feb., 1915; supt., convict estab., Taiping, and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., Jan., 1917; ag. regis. of titles, Perak, in addn., May, 1918; chmn., army, bd., Kinta, Dec., 1918; ag. br. adviser, Kelantan, Feb., 1919; confirmed in app't., Oct., 1919.

THOMSON, JOHN HASTINGS.—B., 1873; ed. Wallace Hall Academy, Dumfriesshire, Scotland; app'td. ast. dist. offr., Somaliland, May, 1901; ag. consul, Berbera, Som., 1901, to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Bulhar, Jan., 1904-5; polit. offr. with Gen. Swayne in Abyssinia, Mar. to Apr., 1905; polit. offr. with troops on Abyssinian border, May to July, 1905; ag. sub-commr., Zeyla, 2nd June, 1905; Somaliland medal with clasp, 1902-4; dist. offr., Makhr Coast, Aug., 1906; ag. sub-commr., Zeyla, 5th June, 1907; dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

THORNE, WM. HOBART HOUGHTON.—B., 1875; ed. at St. Paul's Schl., King's Coll., Camb., and Paris Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1900; Licencié en Droit de la Faculté de Paris, Nov., 1903; British representative on Internat. Claims Comm. at Casa Blanca, 7th May, 1908; employed at F.O. in investigation of Anglo-American pecuniary claims, 15th Sept., 1910, to 11th July, 1911; pres. of dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 20th July, 1911; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1912, and in Ottoman Turkish, June, 1916; judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 18th Sept., 1916; resig. 5th Mar., 1917; British commr. on the mixed indemnities commn., Athens, 5th Mar., 1917; re-app'td. a judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 1st Feb., 1919; is comdr. of the Order of the Saviour.
THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B., 1902; app'td. after compet. exam., ast. clk., sec's office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920.

THORNHILL, PONSONBY.—Supernumerary offr. of cust., B. Guiana, Aug.-Sept., 1887; ast. record clk., P.W.D., Oct., 1887; ast. to clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) P.W.D., New Amsterdam, May, 1888; 5th cls. offr., P.W.D., Apr., 1892; ag. clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) Berbice and distc., Feb. to Aug., 1893; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1896; clk., P.O. savings bank, Jan., 1899; 4th cls. clk., G.P.O., Jan., 1900; 3rd cls. clk., treas., Georgetown, Jan., 1901; ag. cashier, June, 1904, to July, 1905, and May to Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, Lieut.-Col. Sir Edward Newbury, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C.—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosps. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape 1910; major med. br., sec. I. Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.) Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C. S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comttee of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comttee management, Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comttee on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; asst. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for the Union, 1920.

THORNTON, GEORGE.—M.D. (Edin.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.P.H. (Oxford); house physac., Edin. Royal Infirmary, 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's hosp., Hull; asst. med. offr., Tooting Fever hosp., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in S. Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-1908; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Nov., 1908; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Sept.-Dec., 1919; asst. civ. med. offr., Jan., 1920.

THORNTON, Hugh Cholmondley, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1920).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, at Unionist Central Office, 1912-14; major and 2nd in cond., 10th and 11th D.C.L.I., 1915-1916; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916, to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; appld. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, Hon. Robert S., LL.D.—B. 1863; went to Canada, 1884; ed. Fettes Coll., schl., and Univ. of Edinburgh (M.B., C.M.); LL.D., Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada; physician; pres., Manitoba med. coun., 1896; pres., med. coun. of Canada; unsuccessful candidate for legis. assem., Manitoba, 1907 and 1910; elected, 1914 and 1915; min. of educn., Manitoba, in Norris admin., May, 1915.

TRELFALL, WM. HERBERT.—B. 1875; appld., after open compet., exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1898; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1905; supt. of regisn., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907; supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, Jan., 1912; ag. asst. postm.-gen., Penang, July, 1914; acctn. (G.P.O.), Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. P.M.G., Oct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., May, 1919; ag. asst. P.M.G., Singapore, May, 1919; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1919; asst. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1920; asst. P.M.G., Singapore, but continued to act as above, Sept., 1920.
THRUPE, H. R.—Temp. asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877 employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postm. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905; and from June, 1906, to Mar., 1907; postm. of Antigua and federal postm. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua 1910; income and trade tax assessor, 1908 and 1913; chmn., bd. of guardians, May to Nov., 1911, and May, 1913; dir., P.O.G. fund, May 1912.

TIBBITS, Elwood D'Arcy.—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1898; Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., 1st Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 8th July, 1901; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, 2nd Aug., 1905, to 5th Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, 23rd Sept., 1906; 2nd clk., clk. to exec. and legis. couns., and supt. of govt. printing off., Antigua, 11th Oct., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 17th Oct., 1903, to 5th May, 1904; 28th May, 1911 to 31st Jan., 1912, and 15th Apr. to 18th July, 1912; ag. asst. col. sec. 13th Nov. to 11th Dec., 1907, 11th to 22nd Jan., 1908, 21st Aug., 1911 to 31st Jan., 1912, 5th Feb. to 4th Mar., 1912; 15th Apr. to 19th June, 1912; ag. ch. clk. on abolition of off. of asst. col. sec., 20th June to 1st Oct., 1912; ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Leeward Is., 2nd Oct., 1912 asst. col. sec., 1st Oct., 1920.

TIBBITS, Richard White Longmuir.—B. 1846; ed. at high schl., Quebec and Lennoxville coll., formerly coroner, J.P., and sec.-treas., Victoria, New Brunswick; elec. to legislature N.B., 1882; dep. prov. sec., N.B., 1885; King's printer, 1895.
TICE, W. G.—B. 1867; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 19th Feb., 1900; 2nd cl. 1st Feb., 1902; King's messmgr., 22nd May 1907.

TILLEY, ALBERT.—B. 1878; Impl. serv., 1895-92; post off., exchequer and audit depts., transferred to Transvaal serv., Mar., 1902; clk. audit off., Transvaal, 1902; senr. clk., 1909 ditto, aud. off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; prov. audr. and local govt. inspr., Transvaal Prov., May, 1915.
TISDALE, E. G. S.—Asst. dist. commr., F. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.
TITCHENER, Henry Ian Herrick.—B. 1900 entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after comple exam., asst. clk., C.O., Feb., 1919; cler. offr. 1st Jan., 1920.
TOBIT, PURCHW MAHON.—L.R.C.S., L.R.C.I. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); med. offr., G. Coas 16th Dec., 1899; served as P.M.O. in Tian expdn., 1902 (ment. in desps.).
TOLMIE, ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1884; ed. Kincardine high schl., Toronto Univ.; apptd. to Canadian govt. exhibn. comm., 1908; Canadian govt. exhibn. comm., Aug., 1919.

TOLMIE, Roderick Finlayson.—B. 1858; ed. coll. schl., Victoria, British Columbia; clk. to bank of B. Columbia (Cariboo agency) 1874, ent. B.C. govt. serv., 1877; gen. sec. B.C. milk owners assoc., 1899; re-entd. govt. serv. as de-min. of mines, 1902.

TOLMIE, Hon. Simon Fraser.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schl., Ontario, and Vet. Coll. Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres. Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Ve Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. insp. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B. C. representative on live stock comsn., elec. to Canadian H. of C., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. of agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resign. portfolio on defeat of admntr., in g.e., Dec., 1921.
TOMKINSON, C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, B.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—Served in educn. dept., Transvaal, Feb., 1903, to Oct., 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1907; seconded to G. Coast as dir. of educn., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Apr., 1911.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS SYMONDS.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, 5th May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, 2nd Jan., 1911; ag. judge, 23rd Feb. to 4th Nov., 1916; judge, 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admntr. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921.

TOMPKINS, A.C., Lieut.-Col.—Ed. privately, and at Versailles, Paris and Hastings; served in 6th Inniskilling Dragoons, 1880-81; 2nd lieut. and lieut., Roy. Westmorland Militia, 1881-82; served with 1st and 2nd batts., The Black Watch (Roy. Highlanders), The Roy. Horse Guards (Blues), 1882-84; lieut., 4th Roy. Fus., 1884; sub-inspr., B. Honduras constab., Dec., 1887; inspir. and 2nd in command, Frontier Defence Force, 1888-89; capt., Roy. Fus., 1889; commd. a troop of the B.S.A. Co.'s pol. in pioneer exp., Mashonaland, 1889-91; inspir., Sierra Leone Frontier Force, Aug., 1892; sub-inspr.-gen., S. Leone Frontier Force, and ag. inspir.-gen., 1893; J.P., S. Leone, 1893; invalided and resign., Dec., 1893; apptd. under F.O., and seconded for serv. under the Egyptian govt. as adjt. and major, and major, Coast Guard serv. (Camel Corps, Cavalry, Infy. and Marine); commd., Western Frontier dist., Mex., 1894-5; Port Said and Canal dist., 1896-7; red. thanks of H.H. the Khedive and Egyptian govt. for sp. preventive serv., Port Said, 1896; commd., Suez and Red Sea dist., 1897-8; supply and transport offr. during survey and constrn. of state rly. to Lake Victoria Nyanza, E. Africa and Uganda, 1899-1901; major, Roy. Fus., 1899; commanded mil., pol., and vol. forces of the Sultan of Johore, 1901-06; lieut.-col., Roy. Fus., 1906; add. as 2nd in command, 1st Roy. Fus., and 5th Roy. Fus., 1907; dir. of transport, Cairo to Cairo sur. exp., 1907-08; passed mdu-k. and vety. courses, Hythe and Aldershot, 1908; on sp. serv. as dir. of transport (land and water) under Turkish govt., Mesopotamia, 1909-11; on staff of the Lord Chamberlain, St. James' Palace, 1911-12; on sp. serv. in W. Africa, 1913; on sp. serv. in Egypt, Sudan and Equatorial Provs., Red Sea Littoral, Aden, Ceylon, 1914; served throughout the great war, Aug., 1914 to 1918, on western front, viz.:—remount staff, B.E.F., 1914; 2nd in command, sp-rv. batt., Roy. Fus., B.E.F., 1915; A.P.M., 12th div., B.E.F., 1915-16; adminis. comdt., 5th army railheads, B.E.F., 1917; wounded, Nov., 1917; adminis. comdt., 4th army railheads, B.E.F., 1918; twice ment. in desp., 1916-17; adminis. comdt., 3rd army railheads, promoted full col. and comdt. (graded A.A.G.), sub-areas in France and Belgium, Apr. to Nov., 1919; dist. comsnr., Cyprus, 30th Sept., 1920.

TONGE, Lieut. Comdr. C.G., R.N.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; naval transport offr., Aug. to Dec., 1914; lent for serv. in gunboats on Lake Nyassa, Jan., 1915; senr. naval offr., Lake Nyassa, and 1st offr., marine transport dept., June, 1919; Royal Naval Res. Offr.'s Decoration.
TOOGOOD, F. C.—A.D.C. to H.E. the gov., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1919.

TOIPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Diocesan schl., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on taking over of line entd. govt. ser. as sub-acctnt., rly. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; ag. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; aud. gen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. aud. gen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1906.

TORRANCE, FREDERICK.—B. 1859; ed. McGill Univ., B.A., 1878; D.V.Sc., 1882; dist. veterinarian, Man., 1883-90; formerly prof. vet. science, Man. Agric. Coll.; vet. ed., "Nor West Farmer"; ex-pres., American Vet. Med. Assoc., Man. Vet. Assoc.; hon. pres., Cent. Can. Vet. Assoc.; mem. Internat. Coman. on Bovine Tuberculosis; Can. del. to Internat. Vet. Con., London, 1914; vet. dir.-gen., dept. of agric., Can., 1st Dec., 1912.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra asst. to postmtr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; asst. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; asst. sec., cent. irrigtn. bd., 15th Jan., 1900; sec., ditto, and office asst. to dir. of irrigtn., 16th May, 1900; asst. acctnt., gen. treas., Jan., 1907; ag. financial asst. and acctnt., general treas., Apr., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1916; ag. paymr., Ceylon volr. force, Aug., 1914; ag. staff offr. in addition to his own duties, Apr., 1915; ag. financial asst. and acctnt., gen. treas., Nov., 1915; 1st asst. acctnt., Apr., 1916; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., Apr., 1921.

TOUGH, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin; served 11 years in 42nd Highrs.; 1st cl. cert. of educn., 1896; cert. of mil. eng., 1898; Hythe cert. of musk. (distinguished) 1904; mounted infantry cert., 1905; served in South African War, 1899-1902; Queen's medal and 3 clasps; King's medal and 2 clasps; severely wounded at Magersfontein; sub.-inspr. of pol. and instr. nat., St. Kitts, 1907; J.P., 1907; ag. inspr. of pol. and adjt. of local forces, 1908; chmn. of tel. bd., 1908; estab. helio. commun. between St. Kitts and Nevis, 1909; sub.-inspr. of pol., mil. impr. of roads and works, impr. of weights and mens., Montserrat, 1909; J.P., 1909; mem. bd. of health, 1910; sub.-inspr. of pol. and mil. instr., Antigua, 1910; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Is.; returned to Montserrat, 1913.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1879; ag. off. asst., Matara Kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist Temporalities ord. coman., and extra off. asst. to dir. of educn., Nov., 1918; ag. off. asst. to col. auditor, Apl., 1919; apptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., local divn., July, 1919; ag. asst. col. audr., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. to food contr., July, 1921; addnl. asst. comanr. of stamps, Sept., 1921.

TOWNER, H. V.—Mem. San. Inst., Assoc. mem. C.E.A.; asst. supt. of wks., S. Stltlme., Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908; exec. engr., Dec., 1909; ag. dep. col. engr., Penang, Jan., 1910; exec. engr., Singapore, Mar., 1915; on sp. duty in Malacca on road reconstruction, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engr. and survr.-gen., Malacca, Feb., 1919; exc. engnr., grade I., Malacca, Dec., 1919; dep. col. engr., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1921; ag. col. engrnr., Mar., 1921.
TOWNSEND, ALFRED ERNEST.—Ed. at Brisbane gram. schl.; ast. survr., G. Coast survey, 1902-1905; survr., E. Africa Prot., 24th Aug., 1905; dep. dir. of surveys, Dec., 1908.
TOWNSEND, W. H. M.—Ch. offr., Uganda rly. lake steamers, June, 1903; comdr., July, 1905.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913.
TRAFFORD, JOSEPH LOUIS.—B. 1888; ed. Beaumont Coll.; ast. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; ast. dist. comsrr., June, 1915, and from July, 1916 to July, 1917.
TRAILL, FRANCIS STUART FORBES.—Ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 10th Oct., 1898; collr., 1st Apr., 1903; ag. prov. comsrr., 1916; prov. comsrr., June, 1918.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM.—Ed. at Clifton Coll., schlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. ast. land offr. in New Territories, and regiar. of land ct., Mar., 1907; mem. of land ct., June, 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, July, 1907; ag. ast. dist. offr., May, 1909; 2nd ast. regiar. gen., Sept., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, May, 1910; 1st ast. registr.-gen., July, 1911; ag dist. offr., N.T., July, 1911; ag. head of sanitary dept., Nov., 1912; ast. dist. offr. N.T. (South), Nov., 1912; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. offr., N.T., Jan., 1915; ag. head of sanitary dept., Sept., 1915; head of sanitary dept., 1917; ag. supt. of imports and exports, May, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. col. treas., 1920.

TREACY, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1879; ed. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Sallins, Co. Kildare, Ireland; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1906; crown counsel, G. Coast, 25th July, 1915; ag. senr. crown counsel in 1915, 1916 and 1917; ag. solr.-gen. on various occasions, 1916-20; ag. chief regiar., May to Nov., 1917; ag. pol. mag., Sept. to Nov., 1917; ag. atty.-gen., July to Sept., 1920.

TREE, FRANCIS THOMAN, B.A., Dublin.—B. 1887; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1911; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, June, 1911; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., sp. res., Nov., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1916; attd. R.E. for research in poison gas, Mar., 1916; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, May, 1920.

TREMLETT, FREDERICK THOMAS GEORGE, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 1903; attached Lance Fusiliers, 1903; sub-inspr. constab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1908-1911; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 1908; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 1912; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-1912; passed mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1912; transf. to Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius vol. force, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comd. Mauritius labour batt., 1917; comd. Mauritius vol. force, Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, May, 1917, and Mar. to Dec., 1919.
TRESIDDER, CAPT. TOLMIE JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1850; was engnr. for Malta drainage wks.

TRISCOOTT, LIONEL EDWARD LORAIN.—B. 1873; capt., 3rd batt. Buffs, 1897; S. African War, 1900-2 (ment. in desps.); capt., 1st K.A.R., 1914; comdr., column, Nyassaland rising, 1915; (ment. in desps.); res., Nyassaland, 1918.

TROTTER, CECIL DOUGLAS.—B. 1886; ed. Ardingly Coll.; joined R.S.A. Pol., Jan., 1906; transf. to native affairs dept., Sept., 1907; placed on fixed establt., 1908; passed native language and Cape civ. serv. law exam., 1910; ast. nat. comsrr., Apl., 1911; J.P. for S. Rhodesia, May, 1911; ast. mag., Bellingwe, Dec., 1911 to Mar., 1912; ch. clk. to native affairs dept., native comsrr., and ast. mag., Sebungwe, May, 1912 to Nov., 1913; ast. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; ast. clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coal comsrr., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comsrr., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regiar. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TROWELL, WM. JOHN.—B. 1864; M.I.N.A.; engnr. and shipwright survr. and examnr. of engnrs., Liverpool, Feb., 1898; inspr. under bld. of agric., Dec., 1898; inspr. of marine surveys, S. Sttlmts., 16th Jan., 1903; on special ser. in England in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., July to Nov., 1906; on duty at C.O. and Bd. of Trade on draft of merchant shipping ordn., 1910, Apr., 1908, to Jan., 1909; war serv. recog. by S. of S., Apr., 1918.

TRUTER, THEODORUS GUSTAFT, C.M.G. (1818).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1st Dec., 1892; chief examr. of acts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (medal and four clasp); ast. mag., Pretoria, 1st Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermele, 1st Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, 1st Apr., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, 1st July, 1910; comsrr., S. African pol., 15th Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa.

TUBOKU-METZGER, ALBERT EMERIC, B.A.—Ed. in C.M.S. Gram. schl. and Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone; gained Fourah Bay Coll. foundation scholarship, Jan., 1877; grad. in Arts, June, 1880; first cls. hon. in theology, Durham Univ., Dec., 1881; jun. cls. tutor, Fourah Bay Coll., 1881; entd. S. Leone civ. serv. as extra clk., secretariat and treasy., 1st Mar., 1885; gov't. office, 1886; col.-treasy., 1887; served as finan. clk. in the Yonni expnd. under Sir Samuel Rowe, 1887; ch. clk., registr.-gen.'s dept., 1890; regiar. of births and deaths, Freetown dist., 1890; police alk. and clk. of ct. of requesta, Freetown, Apr., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1904 to 1905, and 1907; ast. dist. comsrr., 1st May, 1908; apptd. Crown prosecutor in the Sept. sessions of the sup. ct. at Sherbro, Oct., 1908; is a J.P. for the col.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, F.I.A.A., certified acctnt.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; ast. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; ast. audr., Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (ment. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volrs., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt. Nyassaland Field Force, 1919; ast. auditor, Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; ag. auditor, Apl., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dep. treas., June, 1921; dep. treas., Dec., 1921.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Instit. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec., govt. Lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911.

TUCKETT, GEORGE HENRY.—Imperial postal service, 1891; postmaster, B.C.A. Prot., 1897; ast. agt. and P.M.R., Chinde, 1898; ag. British vice-consul, Chinde, 1899; ag. P.M.G., B.C.A. Prot., 1900-1; ditto, Nyassaland, 1910; ag. paymaster and acctnt. to armed forces, Nyassaland, 1910; deputy P.M.G., Nyassaland, 1911; P.M.G., Nyassaland, Aug., 1912.
TU'DHOPE, W., M.B., Bac. Surg.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Sept., 1913.

TU'DHOPE, WILLIAM STEEL DYKES.—B. 1877; on staff of W. of Scotland Agric. Coll., 1901-07; dir. of agr., Gold Coast, Sept., 1907; ten months in Ceylon before assuming duty; del. of Gold Coast at Internat. Rubber Exhibtn., Lond., June, 1911; and at Internat. Congress of Trop. Agr., Lond., June, 1914; on sp. mission to Ecuador, 1920.

TUDOR, SIR DANIEL THOMAS, KT. BACH. (1917), K.C.—B. 1866; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; eq. dr.; also practised on West. Circ.; atty.-gen. of Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; K.C. of both colonies, May, 1908; acted as chief just., also as col. sec. of Grenada on several occasions; adminstr. gov't. of St. Vincent, June, 1907, to Jan., 1908; and gov't. of Grenada, May and June, 1908, and Mar. to May, 1910; commr. to consolidate and revise the Laws of Grenada, 1911; ag. legal ast., C.O., May to Oct., 1911; chief justice, Bahamas, Nov., 1911; pres. of discharged soldiers comm. of inquiry, Jan., 1918.

TU'DOR, HON. FRANK GWYNNE.—B. 1866; M.P. for Yarra, Victoria, 1900-1901; elec. to first H. of E., C. of A., 1901; min. of trade and custs., C. of A., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914 to S-p.t., 1916; leader of opposition.

TU'PER, HON. SIR CHAM. HIBBERT, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1855; son of late Sir C. Tupper; mem. of N.S. bar; grad. Harvard law schl.; mem. of house of commons, Canada, 1882-1900; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888-1896; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. agt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the Behring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1895; resig. 1896; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUFNELL.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919, 1920 and 1921.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNBULL, AUBREY MARRIOTT DALWAY.—Ed. Bath Coll.; clk., treasy., B.C. Africa, Jan., 1903; editor, B. C. A. Gazette, Nov., 1903, to Sept., 1906; 3rd cls. res., May, 1906; ag. regier. of high ct., offl. recvr. and liquidator, May to Dec., 1908; ag. res. Mlanje dist., July, 1909, to Mar., 1910, and May to Nov., 1912; dist. mag., July, 1906; ag. supt. of native affairs and comm. intell. offr., May, 1910, to Mar., 1911; attached to deputy govrs' office for special duty, Sept., 1911, to Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to the administration, Nyassaland Prot., Dec., 1912; ast. ch. sec., Mar., 1914; mem. of native rising comm., 1915; dist. pol. offr. in occupied territory in German E. Africa, July, 1916; adminis. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1920; hon. major, Nyassaland Field Force.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot sergt-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, commt. of police, provost marshal, and inspnr. of prisons, June, 1919.
TURNER, H. A.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.
TURNER, CAFT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.

TURNER, JAMES POSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; commr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., registr.'s off., Antigua, 3rd May, 1906; commr. to admin. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. to trustees, sup. ct. library, June, 1906; clk. to registr. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; ag. registr. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, Oct., 1910, to Feb., 1912; ditto, Antigua, and ch. registr., Leeward Is., 17th Feb. to 31st March, 1912, 26th Apl. to 20th Nov., 1914, and from 4th May to 5th Nov., 1915; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; registr. and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and chief registr., 1st Sept., 1917.

TURNER, LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON.—B. 1885; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; ast. commr. of excise, Nov., 1913; ast. commr., S. Div., Jan., 1914; ditto, N.W. Div., Aug., 1916; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1917; ag. ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1918; ast. commr. of excise, N.W. divn., and ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1919; ag. ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1919; sup't. of census, July, 1920; dir. of statistics in addn., Sept., 1920.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. Univs.; M.A. (Abdn.), 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; M.D., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick childrn., Edin., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infirmry., Edin., 1880; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp., 1881; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell. obstetrical soc., Edin.; ast. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar, 1882; also surg. civ. prison, med. offr. lunatic asyl., and dist. med. offr., 1882; pub. vaccinator, pd. surg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.
TURPIN, ERNEST ALBERT, M.B., Ch. B., Edin.—B. 1879; entered med. serv., Trinidad, 1st May, 1905; res. surg., colonial hosp. San Fernando, 1st Jan., 1910.

TURTON, NEVILLE HARRY.—B. 1889; ed. at Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; ast. auditor, S. Leone, 1912; ag. auditor, on four occasions; ast. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921.
TWELLS, JOHN.—Ed., at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., 1907; examr., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; ast. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; ast. auditor, E. Africa Prot., July, 1912.

TWOSE, LAWRENCE RENDELE.—B., 1891; 4th elk., C.S.O., Antigua, 1915; elk. to mag., dist. A., 1915; sec. to fire brig., comsnr., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to gov., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; elk. to comsnr., Montserrat, 1920; elk. of couns., dep. coroner, edcn. dist. offr., 1921.

TWYCROSS, HENRY WILLIAM STEPHEN.—Entd. telec. dept., Cape Col., 1st Dec., 1876; 1st cls. elk., secretarial branch, G.P.O., 28th Aug., 1893; prin. elk., 1st July, 1897; attached to agt.-gen. off., in London, on special duty, Apr. to Sept., 1902; ag. survr. and dist. engnr., Western postal dist., Cape, 7th Dec., 1902, to 30th Sept., 1903; ch. elk. sec.'s office, 1st Jan., 1903; ast. sec., 1st Feb., 1908; ag. under sec., for posts and teleg., U. of S. Africa, 1910; under sec., 1st Apr., 1912; P.M.G. and sec., 8th Dec., 1919.
TYLER, JAMES EDWARD.—Govt. printer, S. Stiltns., 1st July, 1905.

TYRELL, FRANCIS GRAHAM.—B., 1876; ed., Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admstg. the govt., Apr., 1902; offr. ast. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1905; cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; ag. govt. agt., N.C.
